verses.kjv: BLESSED is the man that walketh not in the counsel of the ungodly, nor standeth in the way of sinners, nor sitteth in the seat of the scornful.
verses.bsb: Blessed is the man who does not walk in the counsel of the wicked, or set foot on the path of sinners, or sit in the seat of mockers.

verses.kjv: But his delight is in the law of the Lord; and in his law doth he meditate day and night.
verses.bsb: But his delight is in the Law of the LORD, and on His law he meditates day and night.

verses.kjv: And he shall be like a tree planted by the rivers of water, that bringeth forth his fruit in his season; his leaf also shall not wither; and whatsoever he doeth shall prosper.
verses.bsb: He is like a tree planted by streams of water, yielding its fruit in season, whose leaf does not wither, and who prospers in all he does.

verses.kjv: The ungodly are not so: but are like the chaff which the wind driveth away.
verses.bsb: Not so the wicked! For they are like chaff driven off by the wind.

verses.kjv: Therefore the ungodly shall not stand in the judgment, nor sinners in the congregation of the righteous.
verses.bsb: Therefore the wicked will not stand in the judgment, nor sinners in the assembly of the righteous.

verses.kjv: For the Lord knoweth the way of the righteous: but the way of the ungodly shall perish.
verses.bsb: For the LORD guards the path of the righteous, but the way of the wicked will perish.

verses.kjv: WHY do the heathen rage, and the people imagine a vain thing?
verses.bsb: Why do the nations rage and the peoples plot in vain?

verses.kjv: The kings of the earth set themselves, and the rulers take counsel together, against the Lord, and against his anointed, saying,
verses.bsb: The kings of the earth take their stand and the rulers gather together, against the LORD and against His Anointed One:

verses.kjv: Let us break their bands asunder, and cast away their cords from us.
verses.bsb: “Let us break Their chains and cast away Their cords.”

verses.kjv: He that sitteth in the heavens shall laugh: the Lord shall have them in derision.
verses.bsb: The One enthroned in heaven laughs; the Lord taunts them.

verses.kjv: Then shall he speak unto them in his wrath, and vex them in his sore displeasure.
verses.bsb: Then He rebukes them in His anger, and terrifies them in His fury:

verses.kjv: Yet have I set my king upon my holy hill of Zion.
verses.bsb: “I have installed My King on Zion, upon My holy mountain.”

verses.kjv: I will declare the decree: the Lord hath said unto me, Thou art my Son; this day have I begotten thee.
verses.bsb: I will proclaim the decree spoken to Me by the LORD: “You are My Son; today I have become Your Father.

verses.kjv: Ask of me, and I shall give thee the heathen for thine inheritance, and the uttermost parts of the earth for thy possession.
verses.bsb: Ask Me, and I will make the nations Your inheritance, the ends of the earth Your possession.

verses.kjv: Thou shalt break them with a rod of iron; thou shalt dash them in pieces like a potter’s vessel.
verses.bsb: You will break them with an iron scepter; You will shatter them like pottery.”

verses.kjv: Be wise now therefore, O ye kings: be instructed, ye judges of the earth.
verses.bsb: Therefore be wise, O kings; be admonished, O judges of the earth.

verses.kjv: Serve the Lord with fear, and rejoice with trembling.
verses.bsb: Serve the LORD with fear, and rejoice with trembling.

verses.kjv: Kiss the Son, lest he be angry, and ye perish from the way, when his wrath is kindled but a little. Blessed are all they that put their trust in him.
verses.bsb: Kiss the Son, lest He be angry and you perish in your rebellion, when His wrath ignites in an instant.   Blessed are all who take refuge in Him.

verses.kjv: LORD, how are they increased that trouble me! many are they that rise up against me.
verses.bsb: O LORD, how my foes have increased! How many rise up against me!

verses.kjv: Many there be which say of my soul, There is no help for him in God. Selah.
verses.bsb: Many say of me, “God will not deliver him.” Selah

verses.kjv: But thou, O Lord, art a shield for me; my glory, and the lifter up of mine head.
verses.bsb: But You, O LORD, are a shield around me, my glory, and the One who lifts my head.

verses.kjv: I cried unto the Lord with my voice, and he heard me out of his holy hill. Selah.
verses.bsb: To the LORD I cry aloud, and He answers me from His holy mountain. Selah

verses.kjv: I laid me down and slept; I awaked; for the Lord sustained me.
verses.bsb: I lie down and sleep; I wake again, for the LORD sustains me.

verses.kjv: I will not be afraid of ten thousands of people, that have set themselves against me round about.
verses.bsb: I will not fear the myriads set against me on every side.

verses.kjv: Arise, O Lord; save me, O my God: for thou hast smitten all mine enemies upon the cheek bone; thou hast broken the teeth of the ungodly.
verses.bsb: Arise, O LORD! Save me, O my God! Strike all my enemies on the jaw; break the teeth of the wicked.

verses.kjv: Salvation belongeth unto the Lord: thy blessing is upon thy people. Selah.
verses.bsb: Salvation belongs to the LORD; may Your blessing be on Your people. Selah

verses.kjv: HEAR me when I call, O God of my righteousness: thou hast enlarged me when I was in distress; have mercy upon me, and hear my prayer.
verses.bsb: Answer me when I call, O God of my righteousness! You have relieved my distress; show me grace and hear my prayer.

verses.kjv: O ye sons of men, how long will ye turn my glory into shame? how long will ye love vanity, and seek after leasing? Selah.
verses.bsb: How long, O men, will my honor be maligned? How long will you love vanity and seek after lies? Selah

verses.kjv: But know that the Lord hath set apart him that is godly for himself: the Lord will hear when I call unto him.
verses.bsb: Know that the LORD has set apart the godly for Himself; the LORD hears when I call to Him.

verses.kjv: Stand in awe, and sin not: commune with your own heart upon your bed, and be still. Selah.
verses.bsb: Be angry, yet do not sin; on your bed, search your heart and be still. Selah

verses.kjv: Offer the sacrifices of righteousness, and put your trust in the Lord.
verses.bsb: Offer the sacrifices of the righteous and trust in the LORD.

verses.kjv: There be many that say, Who will shew us any good? Lord, lift thou up the light of thy countenance upon us.
verses.bsb: Many ask, “Who can show us the good?” Shine the light of Your face upon us, O LORD.

verses.kjv: Thou hast put gladness in my heart, more than in the time that their corn and their wine increased.
verses.bsb: You have filled my heart with more joy than when grain and new wine abound.

verses.kjv: I will both lay me down in peace, and sleep: for thou, Lord, only makest me dwell in safety.
verses.bsb: I will lie down and sleep in peace, for You alone, O LORD, make me dwell in safety.

verses.kjv: GIVE ear to my words, O Lord, consider my meditation.
verses.bsb: Give ear to my words, O LORD; consider my groaning.

verses.kjv: Hearken unto the voice of my cry, my King, and my God: for unto thee will I pray.
verses.bsb: Attend to the sound of my cry, my King and my God, for to You I pray.

verses.kjv: My voice shalt thou hear in the morning, O Lord; in the morning will I direct my prayer unto thee, and will look up.
verses.bsb: In the morning, O LORD, You hear my voice; at daybreak I lay my plea before You and wait in expectation.

verses.kjv: For thou art not a God that hath pleasure in wickedness: neither shall evil dwell with thee.
verses.bsb: For You are not a God who delights in wickedness; no evil can dwell with You.

verses.kjv: The foolish shall not stand in thy sight: thou hatest all workers of iniquity.
verses.bsb: The boastful cannot stand in Your presence; You hate all workers of iniquity.

verses.kjv: Thou shalt destroy them that speak leasing: the Lord will abhor the bloody and deceitful man.
verses.bsb: You destroy those who tell lies; the LORD abhors the man of bloodshed and deceit.

verses.kjv: But as for me, I will come into thy house in the multitude of thy mercy: and in thy fear will I worship toward thy holy temple.
verses.bsb: But I will enter Your house by the abundance of Your loving devotion; in reverence I will bow down toward Your holy temple.

verses.kjv: Lead me, O Lord, in thy righteousness because of mine enemies; make thy way straight before my face.
verses.bsb: Lead me, O LORD, in Your righteousness because of my enemies; make straight Your way before me.

verses.kjv: For there is no faithfulness in their mouth; their inward part is very wickedness; their throat is an open sepulchre; they flatter with their tongue.
verses.bsb: For not a word they speak can be trusted; destruction lies within them. Their throats are open graves; their tongues practice deceit.

verses.kjv: Destroy thou them, O God; let them fall by their own counsels; cast them out in the multitude of their transgressions; for they have rebelled against thee.
verses.bsb: Declare them guilty, O God; let them fall by their own devices. Drive them out for their many transgressions, for they have rebelled against You.

verses.kjv: But let all those that put their trust in thee rejoice: let them ever shout for joy, because thou defendest them: let them also that love thy name be joyful in thee.
verses.bsb: But let all who take refuge in You rejoice; let them ever shout for joy. May You shelter them, that those who love Your name may rejoice in You.

verses.kjv: For thou, Lord, wilt bless the righteous; with favour wilt thou compass him as with a shield.
verses.bsb: For surely You, O LORD, bless the righteous; You surround them with the shield of Your favor.

verses.kjv: O LORD, rebuke me not in thine anger, neither chasten me in thy hot displeasure.
verses.bsb: O LORD, do not rebuke me in Your anger or discipline me in Your wrath.

verses.kjv: Have mercy upon me, O Lord; for I am weak: O Lord, heal me; for my bones are vexed.
verses.bsb: Be merciful to me, O LORD, for I am frail; heal me, O LORD, for my bones are in agony.

verses.kjv: My soul is also sore vexed: but thou, O Lord, how long?
verses.bsb: My soul is deeply distressed. How long, O LORD, how long?

verses.kjv: Return, O Lord, deliver my soul: oh save me for thy mercies’ sake.
verses.bsb: Turn, O LORD, and deliver my soul; save me because of Your loving devotion.

verses.kjv: For in death there is no remembrance of thee: in the grave who shall give thee thanks?
verses.bsb: For there is no mention of You in death; who can praise You from Sheol?

verses.kjv: I am weary with my groaning; all the night make I my bed to swim; I water my couch with my tears.
verses.bsb: I am weary from groaning; all night I flood my bed with weeping and drench my couch with tears.

verses.kjv: Mine eye is consumed because of grief; it waxeth old because of all mine enemies.
verses.bsb: My eyes fail from grief; they grow dim because of all my foes.

verses.kjv: Depart from me, all ye workers of iniquity; for the Lord hath heard the voice of my weeping.
verses.bsb: Depart from me, all you workers of iniquity, for the LORD has heard my weeping.

verses.kjv: The Lord hath heard my supplication; the Lord will receive my prayer.
verses.bsb: The LORD has heard my cry for mercy; the LORD accepts my prayer.

verses.kjv: Let all mine enemies be ashamed and sore vexed: let them return and be ashamed suddenly.
verses.bsb: All my enemies will be ashamed and dismayed; they will turn back in sudden disgrace.

verses.kjv: O LORD my God, in thee do I put my trust: save me from all them that persecute me, and deliver me:
verses.bsb: O LORD my God, I take refuge in You; save me and deliver me from all my pursuers,

verses.kjv: Lest he tear my soul like a lion, rending it in pieces, while there is none to deliver.
verses.bsb: or they will shred my soul like a lion and tear me to pieces with no one to rescue me.

verses.kjv: O Lord my God, if I have done this; if there be iniquity in my hands;
verses.bsb: O LORD my God, if I have done this, if injustice is on my hands,

verses.kjv: If I have rewarded evil unto him that was at peace with me; (yea, I have delivered him that without cause is mine enemy:)
verses.bsb: if I have rewarded my ally with evil, if I have plundered my foe without cause,

verses.kjv: Let the enemy persecute my soul, and take it; yea, let him tread down my life upon the earth, and lay mine honour in the dust. Selah.
verses.bsb: then may my enemy pursue me and overtake me; may he trample me to the ground and leave my honor in the dust. Selah

verses.kjv: Arise, O Lord, in thine anger, lift up thyself because of the rage of mine enemies: and awake for me to the judgment that thou hast commanded.
verses.bsb: Arise, O LORD, in Your anger; rise up against the fury of my enemies. Awake, my God, and ordain judgment.

verses.kjv: So shall the congregation of the people compass thee about: for their sakes therefore return thou on high.
verses.bsb: Let the assembled peoples gather around You; take Your seat over them on high.

verses.kjv: The Lord shall judge the people: judge me, O Lord, according to my righteousness, and according to mine integrity that is in me.
verses.bsb: The LORD judges the peoples; vindicate me, O LORD, according to my righteousness and integrity.

verses.kjv: Oh let the wickedness of the wicked come to an end; but establish the just: for the righteous God trieth the hearts and reins.
verses.bsb: Put an end to the evil of the wicked, but establish the righteous, O righteous God who searches hearts and minds.

verses.kjv: My defence is of God, which saveth the upright in heart.
verses.bsb: My shield is with God, who saves the upright in heart.

verses.kjv: God judgeth the righteous, and God is angry with the wicked every day.
verses.bsb: God is a righteous judge and a God who feels indignation each day.

verses.kjv: If he turn not, he will whet his sword; he hath bent his bow, and made it ready.
verses.bsb: If one does not repent, God will sharpen His sword; He has bent and strung His bow.

verses.kjv: He hath also prepared for him the instruments of death; he ordaineth his arrows against the persecutors.
verses.bsb: He has prepared His deadly weapons; He ordains His arrows with fire.

verses.kjv: Behold, he travaileth with iniquity, and hath conceived mischief, and brought forth falsehood.
verses.bsb: Behold, the wicked man travails with evil; he conceives trouble and births falsehood.

verses.kjv: He made a pit, and digged it, and is fallen into the ditch which he made.
verses.bsb: He has dug a hole and hollowed it out; he has fallen into a pit of his own making.

verses.kjv: His mischief shall return upon his own head, and his violent dealing shall come down upon his own pate.
verses.bsb: His trouble recoils on himself, and his violence falls on his own head.

verses.kjv: I will praise the Lord according to his righteousness: and will sing praise to the name of the Lord most high.
verses.bsb: I will thank the LORD for His righteousness and sing praise to the name of the LORD Most High.

verses.kjv: O LORD our Lord, how excellent is thy name in all the earth! who hast set thy glory above the heavens.
verses.bsb: O LORD, our Lord, how majestic is Your name in all the earth!   You have set Your glory above the heavens.

verses.kjv: Out of the mouth of babes and sucklings hast thou ordained strength because of thine enemies, that thou mightest still the enemy and the avenger.
verses.bsb: From the mouths of children and infants You have ordained praise on account of Your adversaries, to silence the enemy and avenger.

verses.kjv: When I consider thy heavens, the work of thy fingers, the moon and the stars, which thou hast ordained;
verses.bsb: When I behold Your heavens, the work of Your fingers, the moon and the stars, which You have set in place—

verses.kjv: What is man, that thou art mindful of him? and the son of man, that thou visitest him?
verses.bsb: what is man that You are mindful of him, or the son of man that You care for him?

verses.kjv: For thou hast made him a little lower than the angels, and hast crowned him with glory and honour.
verses.bsb: You made him a little lower than the angels; You crowned him with glory and honor.

verses.kjv: Thou madest him to have dominion over the works of thy hands; thou hast put all things under his feet:
verses.bsb: You made him ruler of the works of Your hands; You have placed everything under his feet:

verses.kjv: All sheep and oxen, yea, and the beasts of the field;
verses.bsb: all sheep and oxen, and even the beasts of the field,

verses.kjv: The fowl of the air, and the fish of the sea, and whatsoever passeth through the paths of the seas.
verses.bsb: the birds of the air and the fish of the sea, all that swim the paths of the seas.

verses.kjv: O Lord our Lord, how excellent is thy name in all the earth!
verses.bsb: O LORD, our Lord, how majestic is Your name in all the earth!

verses.kjv: I WILL praise thee, O Lord, with my whole heart; I will shew forth all thy marvellous works.
verses.bsb: I will give thanks to the LORD with all my heart; I will recount all Your wonders.

verses.kjv: I will be glad and rejoice in thee: I will sing praise to thy name, O thou most High.
verses.bsb: I will be glad and rejoice in You; I will sing praise to Your name, O Most High.

verses.kjv: When mine enemies are turned back, they shall fall and perish at thy presence.
verses.bsb: When my enemies retreat, they stumble and perish before You.

verses.kjv: For thou hast maintained my right and my cause; thou satest in the throne judging right.
verses.bsb: For You have upheld my just cause; You sit on Your throne judging righteously.

verses.kjv: Thou hast rebuked the heathen, thou hast destroyed the wicked, thou hast put out their name for ever and ever.
verses.bsb: You have rebuked the nations; You have destroyed the wicked; You have erased their name forever and ever.

verses.kjv: O thou enemy, destructions are come to a perpetual end: and thou hast destroyed cities; their memorial is perished with them.
verses.bsb: The enemy has come to eternal ruin, and You have uprooted their cities; the very memory of them has vanished.

verses.kjv: But the Lord shall endure for ever: he hath prepared his throne for judgment.
verses.bsb: But the LORD abides forever; He has established His throne for judgment.

verses.kjv: And he shall judge the world in righteousness, he shall minister judgment to the people in uprightness.
verses.bsb: He judges the world with justice; He governs the people with equity.

verses.kjv: The Lord also will be a refuge for the oppressed, a refuge in times of trouble.
verses.bsb: The LORD is a refuge for the oppressed, a stronghold in times of trouble.

verses.kjv: And they that know thy name will put their trust in thee: for thou, Lord, hast not forsaken them that seek thee.
verses.bsb: Those who know Your name trust in You, for You, O LORD, have not forsaken those who seek You.

verses.kjv: Sing praises to the Lord, which dwelleth in Zion: declare among the people his doings.
verses.bsb: Sing praises to the LORD, who dwells in Zion; proclaim His deeds among the nations.

verses.kjv: When he maketh inquisition for blood, he remembereth them: he forgetteth not the cry of the humble.
verses.bsb: For the Avenger of bloodshed remembers; He does not ignore the cry of the afflicted.

verses.kjv: Have mercy upon me, O Lord; consider my trouble which I suffer of them that hate me, thou that liftest me up from the gates of death:
verses.bsb: Be merciful to me, O LORD; see how my enemies afflict me! Lift me up from the gates of death,

verses.kjv: That I may shew forth all thy praise in the gates of the daughter of Zion: I will rejoice in thy salvation.
verses.bsb: that I may declare all Your praises— that within the gates of Daughter Zion I may rejoice in Your salvation.

verses.kjv: The heathen are sunk down in the pit that they made: in the net which they hid is their own foot taken.
verses.bsb: The nations have fallen into a pit of their making; their feet are caught in the net they have hidden.

verses.kjv: The Lord is known by the judgment which he executeth: the wicked is snared in the work of his own hands. Higgaion. Selah.
verses.bsb: The LORD is known by the justice He brings; the wicked are ensnared by the work of their hands. Higgaion Selah

verses.kjv: The wicked shall be turned into hell, and all the nations that forget God.
verses.bsb: The wicked will return to Sheol— all the nations who forget God.

verses.kjv: For the needy shall not alway be forgotten: the expectation of the poor shall not perish for ever.
verses.bsb: For the needy will not always be forgotten; nor the hope of the oppressed forever dashed.

verses.kjv: Arise, O Lord; let not man prevail: let the heathen be judged in thy sight.
verses.bsb: Rise up, O LORD, do not let man prevail; let the nations be judged in Your presence.

verses.kjv: Put them in fear, O Lord: that the nations may know themselves to be but men. Selah.
verses.bsb: Lay terror upon them, O LORD; let the nations know they are but men. Selah

verses.kjv: WHY standest thou afar off, O Lord? why hidest thou thyself in times of trouble?
verses.bsb: Why, O LORD, do You stand far off? Why do You hide in times of trouble?

verses.kjv: The wicked in his pride doth persecute the poor: let them be taken in the devices that they have imagined.
verses.bsb: In pride the wicked pursue the needy; let them be caught in the schemes they devise.

verses.kjv: For the wicked boasteth of his heart’s desire, and blesseth the covetous, whom the Lord abhorreth.
verses.bsb: For the wicked man boasts in the cravings of his heart; he blesses the greedy and reviles the LORD.

verses.kjv: The wicked, through the pride of his countenance, will not seek after God: God is not in all his thoughts.
verses.bsb: In his pride the wicked man does not seek Him; in all his schemes there is no God.

verses.kjv: His ways are always grievous; thy judgments are far above out of his sight: as for all his enemies, he puffeth at them.
verses.bsb: He is secure in his ways at all times; Your lofty judgments are far from him; he sneers at all his foes.

verses.kjv: He hath said in his heart, I shall not be moved: for I shall never be in adversity.
verses.bsb: He says to himself, “I will not be moved; from age to age I am free of distress.”

verses.kjv: His mouth is full of cursing and deceit and fraud: under his tongue is mischief and vanity.
verses.bsb: His mouth is full of cursing, deceit, and violence; trouble and malice are under his tongue.

verses.kjv: He sitteth in the lurking places of the villages: in the secret places doth he murder the innocent: his eyes are privily set against the poor.
verses.bsb: He lies in wait near the villages; in ambush he slays the innocent; his eyes watch in stealth for the helpless.

verses.kjv: He lieth in wait secretly as a lion in his den: he lieth in wait to catch the poor: he doth catch the poor, when he draweth him into his net.
verses.bsb: He lies in wait like a lion in a thicket; he lurks to seize the oppressed; he catches the lowly in his net.

verses.kjv: He croucheth, and humbleth himself, that the poor may fall by his strong ones.
verses.bsb: They are crushed and beaten down; the hapless fall prey to his strength.

verses.kjv: He hath said in his heart, God hath forgotten: he hideth his face; he will never see it.
verses.bsb: He says to himself, “God has forgotten; He hides His face and never sees.”

verses.kjv: Arise, O Lord; O God, lift up thine hand: forget not the humble.
verses.bsb: Arise, O LORD! Lift up Your hand, O God! Do not forget the helpless.

verses.kjv: Wherefore doth the wicked contemn God? he hath said in his heart, Thou wilt not require it.
verses.bsb: Why has the wicked man renounced God? He says to himself, “You will never call me to account.”

verses.kjv: Thou hast seen it; for thou beholdest mischief and spite, to requite it with thy hand: the poor committeth himself unto thee; thou art the helper of the fatherless.
verses.bsb: But You have regarded trouble and grief; You see to repay it by Your hand. The victim entrusts himself to You; You are the helper of the fatherless.

verses.kjv: Break thou the arm of the wicked and the evil man: seek out his wickedness till thou find none.
verses.bsb: Break the arm of the wicked and evildoer; call him to account for his wickedness until none is left to be found.

verses.kjv: The Lord is King for ever and ever: the heathen are perished out of his land.
verses.bsb: The LORD is King forever and ever; the nations perish from His land.

verses.kjv: Lord, thou hast heard the desire of the humble: thou wilt prepare their heart, thou wilt cause thine ear to hear:
verses.bsb: You have heard, O LORD, the desire of the humble; You will strengthen their hearts. You will incline Your ear,

verses.kjv: To judge the fatherless and the oppressed, that the man of the earth may no more oppress.
verses.bsb: to vindicate the fatherless and oppressed, that the men of the earth may strike terror no more.

verses.kjv: IN the Lord put I my trust: how say ye to my soul, Flee as a bird to your mountain?
verses.bsb: In the LORD I take refuge. How then can you say to me:   “Flee like a bird to your mountain!

verses.kjv: For, lo, the wicked bend their bow, they make ready their arrow upon the string, that they may privily shoot at the upright in heart.
verses.bsb: For behold, the wicked bend their bows. They set their arrow on the string to shoot from the shadows at the upright in heart.

verses.kjv: If the foundations be destroyed, what can the righteous do?
verses.bsb: If the foundations are destroyed, what can the righteous do?”

verses.kjv: The Lord is in his holy temple, the Lord’s throne is in heaven: his eyes behold, his eyelids try, the children of men.
verses.bsb: The LORD is in His holy temple; the LORD is on His heavenly throne. His eyes are watching closely; they examine the sons of men.

verses.kjv: The Lord trieth the righteous: but the wicked and him that loveth violence his soul hateth.
verses.bsb: The LORD tests the righteous and the wicked; His soul hates the lover of violence.

verses.kjv: Upon the wicked he shall rain snares, fire and brimstone, and an horrible tempest: this shall be the portion of their cup.
verses.bsb: On the wicked He will rain down fiery coals and sulfur; a scorching wind will be their portion.

verses.kjv: For the righteous Lord loveth righteousness; his countenance doth behold the upright.
verses.bsb: For the LORD is righteous; He loves justice. The upright will see His face.

verses.kjv: HELP, Lord; for the godly man ceaseth; for the faithful fail from among the children of men.
verses.bsb: Help, O LORD, for the godly are no more; the faithful have vanished from among men.

verses.kjv: They speak vanity every one with his neighbour: with flattering lips and with a double heart do they speak.
verses.bsb: They lie to one another; they speak with flattering lips and a double heart.

verses.kjv: The Lord shall cut off all flattering lips, and the tongue that speaketh proud things:
verses.bsb: May the LORD cut off all flattering lips and every boastful tongue.

verses.kjv: Who have said, With our tongue will we prevail; our lips are our own: who is lord over us?
verses.bsb: They say, “With our tongues we will prevail. We own our lips—who can be our master?”

verses.kjv: For the oppression of the poor, for the sighing of the needy, now will I arise, saith the Lord; I will set him in safety from him that puffeth at him.
verses.bsb: “For the cause of the oppressed and for the groaning of the needy, I will now arise,” says the LORD. “I will bring safety to him who yearns.”

verses.kjv: The words of the Lord are pure words: as silver tried in a furnace of earth, purified seven times.
verses.bsb: The words of the LORD are flawless, like silver refined in a furnace, like gold purified sevenfold.

verses.kjv: Thou shalt keep them, O Lord, thou shalt preserve them from this generation for ever.
verses.bsb: You, O LORD, will keep us; You will forever guard us from this generation.

verses.kjv: The wicked walk on every side, when the vilest men are exalted.
verses.bsb: The wicked wander freely, and vileness is exalted among men.

verses.kjv: HOW long wilt thou forget me, O Lord? for ever? how long wilt thou hide thy face from me?
verses.bsb: How long, O LORD? Will You forget me forever? How long will You hide Your face from me?

verses.kjv: How long shall I take counsel in my soul, having sorrow in my heart daily? how long shall mine enemy be exalted over me?
verses.bsb: How long must I wrestle in my soul, with sorrow in my heart each day? How long will my enemy dominate me?

verses.kjv: Consider and hear me, O Lord my God: lighten mine eyes, lest I sleep the sleep of death;
verses.bsb: Consider me and respond, O LORD my God. Give light to my eyes, lest I sleep in death,

verses.kjv: Lest mine enemy say, I have prevailed against him; and those that trouble me rejoice when I am moved.
verses.bsb: lest my enemy say, “I have overcome him,” and my foes rejoice when I fall.

verses.kjv: But I have trusted in thy mercy; my heart shall rejoice in thy salvation.
verses.bsb: But I have trusted in Your loving devotion; my heart will rejoice in Your salvation.

verses.kjv: I will sing unto the Lord, because he hath dealt bountifully with me.
verses.bsb: I will sing to the LORD, for He has been good to me.

verses.kjv: THE fool hath said in his heart, There is no God. They are corrupt, they have done abominable works, there is none that doeth good.
verses.bsb: The fool says in his heart, “There is no God.”   They are corrupt; their acts are vile. There is no one who does good.

verses.kjv: The Lord looked down from heaven upon the children of men, to see if there were any that did understand, and seek God.
verses.bsb: The LORD looks down from heaven upon the sons of men to see if any understand, if any seek God.

verses.kjv: They are all gone aside, they are all together become filthy: there is none that doeth good, no, not one.
verses.bsb: All have turned away, they have together become corrupt; there is no one who does good, not even one.

verses.kjv: Have all the workers of iniquity no knowledge? who eat up my people as they eat bread, and call not upon the Lord.
verses.bsb: Will the workers of iniquity never learn?   They devour my people like bread; they refuse to call upon the LORD.

verses.kjv: There were they in great fear: for God is in the generation of the righteous.
verses.bsb: There they are, overwhelmed with dread, for God is in the company of the righteous.

verses.kjv: Ye have shamed the counsel of the poor, because the Lord is his refuge.
verses.bsb: You sinners frustrate the plans of the oppressed, yet the LORD is their shelter.

verses.kjv: Oh that the salvation of Israel were come out of Zion! when the Lord bringeth back the captivity of his people, Jacob shall rejoice, and Israel shall be glad.
verses.bsb: Oh, that the salvation of Israel would come from Zion!   When the LORD restores His captive people, let Jacob rejoice, let Israel be glad!

verses.kjv: LORD, who shall abide in thy tabernacle? who shall dwell in thy holy hill?
verses.bsb: O LORD, who may abide in Your tent? Who may dwell on Your holy mountain?

verses.kjv: He that walketh uprightly, and worketh righteousness, and speaketh the truth in his heart.
verses.bsb: He who walks with integrity and practices righteousness, who speaks the truth from his heart,

verses.kjv: He that backbiteth not with his tongue, nor doeth evil to his neighbour, nor taketh up a reproach against his neighbour.
verses.bsb: who has no slander on his tongue, who does no harm to his neighbor, who casts no scorn on his friend,

verses.kjv: In whose eyes a vile person is contemned; but he honoureth them that fear the Lord. He that sweareth to his own hurt, and changeth not.
verses.bsb: who despises the vile but honors those who fear the LORD, who does not revise a costly oath,

verses.kjv: He that putteth not out his money to usury, nor taketh reward against the innocent. He that doeth these things shall never be moved.
verses.bsb: who lends his money without interest and refuses a bribe against the innocent.   He who does these things will never be shaken.

verses.kjv: PRESERVE me, O God: for in thee do I put my trust.
verses.bsb: Preserve me, O God, for in You I take refuge.

verses.kjv: O my soul, thou hast said unto the Lord, Thou art my Lord: my goodness extendeth not to thee;
verses.bsb: I said to the LORD, “You are my Lord; apart from You I have no good thing.”

verses.kjv: But to the saints that are in the earth, and to the excellent, in whom is all my delight.
verses.bsb: As for the saints in the land, they are the excellence in whom all my delight resides.

verses.kjv: Their sorrows shall be multiplied that hasten after another god: their drink offerings of blood will I not offer, nor take up their names into my lips.
verses.bsb: Sorrows will multiply to those who chase other gods. I will not pour out their libations of blood, or speak their names with my lips.

verses.kjv: The Lord is the portion of mine inheritance and of my cup: thou maintainest my lot.
verses.bsb: The LORD is my chosen portion and my cup; You have made my lot secure.

verses.kjv: The lines are fallen unto me in pleasant places; yea, I have a goodly heritage.
verses.bsb: The lines of my boundary have fallen in pleasant places; surely my inheritance is delightful.

verses.kjv: I will bless the Lord, who hath given me counsel: my reins also instruct me in the night seasons.
verses.bsb: I will bless the LORD who counsels me; even at night my conscience instructs me.

verses.kjv: I have set the Lord always before me: because he is at my right hand, I shall not be moved.
verses.bsb: I have set the LORD always before me. Because He is at my right hand, I will not be shaken.

verses.kjv: Therefore my heart is glad, and my glory rejoiceth: my flesh also shall rest in hope.
verses.bsb: Therefore my heart is glad and my tongue rejoices; my body also will dwell securely.

verses.kjv: For thou wilt not leave my soul in hell; neither wilt thou suffer thine Holy One to see corruption.
verses.bsb: For You will not abandon my soul to Sheol, nor will You let Your Holy One see decay.

verses.kjv: Thou wilt shew me the path of life: in thy presence is fulness of joy; at thy right hand there are pleasures for evermore.
verses.bsb: You have made known to me the path of life; You will fill me with joy in Your presence, with eternal pleasures at Your right hand.

verses.kjv: HEAR the right, O Lord, attend unto my cry, give ear unto my prayer, that goeth not out of feigned lips.
verses.bsb: Hear, O LORD, my righteous plea; listen to my cry. Give ear to my prayer— it comes from lips free of deceit.

verses.kjv: Let my sentence come forth from thy presence; let thine eyes behold the things that are equal.
verses.bsb: May my vindication come from Your presence; may Your eyes see what is right.

verses.kjv: Thou hast proved mine heart; thou hast visited me in the night; thou hast tried me, and shalt find nothing; I am purposed that my mouth shall not transgress.
verses.bsb: You have tried my heart; You have visited me in the night. You have tested me and found no evil; I have resolved not to sin with my mouth.

verses.kjv: Concerning the works of men, by the word of thy lips I have kept me from the paths of the destroyer.
verses.bsb: As for the deeds of men— by the word of Your lips I have avoided the ways of the violent.

verses.kjv: Hold up my goings in thy paths, that my footsteps slip not.
verses.bsb: My steps have held to Your paths; my feet have not slipped.

verses.kjv: I have called upon thee, for thou wilt hear me, O God: incline thine ear unto me, and hear my speech.
verses.bsb: I call on You, O God, for You will answer me. Incline Your ear to me; hear my words.

verses.kjv: Shew thy marvellous lovingkindness, O thou that savest by thy right hand them which put their trust in thee from those that rise up against them.
verses.bsb: Show the wonders of Your loving devotion, You who save by Your right hand those who seek refuge from their foes.

verses.kjv: Keep me as the apple of the eye, hide me under the shadow of thy wings,
verses.bsb: Keep me as the apple of Your eye; hide me in the shadow of Your wings

verses.kjv: From the wicked that oppress me, from my deadly enemies, who compass me about.
verses.bsb: from the wicked who assail me, from my mortal enemies who surround me.

verses.kjv: They are inclosed in their own fat: with their mouth they speak proudly.
verses.bsb: They have closed their callous hearts; their mouths speak with arrogance.

verses.kjv: They have now compassed us in our steps: they have set their eyes bowing down to the earth;
verses.bsb: They have tracked us down, and now surround us; their eyes are set to cast us to the ground,

verses.kjv: Like as a lion that is greedy of his prey, and as it were a young lion lurking in secret places.
verses.bsb: like a lion greedy for prey, like a young lion lurking in ambush.

verses.kjv: Arise, O Lord, disappoint him, cast him down: deliver my soul from the wicked, which is thy sword:
verses.bsb: Arise, O LORD, confront them! Bring them to their knees; deliver me from the wicked by Your sword,

verses.kjv: From men which are thy hand, O Lord, from men of the world, which have their portion in this life, and whose belly thou fillest with thy hid treasure: they are full of children, and leave the rest of their substance to their babes.
verses.bsb: from such men, O LORD, by Your hand— from men of the world whose portion is in this life. May You fill the bellies of Your treasured ones and satisfy their sons, so they leave their abundance to their children.

verses.kjv: As for me, I will behold thy face in righteousness: I shall be satisfied, when I awake, with thy likeness.
verses.bsb: As for me, I will behold Your face in righteousness; when I awake, I will be satisfied in Your presence.

verses.kjv: I WILL love thee, O Lord, my strength.
verses.bsb: I love You, O LORD, my strength.

verses.kjv: The Lord is my rock, and my fortress, and my deliverer; my God, my strength, in whom I will trust; my buckler, and the horn of my salvation, and my high tower.
verses.bsb: The LORD is my rock, my fortress, and my deliverer. My God is my rock, in whom I take refuge, my shield, and the horn of my salvation, my stronghold.

verses.kjv: I will call upon the Lord, who is worthy to be praised: so shall I be saved from mine enemies.
verses.bsb: I will call upon the LORD, who is worthy to be praised; so shall I be saved from my enemies.

verses.kjv: The sorrows of death compassed me, and the floods of ungodly men made me afraid.
verses.bsb: The cords of death encompassed me; the torrents of chaos overwhelmed me.

verses.kjv: The sorrows of hell compassed me about: the snares of death prevented me.
verses.bsb: The cords of Sheol entangled me; the snares of death confronted me.

verses.kjv: In my distress I called upon the Lord, and cried unto my God: he heard my voice out of his temple, and my cry came before him, even into his ears.
verses.bsb: In my distress I called upon the LORD; I cried to my God for help. From His temple He heard my voice, and my cry for His help reached His ears.

verses.kjv: Then the earth shook and trembled; the foundations also of the hills moved and were shaken, because he was wroth.
verses.bsb: Then the earth shook and quaked, and the foundations of the mountains trembled; they were shaken because He burned with anger.

verses.kjv: There went up a smoke out of his nostrils, and fire out of his mouth devoured: coals were kindled by it.
verses.bsb: Smoke rose from His nostrils, and consuming fire came from His mouth; glowing coals blazed forth.

verses.kjv: He bowed the heavens also, and came down: and darkness was under his feet.
verses.bsb: He parted the heavens and came down with dark clouds beneath His feet.

verses.kjv: And he rode upon a cherub, and did fly: yea, he did fly upon the wings of the wind.
verses.bsb: He mounted a cherub and flew; He soared on the wings of the wind.

verses.kjv: He made darkness his secret place; his pavilion round about him were dark waters and thick clouds of the skies.
verses.bsb: He made darkness His hiding place, and storm clouds a canopy around Him.

verses.kjv: At the brightness that was before him his thick clouds passed, hail stones and coals of fire.
verses.bsb: From the brightness of His presence His clouds advanced— hailstones and coals of fire.

verses.kjv: The Lord also thundered in the heavens, and the Highest gave his voice; hail stones and coals of fire.
verses.bsb: The LORD thundered from heaven; the voice of the Most High resounded— hailstones and coals of fire.

verses.kjv: Yea, he sent out his arrows, and scattered them; and he shot out lightnings, and discomfited them.
verses.bsb: He shot His arrows and scattered the foes; He hurled lightning and routed them.

verses.kjv: Then the channels of waters were seen, and the foundations of the world were discovered at thy rebuke, O Lord, at the blast of the breath of thy nostrils.
verses.bsb: The channels of the sea appeared, and the foundations of the world were exposed, at Your rebuke, O LORD, at the blast of the breath of Your nostrils.

verses.kjv: He sent from above, he took me, he drew me out of many waters.
verses.bsb: He reached down from on high and took hold of me; He drew me out of deep waters.

verses.kjv: He delivered me from my strong enemy, and from them which hated me: for they were too strong for me.
verses.bsb: He rescued me from my powerful enemy, from foes too mighty for me.

verses.kjv: They prevented me in the day of my calamity: but the Lord was my stay.
verses.bsb: They confronted me in my day of calamity, but the LORD was my support.

verses.kjv: He brought me forth also into a large place; he delivered me, because he delighted in me.
verses.bsb: He brought me out into the open; He rescued me because He delighted in me.

verses.kjv: The Lord rewarded me according to my righteousness; according to the cleanness of my hands hath he recompensed me.
verses.bsb: The LORD has rewarded me according to my righteousness; He has repaid me according to the cleanness of my hands.

verses.kjv: For I have kept the ways of the Lord, and have not wickedly departed from my God.
verses.bsb: For I have kept the ways of the LORD and have not wickedly departed from my God.

verses.kjv: For all his judgments were before me, and I did not put away his statutes from me.
verses.bsb: For all His ordinances are before me; I have not disregarded His statutes.

verses.kjv: I was also upright before him, and I kept myself from mine iniquity.
verses.bsb: And I have been blameless before Him and kept myself from iniquity.

verses.kjv: Therefore hath the Lord recompensed me according to my righteousness, according to the cleanness of my hands in his eyesight.
verses.bsb: So the LORD has repaid me according to my righteousness, according to the cleanness of my hands in His sight.

verses.kjv: With the merciful thou wilt shew thyself merciful; with an upright man thou wilt shew thyself upright;
verses.bsb: To the faithful You show Yourself faithful, to the blameless You show Yourself blameless;

verses.kjv: With the pure thou wilt shew thyself pure; and with the froward thou wilt shew thyself froward.
verses.bsb: to the pure You show Yourself pure, but to the crooked You show Yourself shrewd.

verses.kjv: For thou wilt save the afflicted people; but wilt bring down high looks.
verses.bsb: For You save an afflicted people, but You humble those with haughty eyes.

verses.kjv: For thou wilt light my candle: the Lord my God will enlighten my darkness.
verses.bsb: For You, O LORD, light my lamp; my God lights up my darkness.

verses.kjv: For by thee I have run through a troop; and by my God have I leaped over a wall.
verses.bsb: For in You I can charge an army, and with my God I can scale a wall.

verses.kjv: As for God, his way is perfect: the word of the Lord is tried: he is a buckler to all those that trust in him.
verses.bsb: As for God, His way is perfect; the word of the LORD is flawless. He is a shield to all who take refuge in Him.

verses.kjv: For who is God save the Lord? or who is a rock save our God?
verses.bsb: For who is God besides the LORD? And who is the Rock except our God?

verses.kjv: It is God that girdeth me with strength, and maketh my way perfect.
verses.bsb: It is God who arms me with strength and makes my way clear.

verses.kjv: He maketh my feet like hinds’ feet, and setteth me upon my high places.
verses.bsb: He makes my feet like those of a deer and stations me upon the heights.

verses.kjv: He teacheth my hands to war, so that a bow of steel is broken by mine arms.
verses.bsb: He trains my hands for battle; my arms can bend a bow of bronze.

verses.kjv: Thou hast also given me the shield of thy salvation: and thy right hand hath holden me up, and thy gentleness hath made me great.
verses.bsb: You have given me Your shield of salvation; Your right hand upholds me, and Your gentleness exalts me.

verses.kjv: Thou hast enlarged my steps under me, that my feet did not slip.
verses.bsb: You broaden the path beneath me so that my ankles do not give way.

verses.kjv: I have pursued mine enemies, and overtaken them: neither did I turn again till they were consumed.
verses.bsb: I pursued my enemies and overtook them; I did not turn back until they were consumed.

verses.kjv: I have wounded them that they were not able to rise: they are fallen under my feet.
verses.bsb: I crushed them so they could not rise; they have fallen under my feet.

verses.kjv: For thou hast girded me with strength unto the battle: thou hast subdued under me those that rose up against me.
verses.bsb: You have armed me with strength for battle; You have subdued my foes beneath me.

verses.kjv: Thou hast also given me the necks of mine enemies; that I might destroy them that hate me.
verses.bsb: You have made my enemies retreat before me; I put an end to those who hated me.

verses.kjv: They cried, but there was none to save them: even unto the Lord, but he answered them not.
verses.bsb: They cried for help, but there was no one to save them— to the LORD, but He did not answer.

verses.kjv: Then did I beat them small as the dust before the wind: I did cast them out as the dirt in the streets.
verses.bsb: I ground them as dust in the face of the wind; I trampled them like mud in the streets.

verses.kjv: Thou hast delivered me from the strivings of the people; and thou hast made me the head of the heathen: a people whom I have not known shall serve me.
verses.bsb: You have delivered me from the strife of the people; You have made me the head of nations; a people I had not known shall serve me.

verses.kjv: As soon as they hear of me, they shall obey me: the strangers shall submit themselves unto me.
verses.bsb: When they hear me, they obey me; foreigners cower before me.

verses.kjv: The strangers shall fade away, and be afraid out of their close places.
verses.bsb: Foreigners lose heart and come trembling from their strongholds.

verses.kjv: The Lord liveth; and blessed be my rock; and let the God of my salvation be exalted.
verses.bsb: The LORD lives, and blessed be my Rock! And may the God of my salvation be exalted—

verses.kjv: It is God that avengeth me, and subdueth the people under me.
verses.bsb: the God who avenges me and subdues nations beneath me,

verses.kjv: He delivereth me from mine enemies: yea, thou liftest me up above those that rise up against me: thou hast delivered me from the violent man.
verses.bsb: who delivers me from my enemies. You exalt me above my foes; You rescue me from violent men.

verses.kjv: Therefore will I give thanks unto thee, O Lord, among the heathen, and sing praises unto thy name.
verses.bsb: Therefore I will praise You, O LORD, among the nations; I will sing praises to Your name.

verses.kjv: Great deliverance giveth he to his king; and sheweth mercy to his anointed, to David, and to his seed for evermore.
verses.bsb: Great salvation He brings to His king. He shows loving devotion to His anointed, to David and his descendants forever.

verses.kjv: THE heavens declare the glory of God; and the firmament sheweth his handywork.
verses.bsb: The heavens declare the glory of God; the skies proclaim the work of His hands.

verses.kjv: Day unto day uttereth speech, and night unto night sheweth knowledge.
verses.bsb: Day after day they pour forth speech; night after night they reveal knowledge.

verses.kjv: There is no speech nor language, where their voice is not heard.
verses.bsb: Without speech or language, without a sound to be heard,

verses.kjv: Their line is gone out through all the earth, and their words to the end of the world. In them hath he set a tabernacle for the sun,
verses.bsb: their voice has gone out into all the earth, their words to the ends of the world.   In the heavens He has pitched a tent for the sun.

verses.kjv: Which is as a bridegroom coming out of his chamber, and rejoiceth as a strong man to run a race.
verses.bsb: Like a bridegroom emerging from his chamber, like a champion rejoicing to run his course,

verses.kjv: His going forth is from the end of the heaven, and his circuit unto the ends of it: and there is nothing hid from the heat thereof.
verses.bsb: it rises at one end of the heavens and runs its circuit to the other; nothing is deprived of its warmth.

verses.kjv: The law of the Lord is perfect, converting the soul: the testimony of the Lord is sure, making wise the simple.
verses.bsb: The Law of the LORD is perfect, reviving the soul; the testimony of the LORD is trustworthy, making wise the simple.

verses.kjv: The statutes of the Lord are right, rejoicing the heart: the commandment of the Lord is pure, enlightening the eyes.
verses.bsb: The precepts of the LORD are right, bringing joy to the heart; the commandments of the LORD are radiant, giving light to the eyes.

verses.kjv: The fear of the Lord is clean, enduring for ever: the judgments of the Lord are true and righteous altogether.
verses.bsb: The fear of the LORD is pure, enduring forever; the judgments of the LORD are true, being altogether righteous.

verses.kjv: More to be desired are they than gold, yea, than much fine gold: sweeter also than honey and the honeycomb.
verses.bsb: They are more precious than gold, than much pure gold; they are sweeter than honey, than honey from the comb.

verses.kjv: Moreover by them is thy servant warned: and in keeping of them there is great reward.
verses.bsb: By them indeed Your servant is warned; in keeping them is great reward.

verses.kjv: Who can understand his errors? cleanse thou me from secret faults.
verses.bsb: Who can discern his own errors? Cleanse me from my hidden faults.

verses.kjv: Keep back thy servant also from presumptuous sins; let them not have dominion over me: then shall I be upright, and I shall be innocent from the great transgression.
verses.bsb: Keep Your servant also from willful sins; may they not rule over me. Then I will be blameless and cleansed of great transgression.

verses.kjv: Let the words of my mouth, and the meditation of my heart, be acceptable in thy sight, O Lord, my strength, and my redeemer.
verses.bsb: May the words of my mouth and the meditation of my heart be pleasing in Your sight, O LORD, my Rock and my Redeemer.

verses.kjv: THE Lord hear thee in the day of trouble; the name of the God of Jacob defend thee;
verses.bsb: May the LORD answer you in the day of trouble; may the name of the God of Jacob protect you.

verses.kjv: Send thee help from the sanctuary, and strengthen thee out of Zion;
verses.bsb: May He send you help from the sanctuary and sustain you from Zion.

verses.kjv: Remember all thy offerings, and accept thy burnt sacrifice; Selah.
verses.bsb: May He remember all your gifts and look favorably on your burnt offerings. Selah

verses.kjv: Grant thee according to thine own heart, and fulfil all thy counsel.
verses.bsb: May He give you the desires of your heart and make all your plans succeed.

verses.kjv: We will rejoice in thy salvation, and in the name of our God we will set up our banners: the Lord fulfil all thy petitions.
verses.bsb: May we shout for joy at your victory and raise a banner in the name of our God. May the LORD grant all your petitions.

verses.kjv: Now know I that the Lord saveth his anointed; he will hear him from his holy heaven with the saving strength of his right hand.
verses.bsb: Now I know that the LORD saves His anointed; He answers him from His holy heaven with the saving power of His right hand.

verses.kjv: Some trust in chariots, and some in horses: but we will remember the name of the Lord our God.
verses.bsb: Some trust in chariots and others in horses, but we trust in the name of the LORD our God.

verses.kjv: They are brought down and fallen: but we are risen, and stand upright.
verses.bsb: They collapse and fall, but we rise up and stand firm.

verses.kjv: Save, Lord: let the king hear us when we call.
verses.bsb: O LORD, save the king. Answer us on the day we call.

verses.kjv: THE king shall joy in thy strength, O Lord; and in thy salvation how greatly shall he rejoice!
verses.bsb: O LORD, the king rejoices in Your strength. How greatly he exults in Your salvation!

verses.kjv: Thou hast given him his heart’s desire, and hast not withholden the request of his lips. Selah.
verses.bsb: You have granted his heart’s desire and have not withheld the request of his lips. Selah

verses.kjv: For thou preventest him with the blessings of goodness: thou settest a crown of pure gold on his head.
verses.bsb: For You welcomed him with rich blessings; You placed on his head a crown of pure gold.

verses.kjv: He asked life of thee, and thou gavest it him, even length of days for ever and ever.
verses.bsb: He asked You for life, and You granted it— length of days, forever and ever.

verses.kjv: His glory is great in thy salvation: honour and majesty hast thou laid upon him.
verses.bsb: Great is his glory in Your salvation; You bestow on him splendor and majesty.

verses.kjv: For thou hast made him most blessed for ever: thou hast made him exceeding glad with thy countenance.
verses.bsb: For You grant him blessings forever; You cheer him with joy in Your presence.

verses.kjv: For the king trusteth in the Lord, and through the mercy of the most High he shall not be moved.
verses.bsb: For the king trusts in the LORD; through the loving devotion of the Most High, he will not be shaken.

verses.kjv: Thine hand shall find out all thine enemies: thy right hand shall find out those that hate thee.
verses.bsb: Your hand will apprehend all Your enemies; Your right hand will seize those who hate You.

verses.kjv: Thou shalt make them as a fiery oven in the time of thine anger: the Lord shall swallow them up in his wrath, and the fire shall devour them.
verses.bsb: You will place them in a fiery furnace at the time of Your appearing.   In His wrath the LORD will engulf them, and the fire will consume them.

verses.kjv: Their fruit shalt thou destroy from the earth, and their seed from among the children of men.
verses.bsb: You will wipe their descendants from the earth, and their offspring from the sons of men.

verses.kjv: For they intended evil against thee: they imagined a mischievous device, which they are not able to perform.
verses.bsb: Though they intend You harm, the schemes they devise will not prevail.

verses.kjv: Therefore shalt thou make them turn their back, when thou shalt make ready thine arrows upon thy strings against the face of them.
verses.bsb: For You will put them to flight when Your bow is trained upon them.

verses.kjv: Be thou exalted, Lord, in thine own strength: so will we sing and praise thy power.
verses.bsb: Be exalted, O LORD, in Your strength; we will sing and praise Your power.

verses.kjv: MY God, my God, why hast thou forsaken me? why art thou so far from helping me, and from the words of my roaring?
verses.bsb: My God, my God, why have You forsaken me? Why are You so far from saving me, so far from my words of groaning?

verses.kjv: O my God, I cry in the daytime, but thou hearest not; and in the night season, and am not silent.
verses.bsb: I cry out by day, O my God, but You do not answer, and by night, but I have no rest.

verses.kjv: But thou art holy, O thou that inhabitest the praises of Israel.
verses.bsb: Yet You are holy, enthroned on the praises of Israel.

verses.kjv: Our fathers trusted in thee: they trusted, and thou didst deliver them.
verses.bsb: In You our fathers trusted; they trusted and You delivered them.

verses.kjv: They cried unto thee, and were delivered: they trusted in thee, and were not confounded.
verses.bsb: They cried out to You and were set free; they trusted in You and were not disappointed.

verses.kjv: But I am a worm, and no man; a reproach of men, and despised of the people.
verses.bsb: But I am a worm and not a man, scorned by men and despised by the people.

verses.kjv: All they that see me laugh me to scorn: they shoot out the lip, they shake the head, saying,
verses.bsb: All who see me mock me; they sneer and shake their heads:

verses.kjv: He trusted on the Lord that he would deliver him: let him deliver him, seeing he delighted in him.
verses.bsb: “He trusts in the LORD, let the LORD deliver him; let the LORD rescue him, since He delights in him.”

verses.kjv: But thou art he that took me out of the womb: thou didst make me hope when I was upon my mother’s breasts.
verses.bsb: Yet You brought me forth from the womb; You made me secure at my mother’s breast.

verses.kjv: I was cast upon thee from the womb: thou art my God from my mother’s belly.
verses.bsb: From birth I was cast upon You; from my mother’s womb You have been my God.

verses.kjv: Be not far from me; for trouble is near; for there is none to help.
verses.bsb: Be not far from me, for trouble is near and there is no one to help.

verses.kjv: Many bulls have compassed me: strong bulls of Bashan have beset me round.
verses.bsb: Many bulls surround me; strong bulls of Bashan encircle me.

verses.kjv: They gaped upon me with their mouths, as a ravening and a roaring lion.
verses.bsb: They open their jaws against me like lions that roar and maul.

verses.kjv: I am poured out like water, and all my bones are out of joint: my heart is like wax; it is melted in the midst of my bowels.
verses.bsb: I am poured out like water, and all my bones are disjointed. My heart is like wax; it melts away within me.

verses.kjv: My strength is dried up like a potsherd; and my tongue cleaveth to my jaws; and thou hast brought me into the dust of death.
verses.bsb: My strength is dried up like a potsherd, and my tongue sticks to the roof of my mouth. You lay me in the dust of death.

verses.kjv: For dogs have compassed me: the assembly of the wicked have inclosed me: they pierced my hands and my feet.
verses.bsb: For dogs surround me; a band of evil men encircles me; they have pierced my hands and feet.

verses.kjv: I may tell all my bones: they look and stare upon me.
verses.bsb: I can count all my bones; they stare and gloat over me.

verses.kjv: They part my garments among them, and cast lots upon my vesture.
verses.bsb: They divide my garments among them and cast lots for my clothing.

verses.kjv: But be not thou far from me, O Lord: O my strength, haste thee to help me.
verses.bsb: But You, O LORD, be not far off; O my Strength, come quickly to help me.

verses.kjv: Deliver my soul from the sword; my darling from the power of the dog.
verses.bsb: Deliver my soul from the sword, my precious life from the power of wild dogs.

verses.kjv: Save me from the lion’s mouth: for thou hast heard me from the horns of the unicorns.
verses.bsb: Save me from the mouth of the lion; at the horns of the wild oxen You have answered me!

verses.kjv: I will declare thy name unto my brethren: in the midst of the congregation will I praise thee.
verses.bsb: I will proclaim Your name to my brothers; I will praise You in the assembly.

verses.kjv: Ye that fear the Lord, praise him; all ye the seed of Jacob, glorify him; and fear him, all ye the seed of Israel.
verses.bsb: You who fear the LORD, praise Him! All descendants of Jacob, honor Him! All offspring of Israel, revere Him!

verses.kjv: For he hath not despised nor abhorred the affliction of the afflicted; neither hath he hid his face from him; but when he cried unto him, he heard.
verses.bsb: For He has not despised or detested the torment of the afflicted. He has not hidden His face from him, but has attended to his cry for help.

verses.kjv: My praise shall be of thee in the great congregation: I will pay my vows before them that fear him.
verses.bsb: My praise for You resounds in the great assembly; I will fulfill my vows before those who fear You.

verses.kjv: The meek shall eat and be satisfied: they shall praise the Lord that seek him: your heart shall live for ever.
verses.bsb: The poor will eat and be satisfied; those who seek the LORD will praise Him. May your hearts live forever!

verses.kjv: All the ends of the world shall remember and turn unto the Lord: and all the kindreds of the nations shall worship before thee.
verses.bsb: All the ends of the earth will remember and turn to the LORD. All the families of the nations will bow down before Him.

verses.kjv: For the kingdom is the Lord’s: and he is the governor among the nations.
verses.bsb: For dominion belongs to the LORD and He rules over the nations.

verses.kjv: All they that be fat upon earth shall eat and worship: all they that go down to the dust shall bow before him: and none can keep alive his own soul.
verses.bsb: All the rich of the earth will feast and worship; all who go down to the dust will kneel before Him— even those unable to preserve their lives.

verses.kjv: A seed shall serve him; it shall be accounted to the Lord for a generation.
verses.bsb: Posterity will serve Him; they will declare the Lord to a new generation.

verses.kjv: They shall come, and shall declare his righteousness unto a people that shall be born, that he hath done this.
verses.bsb: They will come and proclaim His righteousness to a people yet unborn— all that He has done.

verses.kjv: THE Lord is my shepherd; I shall not want.
verses.bsb: The LORD is my shepherd; I shall not want.

verses.kjv: He maketh me to lie down in green pastures: he leadeth me beside the still waters.
verses.bsb: He makes me lie down in green pastures; He leads me beside quiet waters.

verses.kjv: He restoreth my soul: he leadeth me in the paths of righteousness for his name’s sake.
verses.bsb: He restores my soul; He guides me in the paths of righteousness for the sake of His name.

verses.kjv: Yea, though I walk through the valley of the shadow of death, I will fear no evil: for thou art with me; thy rod and thy staff they comfort me.
verses.bsb: Even though I walk through the valley of the shadow of death, I will fear no evil, for You are with me; Your rod and Your staff, they comfort me.

verses.kjv: Thou preparest a table before me in the presence of mine enemies: thou anointest my head with oil; my cup runneth over.
verses.bsb: You prepare a table before me in the presence of my enemies. You anoint my head with oil; my cup overflows.

verses.kjv: Surely goodness and mercy shall follow me all the days of my life: and I will dwell in the house of the Lord for ever.
verses.bsb: Surely goodness and mercy will follow me all the days of my life, and I will dwell in the house of the LORD forever.

verses.kjv: THE earth is the Lord’s, and the fulness thereof; the world, and they that dwell therein.
verses.bsb: The earth is the LORD’s, and the fullness thereof, the world and all who dwell therein.

verses.kjv: For he hath founded it upon the seas, and established it upon the floods.
verses.bsb: For He has founded it upon the seas and established it upon the waters.

verses.kjv: Who shall ascend into the hill of the Lord? or who shall stand in his holy place?
verses.bsb: Who may ascend the hill of the LORD? Who may stand in His holy place?

verses.kjv: He that hath clean hands, and a pure heart; who hath not lifted up his soul unto vanity, nor sworn deceitfully.
verses.bsb: He who has clean hands and a pure heart, who does not lift up his soul to an idol or swear deceitfully.

verses.kjv: He shall receive the blessing from the Lord, and righteousness from the God of his salvation.
verses.bsb: He will receive blessing from the LORD and vindication from the God of his salvation.

verses.kjv: This is the generation of them that seek him, that seek thy face, O Jacob. Selah.
verses.bsb: Such is the generation of those who seek Him, who seek Your face, O God of Jacob. Selah

verses.kjv: Lift up your heads, O ye gates; and be ye lift up, ye everlasting doors; and the King of glory shall come in.
verses.bsb: Lift up your heads, O gates! Be lifted up, O ancient doors, that the King of Glory may enter!

verses.kjv: Who is this King of glory? The Lord strong and mighty, the Lord mighty in battle.
verses.bsb: Who is this King of Glory? The LORD strong and mighty, the LORD mighty in battle.

verses.kjv: Lift up your heads, O ye gates; even lift them up, ye everlasting doors; and the King of glory shall come in.
verses.bsb: Lift up your heads, O gates! Be lifted up, O ancient doors, that the King of Glory may enter!

verses.kjv: Who is this King of glory? The Lord of hosts, he is the King of glory. Selah.
verses.bsb: Who is He, this King of Glory? The LORD of Hosts— He is the King of Glory. Selah

verses.kjv: UNTO thee, O Lord, do I lift up my soul.
verses.bsb: To You, O LORD, I lift up my soul;

verses.kjv: O my God, I trust in thee: let me not be ashamed, let not mine enemies triumph over me.
verses.bsb: in You, my God, I trust. Do not let me be put to shame; do not let my enemies exult over me.

verses.kjv: Yea, let none that wait on thee be ashamed: let them be ashamed which transgress without cause.
verses.bsb: Surely none who wait for You will be put to shame; but those who are faithless without cause will be disgraced.

verses.kjv: Shew me thy ways, O Lord; teach me thy paths.
verses.bsb: Show me Your ways, O LORD; teach me Your paths.

verses.kjv: Lead me in thy truth, and teach me: for thou art the God of my salvation; on thee do I wait all the day.
verses.bsb: Guide me in Your truth and teach me, for You are the God of my salvation; all day long I wait for You.

verses.kjv: Remember, O Lord, thy tender mercies and thy lovingkindnesses; for they have been ever of old.
verses.bsb: Remember, O LORD, Your compassion and loving devotion, for they are from age to age.

verses.kjv: Remember not the sins of my youth, nor my transgressions: according to thy mercy remember thou me for thy goodness’ sake, O Lord.
verses.bsb: Remember not the sins of my youth, nor my rebellious acts; remember me according to Your loving devotion, because of Your goodness, O LORD.

verses.kjv: Good and upright is the Lord: therefore will he teach sinners in the way.
verses.bsb: Good and upright is the LORD; therefore He shows sinners the way.

verses.kjv: The meek will he guide in judgment: and the meek will he teach his way.
verses.bsb: He guides the humble in what is right and teaches them His way.

verses.kjv: All the paths of the Lord are mercy and truth unto such as keep his covenant and his testimonies.
verses.bsb: All the LORD’s ways are loving and faithful to those who keep His covenant and His decrees.

verses.kjv: For thy name’s sake, O Lord, pardon mine iniquity; for it is great.
verses.bsb: For the sake of Your name, O LORD, forgive my iniquity, for it is great.

verses.kjv: What man is he that feareth the Lord? him shall he teach in the way that he shall choose.
verses.bsb: Who is the man who fears the LORD? He will instruct him in the path chosen for him.

verses.kjv: His soul shall dwell at ease; and his seed shall inherit the earth.
verses.bsb: His soul will dwell in prosperity, and his descendants will inherit the land.

verses.kjv: The secret of the Lord is with them that fear him; and he will shew them his covenant.
verses.bsb: The LORD confides in those who fear Him, and reveals His covenant to them.

verses.kjv: Mine eyes are ever toward the Lord; for he shall pluck my feet out of the net.
verses.bsb: My eyes are always on the LORD, for He will free my feet from the mesh.

verses.kjv: Turn thee unto me, and have mercy upon me; for I am desolate and afflicted.
verses.bsb: Turn to me and be gracious, for I am lonely and afflicted.

verses.kjv: The troubles of my heart are enlarged: O bring thou me out of my distresses.
verses.bsb: The troubles of my heart increase; free me from my distress.

verses.kjv: Look upon mine affliction and my pain; and forgive all my sins.
verses.bsb: Consider my affliction and trouble, and take away all my sins.

verses.kjv: Consider mine enemies; for they are many; and they hate me with cruel hatred.
verses.bsb: Consider my enemies, for they are many, and they hate me with vicious hatred.

verses.kjv: O keep my soul, and deliver me: let me not be ashamed; for I put my trust in thee.
verses.bsb: Guard my soul and deliver me; let me not be put to shame, for I take refuge in You.

verses.kjv: Let integrity and uprightness preserve me; for I wait on thee.
verses.bsb: May integrity and uprightness preserve me, because I wait for You.

verses.kjv: Redeem Israel, O God, out of all his troubles.
verses.bsb: Redeem Israel, O God, from all its distress.

verses.kjv: JUDGE me, O Lord; for I have walked in mine integrity: I have trusted also in the Lord; therefore I shall not slide.
verses.bsb: Vindicate me, O LORD!   For I have walked with integrity; I have trusted in the LORD without wavering.

verses.kjv: Examine me, O Lord, and prove me; try my reins and my heart.
verses.bsb: Test me, O LORD, and try me; examine my heart and mind.

verses.kjv: For thy lovingkindness is before mine eyes: and I have walked in thy truth.
verses.bsb: For Your loving devotion is before my eyes, and I have walked in Your truth.

verses.kjv: I have not sat with vain persons, neither will I go in with dissemblers.
verses.bsb: I do not sit with deceitful men, nor keep company with hypocrites.

verses.kjv: I have hated the congregation of evil doers; and will not sit with the wicked.
verses.bsb: I hate the mob of evildoers, and refuse to sit with the wicked.

verses.kjv: I will wash mine hands in innocency: so will I compass thine altar, O Lord:
verses.bsb: I wash my hands in innocence that I may go about Your altar, O LORD,

verses.kjv: That I may publish with the voice of thanksgiving, and tell of all thy wondrous works.
verses.bsb: to raise my voice in thanksgiving and declare all Your wonderful works.

verses.kjv: Lord, I have loved the habitation of thy house, and the place where thine honour dwelleth.
verses.bsb: O LORD, I love the house where You dwell, the place where Your glory resides.

verses.kjv: Gather not my soul with sinners, nor my life with bloody men:
verses.bsb: Do not take my soul away with sinners, or my life with men of bloodshed,

verses.kjv: In whose hands is mischief, and their right hand is full of bribes.
verses.bsb: in whose hands are wicked schemes, whose right hands are full of bribes.

verses.kjv: But as for me, I will walk in mine integrity: redeem me, and be merciful unto me.
verses.bsb: But I will walk with integrity; redeem me and be merciful to me.

verses.kjv: My foot standeth in an even place: in the congregations will I bless the Lord.
verses.bsb: My feet stand on level ground; in the congregations I will bless the LORD.

verses.kjv: THE Lord is my light and my salvation; whom shall I fear? the Lord is the strength of my life; of whom shall I be afraid?
verses.bsb: The LORD is my light and my salvation— whom shall I fear? The LORD is the stronghold of my life— whom shall I dread?

verses.kjv: When the wicked, even mine enemies and my foes, came upon me to eat up my flesh, they stumbled and fell.
verses.bsb: When the wicked came upon me to devour my flesh, my enemies and foes stumbled and fell.

verses.kjv: Though an host should encamp against me, my heart shall not fear: though war should rise against me, in this will I be confident.
verses.bsb: Though an army encamps around me, my heart will not fear; though a war breaks out against me, I will keep my trust.

verses.kjv: One thing have I desired of the Lord, that will I seek after; that I may dwell in the house of the Lord all the days of my life, to behold the beauty of the Lord, and to inquire in his temple.
verses.bsb: One thing I have asked of the LORD; this is what I desire: to dwell in the house of the LORD all the days of my life, to gaze on the beauty of the LORD and seek Him in His temple.

verses.kjv: For in the time of trouble he shall hide me in his pavilion: in the secret of his tabernacle shall he hide me; he shall set me up upon a rock.
verses.bsb: For in the day of trouble He will hide me in His shelter; He will conceal me under the cover of His tent; He will set me high upon a rock.

verses.kjv: And now shall mine head be lifted up above mine enemies round about me: therefore will I offer in his tabernacle sacrifices of joy; I will sing, yea, I will sing praises unto the Lord.
verses.bsb: Then my head will be held high above my enemies around me. At His tabernacle I will offer sacrifices with shouts of joy; I will sing and make music to the LORD.

verses.kjv: Hear, O Lord, when I cry with my voice: have mercy also upon me, and answer me.
verses.bsb: Hear, O LORD, my voice when I call; be merciful and answer me.

verses.kjv: When thou saidst, Seek ye my face; my heart said unto thee, Thy face, Lord, will I seek.
verses.bsb: My heart said, “Seek His face.” Your face, O LORD, I will seek.

verses.kjv: Hide not thy face far from me; put not thy servant away in anger: thou hast been my help; leave me not, neither forsake me, O God of my salvation.
verses.bsb: Hide not Your face from me, nor turn away Your servant in anger. You have been my helper; do not leave me or forsake me, O God of my salvation.

verses.kjv: When my father and my mother forsake me, then the Lord will take me up.
verses.bsb: Though my father and mother forsake me, the LORD will receive me.

verses.kjv: Teach me thy way, O Lord, and lead me in a plain path, because of mine enemies.
verses.bsb: Teach me Your way, O LORD, and lead me on a level path, because of my oppressors.

verses.kjv: Deliver me not over unto the will of mine enemies: for false witnesses are risen up against me, and such as breathe out cruelty.
verses.bsb: Do not hand me over to the will of my foes, for false witnesses rise up against me, breathing out violence.

verses.kjv: I had fainted, unless I had believed to see the goodness of the Lord in the land of the living.
verses.bsb: Still I am certain to see the goodness of the LORD in the land of the living.

verses.kjv: Wait on the Lord: be of good courage, and he shall strengthen thine heart: wait, I say, on the Lord.
verses.bsb: Wait patiently for the LORD; be strong and courageous.   Wait patiently for the LORD!

verses.kjv: UNTO thee will I cry, O Lord my rock; be not silent to me: lest, if thou be silent to me, I become like them that go down into the pit.
verses.bsb: To You, O LORD, I call; be not deaf to me, O my Rock. For if You remain silent, I will be like those descending to the Pit.

verses.kjv: Hear the voice of my supplications, when I cry unto thee, when I lift up my hands toward thy holy oracle.
verses.bsb: Hear my cry for mercy when I call to You for help, when I lift up my hands toward Your holy sanctuary.

verses.kjv: Draw me not away with the wicked, and with the workers of iniquity, which speak peace to their neighbours, but mischief is in their hearts.
verses.bsb: Do not drag me away with the wicked, and with the workers of iniquity, who speak peace to their neighbors while malice is in their hearts.

verses.kjv: Give them according to their deeds, and according to the wickedness of their endeavours: give them after the work of their hands; render to them their desert.
verses.bsb: Repay them according to their deeds and for their works of evil. Repay them for what their hands have done; bring back on them what they deserve.

verses.kjv: Because they regard not the works of the Lord, nor the operation of his hands, he shall destroy them, and not build them up.
verses.bsb: Since they show no regard for the works of the LORD or what His hands have done, He will tear them down and never rebuild them.

verses.kjv: Blessed be the Lord, because he hath heard the voice of my supplications.
verses.bsb: Blessed be the LORD, for He has heard my cry for mercy.

verses.kjv: The Lord is my strength and my shield; my heart trusted in him, and I am helped: therefore my heart greatly rejoiceth; and with my song will I praise him.
verses.bsb: The LORD is my strength and my shield; my heart trusts in Him, and I am helped. Therefore my heart rejoices, and I give thanks to Him with my song.

verses.kjv: The Lord is their strength, and he is the saving strength of his anointed.
verses.bsb: The LORD is the strength of His people, a stronghold of salvation for His anointed.

verses.kjv: Save thy people, and bless thine inheritance: feed them also, and lift them up for ever.
verses.bsb: Save Your people and bless Your inheritance; shepherd them and carry them forever.

verses.kjv: GIVE unto the Lord, O ye mighty, give unto the Lord glory and strength.
verses.bsb: Ascribe to the LORD, O heavenly beings, ascribe to the LORD glory and strength.

verses.kjv: Give unto the Lord the glory due unto his name; worship the Lord in the beauty of holiness.
verses.bsb: Ascribe to the LORD the glory due His name; worship the LORD in the splendor of His holiness.

verses.kjv: The voice of the Lord is upon the waters: the God of glory thundereth: the Lord is upon many waters.
verses.bsb: The voice of the LORD is over the waters; the God of glory thunders; the LORD is heard over many waters.

verses.kjv: The voice of the Lord is powerful; the voice of the Lord is full of majesty.
verses.bsb: The voice of the LORD is powerful; the voice of the LORD is majestic.

verses.kjv: The voice of the Lord breaketh the cedars; yea, the Lord breaketh the cedars of Lebanon.
verses.bsb: The voice of the LORD breaks the cedars; the LORD shatters the cedars of Lebanon.

verses.kjv: He maketh them also to skip like a calf; Lebanon and Sirion like a young unicorn.
verses.bsb: He makes Lebanon skip like a calf, and Sirion like a young wild ox.

verses.kjv: The voice of the Lord divideth the flames of fire.
verses.bsb: The voice of the LORD strikes with flames of fire.

verses.kjv: The voice of the Lord shaketh the wilderness; the Lord shaketh the wilderness of Kadesh.
verses.bsb: The voice of the LORD shakes the wilderness; the LORD shakes the Wilderness of Kadesh.

verses.kjv: The voice of the Lord maketh the hinds to calve, and discovereth the forests: and in his temple doth every one speak of his glory.
verses.bsb: The voice of the LORD twists the oaks and strips the forests bare. And in His temple all cry, “Glory!”

verses.kjv: The Lord sitteth upon the flood; yea, the Lord sitteth King for ever.
verses.bsb: The LORD sits enthroned over the flood; the LORD is enthroned as King forever.

verses.kjv: The Lord will give strength unto his people; the Lord will bless his people with peace.
verses.bsb: The LORD gives His people strength; the LORD blesses His people with peace.
