verses.kjv: I WILL extol thee, O Lord; for thou hast lifted me up, and hast not made my foes to rejoice over me.
verses.bsb: I will exalt You, O LORD, for You have lifted me up and have not allowed my foes to rejoice over me.

verses.kjv: O Lord my God, I cried unto thee, and thou hast healed me.
verses.bsb: O LORD my God, I cried to You for help, and You healed me.

verses.kjv: O Lord, thou hast brought up my soul from the grave: thou hast kept me alive, that I should not go down to the pit.
verses.bsb: O LORD, You pulled me up from Sheol; You spared me from descending into the Pit.

verses.kjv: Sing unto the Lord, O ye saints of his, and give thanks at the remembrance of his holiness.
verses.bsb: Sing to the LORD, O you His saints, and praise His holy name.

verses.kjv: For his anger endureth but a moment; in his favour is life: weeping may endure for a night, but joy cometh in the morning.
verses.bsb: For His anger is fleeting, but His favor lasts a lifetime. Weeping may stay the night, but joy comes in the morning.

verses.kjv: And in my prosperity I said, I shall never be moved.
verses.bsb: In prosperity I said, “I will never be shaken.”

verses.kjv: Lord, by thy favour thou hast made my mountain to stand strong: thou didst hide thy face, and I was troubled.
verses.bsb: O LORD, You favored me; You made my mountain stand strong.   When You hid Your face, I was dismayed.

verses.kjv: I cried to thee, O Lord; and unto the Lord I made supplication.
verses.bsb: To You, O LORD, I called, and I begged my Lord for mercy:

verses.kjv: What profit is there in my blood, when I go down to the pit? Shall the dust praise thee? shall it declare thy truth?
verses.bsb: “What gain is there in my bloodshed, in my descent to the Pit? Will the dust praise You? Will it proclaim Your faithfulness?

verses.kjv: Hear, O Lord, and have mercy upon me: Lord, be thou my helper.
verses.bsb: Hear me, O LORD, and have mercy; O LORD, be my helper.”

verses.kjv: Thou hast turned for me my mourning into dancing: thou hast put off my sackcloth, and girded me with gladness;
verses.bsb: You turned my mourning into dancing; You peeled off my sackcloth and clothed me with joy,

verses.kjv: To the end that my glory may sing praise to thee, and not be silent. O Lord my God, I will give thanks unto thee for ever.
verses.bsb: that my heart may sing Your praises and not be silent. O LORD my God, I will give thanks forever.

verses.kjv: IN thee, O Lord, do I put my trust; let me never be ashamed: deliver me in thy righteousness.
verses.bsb: In You, O LORD, I have taken refuge; let me never be put to shame; save me by Your righteousness.

verses.kjv: Bow down thine ear to me; deliver me speedily: be thou my strong rock, for an house of defence to save me.
verses.bsb: Incline Your ear to me; come quickly to my rescue. Be my rock of refuge, the stronghold of my deliverance.

verses.kjv: For thou art my rock and my fortress; therefore for thy name’s sake lead me, and guide me.
verses.bsb: For You are my rock and my fortress; lead me and guide me for the sake of Your name.

verses.kjv: Pull me out of the net that they have laid privily for me: for thou art my strength.
verses.bsb: You free me from the net laid out for me, for You are my refuge.

verses.kjv: Into thine hand I commit my spirit: thou hast redeemed me, O Lord God of truth.
verses.bsb: Into Your hands I commit my spirit; You have redeemed me, O LORD, God of truth.

verses.kjv: I have hated them that regard lying vanities: but I trust in the Lord.
verses.bsb: I hate those who cling to worthless idols, but in the LORD I trust.

verses.kjv: I will be glad and rejoice in thy mercy: for thou hast considered my trouble; thou hast known my soul in adversities;
verses.bsb: I will be glad and rejoice in Your loving devotion, for You have seen my affliction; You have known the anguish of my soul.

verses.kjv: And hast not shut me up into the hand of the enemy: thou hast set my feet in a large room.
verses.bsb: You have not delivered me to the enemy; You have set my feet in the open.

verses.kjv: Have mercy upon me, O Lord, for I am in trouble: mine eye is consumed with grief, yea, my soul and my belly.
verses.bsb: Be merciful to me, O LORD, for I am in distress; my eyes fail from sorrow, my soul and body as well.

verses.kjv: For my life is spent with grief, and my years with sighing: my strength faileth because of mine iniquity, and my bones are consumed.
verses.bsb: For my life is consumed with grief and my years with groaning; my iniquity has drained my strength, and my bones are wasting away.

verses.kjv: I was a reproach among all mine enemies, but especially among my neighbours, and a fear to mine acquaintance: they that did see me without fled from me.
verses.bsb: Among all my enemies I am a disgrace, and among my neighbors even more. I am dreaded by my friends— they flee when they see me on the street.

verses.kjv: I am forgotten as a dead man out of mind: I am like a broken vessel.
verses.bsb: I am forgotten like a dead man, out of mind. I am like a broken vessel.

verses.kjv: For I have heard the slander of many: fear was on every side: while they took counsel together against me, they devised to take away my life.
verses.bsb: For I hear the slander of many; there is terror on every side. They conspire against me and plot to take my life.

verses.kjv: But I trusted in thee, O Lord: I said, Thou art my God.
verses.bsb: But I trust in You, O LORD; I say, “You are my God.”

verses.kjv: My times are in thy hand: deliver me from the hand of mine enemies, and from them that persecute me.
verses.bsb: My times are in Your hands; deliver me from my enemies and from those who pursue me.

verses.kjv: Make thy face to shine upon thy servant: save me for thy mercies’ sake.
verses.bsb: Make Your face shine on Your servant; save me by Your loving devotion.

verses.kjv: Let me not be ashamed, O Lord; for I have called upon thee: let the wicked be ashamed, and let them be silent in the grave.
verses.bsb: O LORD, let me not be ashamed, for I have called on You. Let the wicked be put to shame; let them lie silent in Sheol.

verses.kjv: Let the lying lips be put to silence; which speak grievous things proudly and contemptuously against the righteous.
verses.bsb: May lying lips be silenced— lips that speak with arrogance against the righteous, full of pride and contempt.

verses.kjv: Oh how great is thy goodness, which thou hast laid up for them that fear thee; which thou hast wrought for them that trust in thee before the sons of men!
verses.bsb: How great is Your goodness which You have laid up for those who fear You, which You have bestowed before the sons of men on those who take refuge in You!

verses.kjv: Thou shalt hide them in the secret of thy presence from the pride of man: thou shalt keep them secretly in a pavilion from the strife of tongues.
verses.bsb: You hide them in the secret place of Your presence from the schemes of men. You conceal them in Your shelter from accusing tongues.

verses.kjv: Blessed be the Lord: for he hath shewed me his marvellous kindness in a strong city.
verses.bsb: Blessed be the LORD, for He has shown me His loving devotion in a city under siege.

verses.kjv: For I said in my haste, I am cut off from before thine eyes: nevertheless thou heardest the voice of my supplications when I cried unto thee.
verses.bsb: In my alarm I said, “I am cut off from Your sight!” But You heard my plea for mercy when I called to You for help.

verses.kjv: O love the Lord, all ye his saints: for the Lord preserveth the faithful, and plentifully rewardeth the proud doer.
verses.bsb: Love the LORD, all His saints. The LORD preserves the faithful, but fully repays the arrogant.

verses.kjv: Be of good courage, and he shall strengthen your heart, all ye that hope in the Lord.
verses.bsb: Be strong and courageous, all you who hope in the LORD.

verses.kjv: BLESSED is he whose transgression is forgiven, whose sin is covered.
verses.bsb: Blessed is he whose transgressions are forgiven, whose sins are covered.

verses.kjv: Blessed is the man unto whom the Lord imputeth not iniquity, and in whose spirit there is no guile.
verses.bsb: Blessed is the man whose iniquity the LORD does not count against him, in whose spirit there is no deceit.

verses.kjv: When I kept silence, my bones waxed old through my roaring all the day long.
verses.bsb: When I kept silent, my bones became brittle from my groaning all day long.

verses.kjv: For day and night thy hand was heavy upon me: my moisture is turned into the drought of summer. Selah.
verses.bsb: For day and night Your hand was heavy upon me; my strength was drained as in the summer heat. Selah

verses.kjv: I acknowledged my sin unto thee, and mine iniquity have I not hid. I said, I will confess my transgressions unto the Lord; and thou forgavest the iniquity of my sin. Selah.
verses.bsb: Then I acknowledged my sin to You and did not hide my iniquity. I said, “I will confess my transgressions to the LORD,” and You forgave the guilt of my sin. Selah

verses.kjv: For this shall every one that is godly pray unto thee in a time when thou mayest be found: surely in the floods of great waters they shall not come nigh unto him.
verses.bsb: Therefore let all the godly pray to You while You may be found. Surely when great waters rise, they will not come near.

verses.kjv: Thou art my hiding place; thou shalt preserve me from trouble; thou shalt compass me about with songs of deliverance. Selah.
verses.bsb: You are my hiding place. You protect me from trouble; You surround me with songs of deliverance. Selah

verses.kjv: I will instruct thee and teach thee in the way which thou shalt go: I will guide thee with mine eye.
verses.bsb: I will instruct you and teach you the way you should go; I will give you counsel and watch over you.

verses.kjv: Be ye not as the horse, or as the mule, which have no understanding: whose mouth must be held in with bit and bridle, lest they come near unto thee.
verses.bsb: Do not be like the horse or mule, which have no understanding; they must be controlled with bit and bridle to make them come to you.

verses.kjv: Many sorrows shall be to the wicked: but he that trusteth in the Lord, mercy shall compass him about.
verses.bsb: Many are the sorrows of the wicked, but loving devotion surrounds him who trusts in the LORD.

verses.kjv: Be glad in the Lord, and rejoice, ye righteous: and shout for joy, all ye that are upright in heart.
verses.bsb: Be glad in the LORD and rejoice, O righteous ones; shout for joy, all you upright in heart.

verses.kjv: REJOICE in the Lord, O ye righteous: for praise is comely for the upright.
verses.bsb: Rejoice in the LORD, O righteous ones; it is fitting for the upright to praise Him.

verses.kjv: Praise the Lord with harp: sing unto him with the psaltery and an instrument of ten strings.
verses.bsb: Praise the LORD with the harp; make music to Him with ten strings.

verses.kjv: Sing unto him a new song; play skilfully with a loud noise.
verses.bsb: Sing to Him a new song; play skillfully with a shout of joy.

verses.kjv: For the word of the Lord is right; and all his works are done in truth.
verses.bsb: For the word of the LORD is upright, and all His work is trustworthy.

verses.kjv: He loveth righteousness and judgment: the earth is full of the goodness of the Lord.
verses.bsb: The LORD loves righteousness and justice; the earth is full of His loving devotion.

verses.kjv: By the word of the Lord were the heavens made; and all the host of them by the breath of his mouth.
verses.bsb: By the word of the LORD the heavens were made, and all the stars by the breath of His mouth.

verses.kjv: He gathereth the waters of the sea together as an heap: he layeth up the depth in storehouses.
verses.bsb: He piles up the waters of the sea; He puts the depths into storehouses.

verses.kjv: Let all the earth fear the Lord: let all the inhabitants of the world stand in awe of him.
verses.bsb: Let all the earth fear the LORD; let all the people of the world revere Him.

verses.kjv: For he spake, and it was done; he commanded, and it stood fast.
verses.bsb: For He spoke, and it came to be; He commanded, and it stood firm.

verses.kjv: The Lord bringeth the counsel of the heathen to nought: he maketh the devices of the people of none effect.
verses.bsb: The LORD frustrates the plans of the nations; He thwarts the devices of the peoples.

verses.kjv: The counsel of the Lord standeth for ever, the thoughts of his heart to all generations.
verses.bsb: The counsel of the LORD stands forever, the purposes of His heart to all generations.

verses.kjv: Blessed is the nation whose God is the Lord; and the people whom he hath chosen for his own inheritance.
verses.bsb: Blessed is the nation whose God is the LORD, the people He has chosen as His inheritance!

verses.kjv: The Lord looketh from heaven; he beholdeth all the sons of men.
verses.bsb: The LORD looks down from heaven; He sees all the sons of men.

verses.kjv: From the place of his habitation he looketh upon all the inhabitants of the earth.
verses.bsb: From His dwelling place He gazes on all who inhabit the earth.

verses.kjv: He fashioneth their hearts alike; he considereth all their works.
verses.bsb: He shapes the hearts of each; He considers all their works.

verses.kjv: There is no king saved by the multitude of an host: a mighty man is not delivered by much strength.
verses.bsb: No king is saved by his vast army; no warrior is delivered by his great strength.

verses.kjv: An horse is a vain thing for safety: neither shall he deliver any by his great strength.
verses.bsb: A horse is a vain hope for salvation; even its great strength cannot save.

verses.kjv: Behold, the eye of the Lord is upon them that fear him, upon them that hope in his mercy;
verses.bsb: Surely the eyes of the LORD are on those who fear Him, on those whose hope is in His loving devotion

verses.kjv: To deliver their soul from death, and to keep them alive in famine.
verses.bsb: to deliver them from death and keep them alive in famine.

verses.kjv: Our soul waiteth for the Lord: he is our help and our shield.
verses.bsb: Our soul waits for the LORD; He is our help and our shield.

verses.kjv: For our heart shall rejoice in him, because we have trusted in his holy name.
verses.bsb: For our hearts rejoice in Him, since we trust in His holy name.

verses.kjv: Let thy mercy, O Lord, be upon us, according as we hope in thee.
verses.bsb: May Your loving devotion rest on us, O LORD, as we put our hope in You.

verses.kjv: I WILL bless the Lord at all times: his praise shall continually be in my mouth.
verses.bsb: I will bless the LORD at all times; His praise will always be on my lips.

verses.kjv: My soul shall make her boast in the Lord: the humble shall hear thereof, and be glad.
verses.bsb: My soul boasts in the LORD; let the oppressed hear and rejoice.

verses.kjv: O magnify the Lord with me, and let us exalt his name together.
verses.bsb: Magnify the LORD with me; let us exalt His name together.

verses.kjv: I sought the Lord, and he heard me, and delivered me from all my fears.
verses.bsb: I sought the LORD, and He answered me; He delivered me from all my fears.

verses.kjv: They looked unto him, and were lightened: and their faces were not ashamed.
verses.bsb: Those who look to Him are radiant with joy; their faces shall never be ashamed.

verses.kjv: This poor man cried, and the Lord heard him, and saved him out of all his troubles.
verses.bsb: This poor man called out, and the LORD heard him; He saved him from all his troubles.

verses.kjv: The angel of the Lord encampeth round about them that fear him, and delivereth them.
verses.bsb: The angel of the LORD encamps around those who fear Him, and he delivers them.

verses.kjv: O taste and see that the Lord is good: blessed is the man that trusteth in him.
verses.bsb: Taste and see that the LORD is good; blessed is the man who takes refuge in Him!

verses.kjv: O fear the Lord, ye his saints: for there is no want to them that fear him.
verses.bsb: Fear the LORD, you His saints, for those who fear Him lack nothing.

verses.kjv: The young lions do lack, and suffer hunger: but they that seek the Lord shall not want any good thing.
verses.bsb: Young lions go lacking and hungry, but those who seek the LORD lack no good thing.

verses.kjv: Come, ye children, hearken unto me: I will teach you the fear of the Lord.
verses.bsb: Come, children, listen to me; I will teach you the fear of the LORD.

verses.kjv: What man is he that desireth life, and loveth many days, that he may see good?
verses.bsb: Who is the man who delights in life, who desires to see good days?

verses.kjv: Keep thy tongue from evil, and thy lips from speaking guile.
verses.bsb: Keep your tongue from evil and your lips from deceitful speech.

verses.kjv: Depart from evil, and do good; seek peace, and pursue it.
verses.bsb: Turn away from evil and do good; seek peace and pursue it.

verses.kjv: The eyes of the Lord are upon the righteous, and his ears are open unto their cry.
verses.bsb: The eyes of the LORD are on the righteous, and His ears are inclined to their cry.

verses.kjv: The face of the Lord is against them that do evil, to cut off the remembrance of them from the earth.
verses.bsb: But the face of the LORD is against those who do evil, to wipe out all memory of them from the earth.

verses.kjv: The righteous cry, and the Lord heareth, and delivereth them out of all their troubles.
verses.bsb: The righteous cry out, and the LORD hears; He delivers them from all their troubles.

verses.kjv: The Lord is nigh unto them that are of a broken heart; and saveth such as be of a contrite spirit.
verses.bsb: The LORD is near to the brokenhearted; He saves the contrite in spirit.

verses.kjv: Many are the afflictions of the righteous: but the Lord delivereth him out of them all.
verses.bsb: Many are the afflictions of the righteous, but the LORD delivers him from them all.

verses.kjv: He keepeth all his bones: not one of them is broken.
verses.bsb: He protects all his bones; not one of them will be broken.

verses.kjv: Evil shall slay the wicked: and they that hate the righteous shall be desolate.
verses.bsb: Evil will slay the wicked, and the haters of the righteous will be condemned.

verses.kjv: The Lord redeemeth the soul of his servants: and none of them that trust in him shall be desolate.
verses.bsb: The LORD redeems His servants, and none who take refuge in Him will be condemned.

verses.kjv: PLEAD my cause, O Lord, with them that strive with me: fight against them that fight against me.
verses.bsb: Contend with my opponents, O LORD; fight against those who fight against me.

verses.kjv: Take hold of shield and buckler, and stand up for mine help.
verses.bsb: Take up Your shield and buckler; arise and come to my aid.

verses.kjv: Draw out also the spear, and stop the way against them that persecute me: say unto my soul, I am thy salvation.
verses.bsb: Draw the spear and javelin against my pursuers; say to my soul: “I am your salvation.”

verses.kjv: Let them be confounded and put to shame that seek after my soul: let them be turned back and brought to confusion that devise my hurt.
verses.bsb: May those who seek my life be disgraced and put to shame; may those who plan to harm me be driven back and confounded.

verses.kjv: Let them be as chaff before the wind: and let the angel of the Lord chase them.
verses.bsb: May they be like chaff in the wind, as the angel of the LORD drives them away.

verses.kjv: Let their way be dark and slippery: and let the angel of the Lord persecute them.
verses.bsb: May their path be dark and slick, as the angel of the LORD pursues.

verses.kjv: For without cause have they hid for me their net in a pit, which without cause they have digged for my soul.
verses.bsb: For without cause they laid their net for me; without reason they dug a pit for my soul.

verses.kjv: Let destruction come upon him at unawares; and let his net that he hath hid catch himself: into that very destruction let him fall.
verses.bsb: May ruin befall them by surprise; may the net they hid ensnare them; may they fall into the hazard they created.

verses.kjv: And my soul shall be joyful in the Lord: it shall rejoice in his salvation.
verses.bsb: Then my soul will rejoice in the LORD and exult in His salvation.

verses.kjv: All my bones shall say, Lord, who is like unto thee, which deliverest the poor from him that is too strong for him, yea, the poor and the needy from him that spoileth him?
verses.bsb: All my bones will exclaim, “Who is like You, O LORD, who delivers the afflicted from the aggressor, the poor and needy from the robber?”

verses.kjv: False witnesses did rise up; they laid to my charge things that I knew not.
verses.bsb: Hostile witnesses come forward; they make charges I know nothing about.

verses.kjv: They rewarded me evil for good to the spoiling of my soul.
verses.bsb: They repay me evil for good, to the bereavement of my soul.

verses.kjv: But as for me, when they were sick, my clothing was sackcloth: I humbled my soul with fasting; and my prayer returned into mine own bosom.
verses.bsb: Yet when they were ill, I put on sackcloth; I humbled myself with fasting, but my prayers returned unanswered.

verses.kjv: I behaved myself as though he had been my friend or brother: I bowed down heavily, as one that mourneth for his mother.
verses.bsb: I paced about as for my friend or brother; I was bowed down with grief, like one mourning for his mother.

verses.kjv: But in mine adversity they rejoiced, and gathered themselves together: yea, the abjects gathered themselves together against me, and I knew it not; they did tear me, and ceased not:
verses.bsb: But when I stumbled, they assembled in glee; they gathered together against me. Assailants I did not know slandered me without ceasing.

verses.kjv: With hypocritical mockers in feasts, they gnashed upon me with their teeth.
verses.bsb: Like godless jesters at a feast, they gnashed their teeth at me.

verses.kjv: Lord, how long wilt thou look on? rescue my soul from their destructions, my darling from the lions.
verses.bsb: How long, O Lord, will You look on? Rescue my soul from their ravages, my precious life from these lions.

verses.kjv: I will give thee thanks in the great congregation: I will praise thee among much people.
verses.bsb: Then I will give You thanks in the great assembly; I will praise You among many people.

verses.kjv: Let not them that are mine enemies wrongfully rejoice over me: neither let them wink with the eye that hate me without a cause.
verses.bsb: Let not my enemies gloat over me without cause, nor those who hate me without reason wink in malice.

verses.kjv: For they speak not peace: but they devise deceitful matters against them that are quiet in the land.
verses.bsb: For they do not speak peace, but they devise deceitful schemes against those who live quietly in the land.

verses.kjv: Yea, they opened their mouth wide against me, and said, Aha, aha, our eye hath seen it.
verses.bsb: They gape at me and say, “Aha, aha! Our eyes have seen!”

verses.kjv: This thou hast seen, O Lord: keep not silence: O Lord, be not far from me.
verses.bsb: O LORD, You have seen it; be not silent. O Lord, be not far from me.

verses.kjv: Stir up thyself, and awake to my judgment, even unto my cause, my God and my Lord.
verses.bsb: Awake and rise to my defense, to my cause, my God and my Lord!

verses.kjv: Judge me, O Lord my God, according to thy righteousness; and let them not rejoice over me.
verses.bsb: Vindicate me by Your righteousness, O LORD my God, and do not let them gloat over me.

verses.kjv: Let them not say in their hearts, Ah, so would we have it: let them not say, We have swallowed him up.
verses.bsb: Let them not say in their hearts, “Aha, just what we wanted!” Let them not say, “We have swallowed him up!”

verses.kjv: Let them be ashamed and brought to confusion together that rejoice at mine hurt: let them be clothed with shame and dishonour that magnify themselves against me.
verses.bsb: May those who gloat in my distress be ashamed and confounded; may those who exalt themselves over me be clothed in shame and reproach.

verses.kjv: Let them shout for joy, and be glad, that favour my righteous cause: yea, let them say continually, Let the Lord be magnified, which hath pleasure in the prosperity of his servant.
verses.bsb: May those who favor my vindication shout for joy and gladness; may they always say, “Exalted be the LORD who delights in His servant’s well-being.”

verses.kjv: And my tongue shall speak of thy righteousness and of thy praise all the day long.
verses.bsb: Then my tongue will proclaim Your righteousness and Your praises all day long.

verses.kjv: THE transgression of the wicked saith within my heart, that there is no fear of God before his eyes.
verses.bsb: An oracle is in my heart regarding the transgression of the wicked man: There is no fear of God before his eyes.

verses.kjv: For he flattereth himself in his own eyes, until his iniquity be found to be hateful.
verses.bsb: For his eyes are too full of conceit to detect or hate his own sin.

verses.kjv: The words of his mouth are iniquity and deceit: he hath left off to be wise, and to do good.
verses.bsb: The words of his mouth are wicked and deceitful; he has ceased to be wise and well-doing.

verses.kjv: He deviseth mischief upon his bed; he setteth himself in a way that is not good; he abhorreth not evil.
verses.bsb: Even on his bed he plots wickedness; he sets himself on a path that is not good; he fails to reject evil.

verses.kjv: Thy mercy, O Lord, is in the heavens; and thy faithfulness reacheth unto the clouds.
verses.bsb: Your loving devotion, O LORD, reaches to the heavens, Your faithfulness to the clouds.

verses.kjv: Thy righteousness is like the great mountains; thy judgments are a great deep: O Lord, thou preservest man and beast.
verses.bsb: Your righteousness is like the highest mountains; Your judgments are like the deepest sea. O LORD, You preserve man and beast.

verses.kjv: How excellent is thy lovingkindness, O God! therefore the children of men put their trust under the shadow of thy wings.
verses.bsb: How precious is Your loving devotion, O God, that the children of men take refuge in the shadow of Your wings!

verses.kjv: They shall be abundantly satisfied with the fatness of thy house; and thou shalt make them drink of the river of thy pleasures.
verses.bsb: They feast on the abundance of Your house, and You give them drink from Your river of delights.

verses.kjv: For with thee is the fountain of life: in thy light shall we see light.
verses.bsb: For with You is the fountain of life; in Your light we see light.

verses.kjv: O continue thy lovingkindness unto them that know thee; and thy righteousness to the upright in heart.
verses.bsb: Extend Your loving devotion to those who know You, and Your righteousness to the upright in heart.

verses.kjv: Let not the foot of pride come against me, and let not the hand of the wicked remove me.
verses.bsb: Let not the foot of the proud come against me, nor the hand of the wicked drive me away.

verses.kjv: There are the workers of iniquity fallen: they are cast down, and shall not be able to rise.
verses.bsb: There the evildoers lie fallen, thrown down and unable to rise.

verses.kjv: FRET not thyself because of evildoers, neither be thou envious against the workers of iniquity.
verses.bsb: Do not fret over those who do evil; do not envy those who do wrong.

verses.kjv: For they shall soon be cut down like the grass, and wither as the green herb.
verses.bsb: For they wither quickly like grass and wilt like tender plants.

verses.kjv: Trust in the Lord, and do good; so shalt thou dwell in the land, and verily thou shalt be fed.
verses.bsb: Trust in the LORD and do good; dwell in the land and cultivate faithfulness.

verses.kjv: Delight thyself also in the Lord; and he shall give thee the desires of thine heart.
verses.bsb: Delight yourself in the LORD, and He will give you the desires of your heart.

verses.kjv: Commit thy way unto the Lord; trust also in him; and he shall bring it to pass.
verses.bsb: Commit your way to the LORD; trust in Him, and He will do it.

verses.kjv: And he shall bring forth thy righteousness as the light, and thy judgment as the noonday.
verses.bsb: He will bring forth your righteousness like the dawn, your justice like the noonday sun.

verses.kjv: Rest in the Lord, and wait patiently for him: fret not thyself because of him who prospereth in his way, because of the man who bringeth wicked devices to pass.
verses.bsb: Be still before the LORD and wait patiently for Him; do not fret when men prosper in their ways, when they carry out wicked schemes.

verses.kjv: Cease from anger, and forsake wrath: fret not thyself in any wise to do evil.
verses.bsb: Refrain from anger and abandon wrath; do not fret—it can only bring harm.

verses.kjv: For evildoers shall be cut off: but those that wait upon the Lord, they shall inherit the earth.
verses.bsb: For the evildoers will be cut off, but those who hope in the LORD will inherit the land.

verses.kjv: For yet a little while, and the wicked shall not be: yea, thou shalt diligently consider his place, and it shall not be.
verses.bsb: Yet a little while, and the wicked will be no more; though you look for them, they will not be found.

verses.kjv: But the meek shall inherit the earth; and shall delight themselves in the abundance of peace.
verses.bsb: But the meek will inherit the land and delight in abundant prosperity.

verses.kjv: The wicked plotteth against the just, and gnasheth upon him with his teeth.
verses.bsb: The wicked scheme against the righteous and gnash their teeth at them,

verses.kjv: The Lord shall laugh at him: for he seeth that his day is coming.
verses.bsb: but the Lord laughs, seeing that their day is coming.

verses.kjv: The wicked have drawn out the sword, and have bent their bow, to cast down the poor and needy, and to slay such as be of upright conversation.
verses.bsb: The wicked have drawn the sword and bent the bow to bring down the poor and needy, to slay those whose ways are upright.

verses.kjv: Their sword shall enter into their own heart, and their bows shall be broken.
verses.bsb: But their swords will pierce their own hearts, and their bows will be broken.

verses.kjv: A little that a righteous man hath is better than the riches of many wicked.
verses.bsb: Better is the little of the righteous than the abundance of many who are wicked.

verses.kjv: For the arms of the wicked shall be broken: but the Lord upholdeth the righteous.
verses.bsb: For the arms of the wicked will be broken, but the LORD upholds the righteous.

verses.kjv: The Lord knoweth the days of the upright: and their inheritance shall be for ever.
verses.bsb: The LORD knows the days of the blameless, and their inheritance will last forever.

verses.kjv: They shall not be ashamed in the evil time: and in the days of famine they shall be satisfied.
verses.bsb: In the time of evil they will not be ashamed, and in the days of famine they will be satisfied.

verses.kjv: But the wicked shall perish, and the enemies of the Lord shall be as the fat of lambs: they shall consume; into smoke shall they consume away.
verses.bsb: But the wicked and enemies of the LORD will perish like the glory of the fields. They will vanish; like smoke they will fade away.

verses.kjv: The wicked borroweth, and payeth not again: but the righteous sheweth mercy, and giveth.
verses.bsb: The wicked borrow and do not repay, but the righteous are gracious and giving.

verses.kjv: For such as be blessed of him shall inherit the earth; and they that be cursed of him shall be cut off.
verses.bsb: Surely those He blesses will inherit the land, but the cursed will be destroyed.

verses.kjv: The steps of a good man are ordered by the Lord: and he delighteth in his way.
verses.bsb: The steps of a man are ordered by the LORD who takes delight in his journey.

verses.kjv: Though he fall, he shall not be utterly cast down: for the Lord upholdeth him with his hand.
verses.bsb: Though he falls, he will not be overwhelmed, for the LORD is holding his hand.

verses.kjv: I have been young, and now am old; yet have I not seen the righteous forsaken, nor his seed begging bread.
verses.bsb: I once was young and now am old, yet never have I seen the righteous abandoned or their children begging for bread.

verses.kjv: He is ever merciful, and lendeth; and his seed is blessed.
verses.bsb: They are ever generous and quick to lend, and their children are a blessing.

verses.kjv: Depart from evil, and do good; and dwell for evermore.
verses.bsb: Turn away from evil and do good, so that you will abide forever.

verses.kjv: For the Lord loveth judgment, and forsaketh not his saints; they are preserved for ever: but the seed of the wicked shall be cut off.
verses.bsb: For the LORD loves justice and will not forsake His saints. They are preserved forever, but the offspring of the wicked will be cut off.

verses.kjv: The righteous shall inherit the land, and dwell therein for ever.
verses.bsb: The righteous will inherit the land and dwell in it forever.

verses.kjv: The mouth of the righteous speaketh wisdom, and his tongue talketh of judgment.
verses.bsb: The mouth of the righteous man utters wisdom, and his tongue speaks justice.

verses.kjv: The law of his God is in his heart; none of his steps shall slide.
verses.bsb: The law of his God is in his heart; his steps do not falter.

verses.kjv: The wicked watcheth the righteous, and seeketh to slay him.
verses.bsb: Though the wicked lie in wait for the righteous, and seek to slay them,

verses.kjv: The Lord will not leave him in his hand, nor condemn him when he is judged.
verses.bsb: the LORD will not leave them in their power or let them be condemned under judgment.

verses.kjv: Wait on the Lord, and keep his way, and he shall exalt thee to inherit the land: when the wicked are cut off, thou shalt see it.
verses.bsb: Wait for the LORD and keep His way, and He will raise you up to inherit the land. When the wicked are cut off, you will see it.

verses.kjv: I have seen the wicked in great power, and spreading himself like a green bay tree.
verses.bsb: I have seen a wicked, ruthless man flourishing like a well-rooted native tree,

verses.kjv: Yet he passed away, and, lo, he was not: yea, I sought him, but he could not be found.
verses.bsb: yet he passed away and was no more; though I searched, he could not be found.

verses.kjv: Mark the perfect man, and behold the upright: for the end of that man is peace.
verses.bsb: Consider the blameless and observe the upright, for posterity awaits the man of peace.

verses.kjv: But the transgressors shall be destroyed together: the end of the wicked shall be cut off.
verses.bsb: But the transgressors will all be destroyed; the future of the wicked will be cut off.

verses.kjv: But the salvation of the righteous is of the Lord: he is their strength in the time of trouble.
verses.bsb: The salvation of the righteous is from the LORD; He is their stronghold in time of trouble.

verses.kjv: And the Lord shall help them, and deliver them: he shall deliver them from the wicked, and save them, because they trust in him.
verses.bsb: The LORD helps and delivers them; He rescues and saves them from the wicked, because they take refuge in Him.

verses.kjv: O LORD, rebuke me not in thy wrath: neither chasten me in thy hot displeasure.
verses.bsb: O LORD, do not rebuke me in Your anger or discipline me in Your wrath.

verses.kjv: For thine arrows stick fast in me, and thy hand presseth me sore.
verses.bsb: For Your arrows have pierced me deeply, and Your hand has pressed down on me.

verses.kjv: There is no soundness in my flesh because of thine anger; neither is there any rest in my bones because of my sin.
verses.bsb: There is no soundness in my body because of Your anger; there is no rest in my bones because of my sin.

verses.kjv: For mine iniquities are gone over mine head: as an heavy burden they are too heavy for me.
verses.bsb: For my iniquities have overwhelmed me; they are a burden too heavy to bear.

verses.kjv: My wounds stink and are corrupt because of my foolishness.
verses.bsb: My wounds are foul and festering because of my sinful folly.

verses.kjv: I am troubled; I am bowed down greatly; I go mourning all the day long.
verses.bsb: I am bent and brought low; all day long I go about mourning.

verses.kjv: For my loins are filled with a loathsome disease: and there is no soundness in my flesh.
verses.bsb: For my loins are full of burning pain, and no soundness remains in my body.

verses.kjv: I am feeble and sore broken: I have roared by reason of the disquietness of my heart.
verses.bsb: I am numb and badly crushed; I groan in anguish of heart.

verses.kjv: Lord, all my desire is before thee; and my groaning is not hid from thee.
verses.bsb: O Lord, my every desire is before You; my groaning is not hidden from You.

verses.kjv: My heart panteth, my strength faileth me: as for the light of mine eyes, it also is gone from me.
verses.bsb: My heart pounds, my strength fails, and even the light of my eyes has faded.

verses.kjv: My lovers and my friends stand aloof from my sore; and my kinsmen stand afar off.
verses.bsb: My beloved and friends shun my disease, and my kinsmen stand at a distance.

verses.kjv: They also that seek after my life lay snares for me: and they that seek my hurt speak mischievous things, and imagine deceits all the day long.
verses.bsb: Those who seek my life lay snares; those who wish me harm speak destruction, plotting deceit all day long.

verses.kjv: But I, as a deaf man, heard not; and I was as a dumb man that openeth not his mouth.
verses.bsb: But like a deaf man, I do not hear; and like a mute man, I do not open my mouth.

verses.kjv: Thus I was as a man that heareth not, and in whose mouth are no reproofs.
verses.bsb: I am like a man who cannot hear, whose mouth offers no reply.

verses.kjv: For in thee, O Lord, do I hope: thou wilt hear, O Lord my God.
verses.bsb: I wait for You, O LORD; You will answer, O Lord my God.

verses.kjv: For I said, Hear me, lest otherwise they should rejoice over me: when my foot slippeth, they magnify themselves against me.
verses.bsb: For I said, “Let them not gloat over me— those who taunt me when my foot slips.”

verses.kjv: For I am ready to halt, and my sorrow is continually before me.
verses.bsb: For I am ready to fall, and my pain is ever with me.

verses.kjv: For I will declare mine iniquity; I will be sorry for my sin.
verses.bsb: Yes, I confess my iniquity; I am troubled by my sin.

verses.kjv: But mine enemies are lively, and they are strong: and they that hate me wrongfully are multiplied.
verses.bsb: Many are my enemies without cause, and many hate me without reason.

verses.kjv: They also that render evil for good are mine adversaries; because I follow the thing that good is.
verses.bsb: Those who repay my good with evil attack me for pursuing the good.

verses.kjv: Forsake me not, O Lord: O my God, be not far from me.
verses.bsb: Do not forsake me, O LORD; be not far from me, O my God.

verses.kjv: Make haste to help me, O Lord my salvation.
verses.bsb: Come quickly to help me, O Lord my Savior.

verses.kjv: I SAID, I will take heed to my ways, that I sin not with my tongue: I will keep my mouth with a bridle, while the wicked is before me.
verses.bsb: I said, “I will watch my ways so that I will not sin with my tongue; I will guard my mouth with a muzzle as long as the wicked are present.”

verses.kjv: I was dumb with silence, I held my peace, even from good; and my sorrow was stirred.
verses.bsb: I was speechless and still; I remained silent, even from speaking good, and my sorrow was stirred.

verses.kjv: My heart was hot within me, while I was musing the fire burned: then spake I with my tongue,
verses.bsb: My heart grew hot within me; as I mused, the fire burned.   Then I spoke with my tongue:

verses.kjv: Lord, make me to know mine end, and the measure of my days, what it is; that I may know how frail I am.
verses.bsb: “Show me, O LORD, my end and the measure of my days. Let me know how fleeting my life is.

verses.kjv: Behold, thou hast made my days as an handbreadth; and mine age is as nothing before thee: verily every man at his best state is altogether vanity. Selah.
verses.bsb: You, indeed, have made my days as handbreadths, and my lifetime as nothing before You. Truly each man at his best exists as but a breath. Selah

verses.kjv: Surely every man walketh in a vain shew: surely they are disquieted in vain: he heapeth up riches, and knoweth not who shall gather them.
verses.bsb: Surely every man goes about like a phantom; surely he bustles in vain; he heaps up riches not knowing who will haul them away.

verses.kjv: And now, Lord, what wait I for? my hope is in thee.
verses.bsb: And now, O Lord, for what do I wait? My hope is in You.

verses.kjv: Deliver me from all my transgressions: make me not the reproach of the foolish.
verses.bsb: Deliver me from all my transgressions; do not make me the reproach of fools.

verses.kjv: I was dumb, I opened not my mouth; because thou didst it.
verses.bsb: I have become mute; I do not open my mouth because of what You have done.

verses.kjv: Remove thy stroke away from me: I am consumed by the blow of thine hand.
verses.bsb: Remove Your scourge from me; I am perishing by the force of Your hand.

verses.kjv: When thou with rebukes dost correct man for iniquity, thou makest his beauty to consume away like a moth: surely every man is vanity. Selah.
verses.bsb: You discipline and correct a man for his iniquity, consuming like a moth what he holds dear; surely each man is but a vapor. Selah

verses.kjv: Hear my prayer, O Lord, and give ear unto my cry; hold not thy peace at my tears: for I am a stranger with thee, and a sojourner, as all my fathers were.
verses.bsb: Hear my prayer, O LORD, and give ear to my cry for help; do not be deaf to my weeping. For I am a foreigner dwelling with You, a stranger like all my fathers.

verses.kjv: O spare me, that I may recover strength, before I go hence, and be no more.
verses.bsb: Turn Your gaze away from me, that I may again be cheered before I depart and am no more.”

verses.kjv: I WAITED patiently for the Lord; and he inclined unto me, and heard my cry.
verses.bsb: I waited patiently for the LORD; He inclined to me and heard my cry.

verses.kjv: He brought me up also out of an horrible pit, out of the miry clay, and set my feet upon a rock, and established my goings.
verses.bsb: He lifted me up from the pit of despair, out of the miry clay; He set my feet upon a rock, and made my footsteps firm.

verses.kjv: And he hath put a new song in my mouth, even praise unto our God: many shall see it, and fear, and shall trust in the Lord.
verses.bsb: He put a new song in my mouth, a hymn of praise to our God. Many will see and fear and put their trust in the LORD.

verses.kjv: Blessed is that man that maketh the Lord his trust, and respecteth not the proud, nor such as turn aside to lies.
verses.bsb: Blessed is the man who has made the LORD his trust, who has not turned to the proud, nor to those who lapse into falsehood.

verses.kjv: Many, O Lord my God, are thy wonderful works which thou hast done, and thy thoughts which are to us-ward: they cannot be reckoned up in order unto thee: if I would declare and speak of them, they are more than can be numbered.
verses.bsb: Many, O LORD my God, are the wonders You have done, and the plans You have for us— none can compare to You— if I proclaim and declare them, they are more than I can count.

verses.kjv: Sacrifice and offering thou didst not desire; mine ears hast thou opened: burnt offering and sin offering hast thou not required.
verses.bsb: Sacrifice and offering You did not desire, but my ears You have opened. Burnt offerings and sin offerings You did not require.

verses.kjv: Then said I, Lo, I come: in the volume of the book it is written of me,
verses.bsb: Then I said, “Here I am, I have come— it is written about me in the scroll:

verses.kjv: I delight to do thy will, O my God: yea, thy law is within my heart.
verses.bsb: I delight to do Your will, O my God; Your law is within my heart.”

verses.kjv: I have preached righteousness in the great congregation: lo, I have not refrained my lips, O Lord, thou knowest.
verses.bsb: I proclaim righteousness in the great assembly; behold, I do not seal my lips, as You, O LORD, do know.

verses.kjv: I have not hid thy righteousness within my heart; I have declared thy faithfulness and thy salvation: I have not concealed thy lovingkindness and thy truth from the great congregation.
verses.bsb: I have not covered up Your righteousness in my heart; I have declared Your faithfulness and salvation; I have not concealed Your loving devotion and faithfulness from the great assembly.

verses.kjv: Withhold not thou thy tender mercies from me, O Lord: let thy lovingkindness and thy truth continually preserve me.
verses.bsb: O LORD, do not withhold Your mercy from me; Your loving devotion and faithfulness will always guard me.

verses.kjv: For innumerable evils have compassed me about: mine iniquities have taken hold upon me, so that I am not able to look up; they are more than the hairs of mine head: therefore my heart faileth me.
verses.bsb: For evils without number surround me; my sins have overtaken me, so that I cannot see. They are more than the hairs of my head, and my heart has failed within me.

verses.kjv: Be pleased, O Lord, to deliver me: O Lord, make haste to help me.
verses.bsb: Be pleased, O LORD, to deliver me; hurry, O LORD, to help me.

verses.kjv: Let them be ashamed and confounded together that seek after my soul to destroy it; let them be driven backward and put to shame that wish me evil.
verses.bsb: May those who seek my life be ashamed and confounded; may those who wish me harm be repelled and humiliated.

verses.kjv: Let them be desolate for a reward of their shame that say unto me, Aha, aha.
verses.bsb: May those who say to me, “Aha, aha!” be appalled at their own shame.

verses.kjv: Let all those that seek thee rejoice and be glad in thee: let such as love thy salvation say continually, The Lord be magnified.
verses.bsb: May all who seek You rejoice and be glad in You; may those who love Your salvation always say, “The LORD be magnified!”

verses.kjv: But I am poor and needy; yet the Lord thinketh upon me: thou art my help and my deliverer; make no tarrying, O my God.
verses.bsb: But I am poor and needy; may the Lord think of me. You are my helper and deliverer; O my God, do not delay.

verses.kjv: BLESSED is he that considereth the poor: the Lord will deliver him in time of trouble.
verses.bsb: Blessed is the one who cares for the poor; the LORD will deliver him in the day of trouble.

verses.kjv: The Lord will preserve him, and keep him alive; and he shall be blessed upon the earth: and thou wilt not deliver him unto the will of his enemies.
verses.bsb: The LORD will protect and preserve him; He will bless him in the land and refuse to surrender him to the will of his foes.

verses.kjv: The Lord will strengthen him upon the bed of languishing: thou wilt make all his bed in his sickness.
verses.bsb: The LORD will sustain him on his bed of illness and restore him from his bed of sickness.

verses.kjv: I said, Lord, be merciful unto me: heal my soul; for I have sinned against thee.
verses.bsb: I said, “O LORD, be gracious to me; heal me, for I have sinned against You.”

verses.kjv: Mine enemies speak evil of me, When shall he die, and his name perish?
verses.bsb: My enemies say with malice: “When will he die and be forgotten?”

verses.kjv: And if he come to see me, he speaketh vanity: his heart gathereth iniquity to itself; when he goeth abroad, he telleth it.
verses.bsb: My visitor speaks falsehood; he gathers slander in his heart; he goes out and spreads it abroad.

verses.kjv: All that hate me whisper together against me: against me do they devise my hurt.
verses.bsb: All who hate me whisper against me; they imagine the worst for me:

verses.kjv: An evil disease, say they, cleaveth fast unto him: and now that he lieth he shall rise up no more.
verses.bsb: “A vile disease has been poured into him; he will never get up from where he lies!”

verses.kjv: Yea, mine own familiar friend, in whom I trusted, which did eat of my bread, hath lifted up his heel against me.
verses.bsb: Even my close friend whom I trusted, the one who shared my bread, has lifted up his heel against me.

verses.kjv: But thou, O Lord, be merciful unto me, and raise me up, that I may requite them.
verses.bsb: But You, O LORD, be gracious to me and raise me up, that I may repay them.

verses.kjv: By this I know that thou favourest me, because mine enemy doth not triumph over me.
verses.bsb: By this I know that You delight in me, for my enemy does not triumph over me.

verses.kjv: And as for me, thou upholdest me in mine integrity, and settest me before thy face for ever.
verses.bsb: In my integrity You uphold me and set me in Your presence forever.

verses.kjv: Blessed be the Lord God of Israel from everlasting, and to everlasting. Amen, and Amen.
verses.bsb: Blessed be the LORD, the God of Israel, from everlasting to everlasting.   Amen and Amen.

verses.kjv: AS the hart panteth after the water brooks, so panteth my soul after thee, O God.
verses.bsb: As the deer pants for streams of water, so my soul longs after You, O God.

verses.kjv: My soul thirsteth for God, for the living God: when shall I come and appear before God?
verses.bsb: My soul thirsts for God, the living God. When shall I come and appear in God’s presence?

verses.kjv: My tears have been my meat day and night, while they continually say unto me, Where is thy God?
verses.bsb: My tears have been my food both day and night, while men ask me all day long, “Where is your God?”

verses.kjv: When I remember these things, I pour out my soul in me: for I had gone with the multitude, I went with them to the house of God, with the voice of joy and praise, with a multitude that kept holyday.
verses.bsb: These things come to mind as I pour out my soul: how I walked with the multitude, leading the procession to the house of God with shouts of joy and praise.

verses.kjv: Why art thou cast down, O my soul? and why art thou disquieted in me? hope thou in God: for I shall yet praise him for the help of his countenance.
verses.bsb: Why are you downcast, O my soul? Why the unease within me? Put your hope in God, for I will yet praise Him for the salvation of His presence.

verses.kjv: O my God, my soul is cast down within me: therefore will I remember thee from the land of Jordan, and of the Hermonites, from the hill Mizar.
verses.bsb: O my God, my soul despairs within me. Therefore I remember You from the land of Jordan and the peaks of Hermon— even from Mount Mizar.

verses.kjv: Deep calleth unto deep at the noise of thy waterspouts: all thy waves and thy billows are gone over me.
verses.bsb: Deep calls to deep in the roar of Your waterfalls; all Your breakers and waves have rolled over me.

verses.kjv: Yet the Lord will command his lovingkindness in the daytime, and in the night his song shall be with me, and my prayer unto the God of my life.
verses.bsb: The LORD decrees His loving devotion by day, and at night His song is with me as a prayer to the God of my life.

verses.kjv: I will say unto God my rock, Why hast thou forgotten me? why go I mourning because of the oppression of the enemy?
verses.bsb: I say to God my Rock, “Why have You forgotten me? Why must I walk in sorrow because of the enemy’s oppression?”

verses.kjv: As with a sword in my bones, mine enemies reproach me; while they say daily unto me, Where is thy God?
verses.bsb: Like the crushing of my bones, my enemies taunt me, while they say to me all day long, “Where is your God?”

verses.kjv: Why art thou cast down, O my soul? and why art thou disquieted within me? hope thou in God: for I shall yet praise him, who is the health of my countenance, and my God.
verses.bsb: Why are you downcast, O my soul? Why the unease within me? Put your hope in God, for I will yet praise Him, my Savior and my God.

verses.kjv: JUDGE me, O God, and plead my cause against an ungodly nation: O deliver me from the deceitful and unjust man.
verses.bsb: Vindicate me, O God, and plead my case against an ungodly nation; deliver me from deceitful and unjust men.

verses.kjv: For thou art the God of my strength: why dost thou cast me off? why go I mourning because of the oppression of the enemy?
verses.bsb: For You are the God of my refuge. Why have You rejected me? Why must I walk in sorrow because of the enemy’s oppression?

verses.kjv: O send out thy light and thy truth: let them lead me; let them bring me unto thy holy hill, and to thy tabernacles.
verses.bsb: Send out Your light and Your truth; let them lead me. Let them bring me to Your holy mountain, and to the place where You dwell.

verses.kjv: Then will I go unto the altar of God, unto God my exceeding joy: yea, upon the harp will I praise thee, O God my God.
verses.bsb: Then I will go to the altar of God, to God, my greatest joy. I will praise You with the harp, O God, my God.

verses.kjv: Why art thou cast down, O my soul? and why art thou disquieted within me? hope in God: for I shall yet praise him, who is the health of my countenance, and my God.
verses.bsb: Why are you downcast, O my soul? Why the unease within me? Put your hope in God, for I will yet praise Him, my Savior and my God.

verses.kjv: WE have heard with our ears, O God, our fathers have told us, what work thou didst in their days, in the times of old.
verses.bsb: We have heard with our ears, O God; our fathers have told us the work You did in their days, in the days of old.

verses.kjv: How thou didst drive out the heathen with thy hand, and plantedst them; how thou didst afflict the people, and cast them out.
verses.bsb: With Your hand You drove out the nations and planted our fathers there; You crushed the peoples and cast them out.

verses.kjv: For they got not the land in possession by their own sword, neither did their own arm save them: but thy right hand, and thine arm, and the light of thy countenance, because thou hadst a favour unto them.
verses.bsb: For it was not by their sword that they took the land; their arm did not bring them victory. It was by Your right hand, Your arm, and the light of Your face, because You favored them.

verses.kjv: Thou art my King, O God: command deliverances for Jacob.
verses.bsb: You are my King, O God, who ordains victories for Jacob.

verses.kjv: Through thee will we push down our enemies: through thy name will we tread them under that rise up against us.
verses.bsb: Through You we repel our foes; through Your name we trample our enemies.

verses.kjv: For I will not trust in my bow, neither shall my sword save me.
verses.bsb: For I do not trust in my bow, nor does my sword save me.

verses.kjv: But thou hast saved us from our enemies, and hast put them to shame that hated us.
verses.bsb: For You save us from our enemies; You put those who hate us to shame.

verses.kjv: In God we boast all the day long, and praise thy name for ever. Selah.
verses.bsb: In God we have boasted all day long, and Your name we will praise forever. Selah

verses.kjv: But thou hast cast off, and put us to shame; and goest not forth with our armies.
verses.bsb: But You have rejected and humbled us; You no longer go forth with our armies.

verses.kjv: Thou makest us to turn back from the enemy: and they which hate us spoil for themselves.
verses.bsb: You have made us retreat from the foe, and those who hate us have plundered us.

verses.kjv: Thou hast given us like sheep appointed for meat; and hast scattered us among the heathen.
verses.bsb: You have given us up as sheep to be devoured; You have scattered us among the nations.

verses.kjv: Thou sellest thy people for nought, and dost not increase thy wealth by their price.
verses.bsb: You sell Your people for nothing; no profit do You gain from their sale.

verses.kjv: Thou makest us a reproach to our neighbours, a scorn and a derision to them that are round about us.
verses.bsb: You have made us a reproach to our neighbors, a mockery and derision to those around us.

verses.kjv: Thou makest us a byword among the heathen, a shaking of the head among the people.
verses.bsb: You have made us a byword among the nations, a laughingstock among the peoples.

verses.kjv: My confusion is continually before me, and the shame of my face hath covered me,
verses.bsb: All day long my disgrace is before me, and shame has covered my face,

verses.kjv: For the voice of him that reproacheth and blasphemeth; by reason of the enemy and avenger.
verses.bsb: at the voice of the scorner and reviler, because of the enemy, bent on revenge.

verses.kjv: All this is come upon us; yet have we not forgotten thee, neither have we dealt falsely in thy covenant.
verses.bsb: All this has come upon us, though we have not forgotten You or betrayed Your covenant.

verses.kjv: Our heart is not turned back, neither have our steps declined from thy way;
verses.bsb: Our hearts have not turned back; our steps have not strayed from Your path.

verses.kjv: Though thou hast sore broken us in the place of dragons, and covered us with the shadow of death.
verses.bsb: But You have crushed us in the lair of jackals; You have covered us with deepest darkness.

verses.kjv: If we have forgotten the name of our God, or stretched out our hands to a strange god;
verses.bsb: If we had forgotten the name of our God or spread out our hands to a foreign god,

verses.kjv: Shall not God search this out? for he knoweth the secrets of the heart.
verses.bsb: would not God have discovered, since He knows the secrets of the heart?

verses.kjv: Yea, for thy sake are we killed all the day long; we are counted as sheep for the slaughter.
verses.bsb: Yet for Your sake we face death all day long; we are considered as sheep to be slaughtered.

verses.kjv: Awake, why sleepest thou, O Lord? arise, cast us not off for ever.
verses.bsb: Wake up, O Lord! Why are You sleeping? Arise! Do not reject us forever.

verses.kjv: Wherefore hidest thou thy face, and forgettest our affliction and our oppression?
verses.bsb: Why do You hide Your face and forget our affliction and oppression?

verses.kjv: For our soul is bowed down to the dust: our belly cleaveth unto the earth.
verses.bsb: For our soul has sunk to the dust; our bodies cling to the earth.

verses.kjv: Arise for our help, and redeem us for thy mercies’ sake.
verses.bsb: Rise up; be our help! Redeem us on account of Your loving devotion.

verses.kjv: MY heart is inditing a good matter: I speak of the things which I have made touching the king: my tongue is the pen of a ready writer.
verses.bsb: My heart is stirred by a noble theme as I recite my verses to the king; my tongue is the pen of a skillful writer.

verses.kjv: Thou art fairer than the children of men: grace is poured into thy lips: therefore God hath blessed thee for ever.
verses.bsb: You are the most handsome of men; grace has anointed your lips, since God has blessed you forever.

verses.kjv: Gird thy sword upon thy thigh, O most mighty, with thy glory and thy majesty.
verses.bsb: Strap your sword at your side, O mighty warrior; appear in your majesty and splendor.

verses.kjv: And in thy majesty ride prosperously because of truth and meekness and righteousness; and thy right hand shall teach thee terrible things.
verses.bsb: In your splendor ride forth in victory on behalf of truth and humility and justice; may your right hand show your awesome deeds.

verses.kjv: Thine arrows are sharp in the heart of the king’s enemies; whereby the people fall under thee.
verses.bsb: Your arrows pierce the hearts of the king’s foes; the nations fall beneath your feet.

verses.kjv: Thy throne, O God, is for ever and ever: the sceptre of thy kingdom is a right sceptre.
verses.bsb: Your throne, O God, endures forever and ever, and justice is the scepter of Your kingdom.

verses.kjv: Thou lovest righteousness, and hatest wickedness: therefore God, thy God, hath anointed thee with the oil of gladness above thy fellows.
verses.bsb: You have loved righteousness and hated wickedness; therefore God, your God, has anointed you above your companions with the oil of joy.

verses.kjv: All thy garments smell of myrrh, and aloes, and cassia, out of the ivory palaces, whereby they have made thee glad.
verses.bsb: All your garments are fragrant with myrrh and aloes and cassia; from palaces of ivory the harps make you glad.

verses.kjv: Kings’ daughters were among thy honourable women: upon thy right hand did stand the queen in gold of Ophir.
verses.bsb: The daughters of kings are among your honored women; the queen stands at your right hand, adorned with the gold of Ophir.

verses.kjv: Hearken, O daughter, and consider, and incline thine ear; forget also thine own people, and thy father’s house;
verses.bsb: Listen, O daughter! Consider and incline your ear: Forget your people and your father’s house,

verses.kjv: So shall the king greatly desire thy beauty: for he is thy Lord; and worship thou him.
verses.bsb: and the king will desire your beauty; bow to him, for he is your lord.

verses.kjv: And the daughter of Tyre shall be there with a gift; even the rich among the people shall intreat thy favour.
verses.bsb: The Daughter of Tyre will come with a gift; men of wealth will seek your favor.

verses.kjv: The king’s daughter is all glorious within: her clothing is of wrought gold.
verses.bsb: All glorious is the princess in her chamber; her gown is embroidered with gold.

verses.kjv: She shall be brought unto the king in raiment of needlework: the virgins her companions that follow her shall be brought unto thee.
verses.bsb: In colorful garments she is led to the king; her virgin companions are brought before you.

verses.kjv: With gladness and rejoicing shall they be brought: they shall enter into the king’s palace.
verses.bsb: They are led in with joy and gladness; they enter the palace of the king.

verses.kjv: Instead of thy fathers shall be thy children, whom thou mayest make princes in all the earth.
verses.bsb: Your sons will succeed your fathers; you will make them princes throughout the land.

verses.kjv: I will make thy name to be remembered in all generations: therefore shall the people praise thee for ever and ever.
verses.bsb: I will commemorate your name through all generations; therefore the nations will praise you forever and ever.

verses.kjv: GOD is our refuge and strength, a very present help in trouble.
verses.bsb: God is our refuge and strength, an ever-present help in times of trouble.

verses.kjv: Therefore will not we fear, though the earth be removed, and though the mountains be carried into the midst of the sea;
verses.bsb: Therefore we will not fear, though the earth is transformed and the mountains are toppled into the depths of the seas,

verses.kjv: Though the waters thereof roar and be troubled, though the mountains shake with the swelling thereof. Selah.
verses.bsb: though their waters roar and foam and the mountains quake in the surge. Selah

verses.kjv: There is a river, the streams whereof shall make glad the city of God, the holy place of the tabernacles of the most High.
verses.bsb: There is a river whose streams delight the city of God, the holy place where the Most High dwells.

verses.kjv: God is in the midst of her; she shall not be moved: God shall help her, and that right early.
verses.bsb: God is within her; she will not be moved. God will help her when morning dawns.

verses.kjv: The heathen raged, the kingdoms were moved: he uttered his voice, the earth melted.
verses.bsb: Nations rage, kingdoms crumble; the earth melts when He lifts His voice.

verses.kjv: The Lord of hosts is with us; the God of Jacob is our refuge. Selah.
verses.bsb: The LORD of Hosts is with us; the God of Jacob is our fortress. Selah

verses.kjv: Come, behold the works of the Lord, what desolations he hath made in the earth.
verses.bsb: Come, see the works of the LORD, who brings devastation upon the earth.

verses.kjv: He maketh wars to cease unto the end of the earth; he breaketh the bow, and cutteth the spear in sunder; he burneth the chariot in the fire.
verses.bsb: He makes wars to cease throughout the earth; He breaks the bow and shatters the spear; He burns the shields in the fire.

verses.kjv: Be still, and know that I am God: I will be exalted among the heathen, I will be exalted in the earth.
verses.bsb: “Be still and know that I am God; I will be exalted among the nations, I will be exalted over the earth.”

verses.kjv: The Lord of hosts is with us; the God of Jacob is our refuge. Selah.
verses.bsb: The LORD of Hosts is with us; the God of Jacob is our fortress. Selah

verses.kjv: O CLAP your hands, all ye people; shout unto God with the voice of triumph.
verses.bsb: Clap your hands, all you peoples; shout unto God with a voice of triumph.

verses.kjv: For the Lord most high is terrible; he is a great King over all the earth.
verses.bsb: How awesome is the LORD Most High, the great King over all the earth!

verses.kjv: He shall subdue the people under us, and the nations under our feet.
verses.bsb: He subdues nations beneath us, and peoples under our feet.

verses.kjv: He shall choose our inheritance for us, the excellency of Jacob whom he loved. Selah.
verses.bsb: He chooses our inheritance for us, the pride of Jacob, whom He loves. Selah

verses.kjv: God is gone up with a shout, the Lord with the sound of a trumpet.
verses.bsb: God has ascended amid shouts of joy, the LORD with the sound of the horn.

verses.kjv: Sing praises to God, sing praises: sing praises unto our King, sing praises.
verses.bsb: Sing praises to God, sing praises; sing praises to our King, sing praises!

verses.kjv: For God is the King of all the earth: sing ye praises with understanding.
verses.bsb: For God is King of all the earth; sing profound praises to Him.

verses.kjv: God reigneth over the heathen: God sitteth upon the throne of his holiness.
verses.bsb: God reigns over the nations; God is seated on His holy throne.

verses.kjv: The princes of the people are gathered together, even the people of the God of Abraham: for the shields of the earth belong unto God: he is greatly exalted.
verses.bsb: The nobles of the nations have assembled as the people of the God of Abraham; for the shields of the earth belong to God; He is highly exalted.

verses.kjv: GREAT is the Lord, and greatly to be praised in the city of our God, in the mountain of his holiness.
verses.bsb: Great is the LORD, and greatly to be praised in the city of our God, His holy mountain.

verses.kjv: Beautiful for situation, the joy of the whole earth, is mount Zion, on the sides of the north, the city of the great King.
verses.bsb: Beautiful in loftiness, the joy of all the earth, like the peaks of Zaphon is Mount Zion, the city of the great King.

verses.kjv: God is known in her palaces for a refuge.
verses.bsb: God is in her citadels; He has shown Himself to be a fortress.

verses.kjv: For, lo, the kings were assembled, they passed by together.
verses.bsb: For behold, the kings assembled; they all advanced together.

verses.kjv: They saw it, and so they marvelled; they were troubled, and hasted away.
verses.bsb: They saw and were astounded; they fled in terror.

verses.kjv: Fear took hold upon them there, and pain, as of a woman in travail.
verses.bsb: Trembling seized them there, anguish like a woman in labor.

verses.kjv: Thou breakest the ships of Tarshish with an east wind.
verses.bsb: With a wind from the east You wrecked the ships of Tarshish.

verses.kjv: As we have heard, so have we seen in the city of the Lord of hosts, in the city of our God: God will establish it for ever. Selah.
verses.bsb: As we have heard, so we have seen in the city of the LORD of Hosts, in the city of our God: God will establish her forever. Selah

verses.kjv: We have thought of thy lovingkindness, O God, in the midst of thy temple.
verses.bsb: Within Your temple, O God, we contemplate Your loving devotion.

verses.kjv: According to thy name, O God, so is thy praise unto the ends of the earth: thy right hand is full of righteousness.
verses.bsb: Your name, O God, like Your praise, reaches to the ends of the earth; Your right hand is full of righteousness.

verses.kjv: Let mount Zion rejoice, let the daughters of Judah be glad, because of thy judgments.
verses.bsb: Mount Zion is glad, the daughters of Judah rejoice, on account of Your judgments.

verses.kjv: Walk about Zion, and go round about her: tell the towers thereof.
verses.bsb: March around Zion, encircle her, count her towers,

verses.kjv: Mark ye well her bulwarks, consider her palaces; that ye may tell it to the generation following.
verses.bsb: consider her ramparts, tour her citadels, that you may tell the next generation.

verses.kjv: For this God is our God for ever and ever: he will be our guide even unto death.
verses.bsb: For this God is our God forever and ever; He will be our guide even till death.

verses.kjv: HEAR this, all ye people; give ear, all ye inhabitants of the world:
verses.bsb: Hear this, all you peoples; listen, all inhabitants of the world,

verses.kjv: Both low and high, rich and poor, together.
verses.bsb: both low and high, rich and poor alike.

verses.kjv: My mouth shall speak of wisdom; and the meditation of my heart shall be of understanding.
verses.bsb: My mouth will impart wisdom, and the meditation of my heart will bring understanding.

verses.kjv: I will incline mine ear to a parable: I will open my dark saying upon the harp.
verses.bsb: I will incline my ear to a proverb; I will express my riddle with the harp:

verses.kjv: Wherefore should I fear in the days of evil, when the iniquity of my heels shall compass me about?
verses.bsb: Why should I fear in times of trouble, when wicked usurpers surround me?

verses.kjv: They that trust in their wealth, and boast themselves in the multitude of their riches;
verses.bsb: They trust in their wealth and boast in their great riches.

verses.kjv: None of them can by any means redeem his brother, nor give to God a ransom for him:
verses.bsb: No man can possibly redeem his brother or pay his ransom to God.

verses.kjv: (For the redemption of their soul is precious, and it ceaseth for ever:)
verses.bsb: For the redemption of his soul is costly, and never can payment suffice,

verses.kjv: That he should still live for ever, and not see corruption.
verses.bsb: that he should live on forever and not see decay.

verses.kjv: For he seeth that wise men die, likewise the fool and the brutish person perish, and leave their wealth to others.
verses.bsb: For it is clear that wise men die, and the foolish and the senseless both perish and leave their wealth to others.

verses.kjv: Their inward thought is, that their houses shall continue for ever, and their dwelling places to all generations; they call their lands after their own names.
verses.bsb: Their graves are their eternal homes— their dwellings for endless generations— even though their lands were their namesakes.

verses.kjv: Nevertheless man being in honour abideth not: he is like the beasts that perish.
verses.bsb: But a man, despite his wealth, cannot endure; he is like the beasts that perish.

verses.kjv: This their way is their folly: yet their posterity approve their sayings. Selah.
verses.bsb: This is the fate of the self-confident and their followers who endorse their sayings. Selah

verses.kjv: Like sheep they are laid in the grave; death shall feed on them; and the upright shall have dominion over them in the morning; and their beauty shall consume in the grave from their dwelling.
verses.bsb: Like sheep they are destined for Sheol. Death will be their shepherd. The upright will rule them in the morning, and their form will decay in Sheol, far from their lofty abode.

verses.kjv: But God will redeem my soul from the power of the grave: for he shall receive me. Selah.
verses.bsb: But God will redeem my life from Sheol, for He will surely take me to Himself. Selah

verses.kjv: Be not thou afraid when one is made rich, when the glory of his house is increased;
verses.bsb: Do not be amazed when a man grows rich, when the splendor of his house increases.

verses.kjv: For when he dieth he shall carry nothing away: his glory shall not descend after him.
verses.bsb: For when he dies, he will carry nothing away; his abundance will not follow him down.

verses.kjv: Though while he lived he blessed his soul: and men will praise thee, when thou doest well to thyself.
verses.bsb: Though in his lifetime he blesses his soul— and men praise you when you prosper—

verses.kjv: He shall go to the generation of his fathers; they shall never see light.
verses.bsb: he will join the generation of his fathers, who will never see the light of day.

verses.kjv: Man that is in honour, and understandeth not, is like the beasts that perish.
verses.bsb: A man who has riches without understanding is like the beasts that perish.

verses.kjv: THE mighty God, even the Lord, hath spoken, and called the earth from the rising of the sun unto the going down thereof.
verses.bsb: The Mighty One, God the LORD, speaks and summons the earth from where the sun rises to where it sets.

verses.kjv: Out of Zion, the perfection of beauty, God hath shined.
verses.bsb: From Zion, perfect in beauty, God shines forth.

verses.kjv: Our God shall come, and shall not keep silence: a fire shall devour before him, and it shall be very tempestuous round about him.
verses.bsb: Our God approaches and will not be silent! Consuming fire precedes Him, and a tempest rages around Him.

verses.kjv: He shall call to the heavens from above, and to the earth, that he may judge his people.
verses.bsb: He summons the heavens above, and the earth, that He may judge His people:

verses.kjv: Gather my saints together unto me; those that have made a covenant with me by sacrifice.
verses.bsb: “Gather to Me My saints, who made a covenant with Me by sacrifice.”

verses.kjv: And the heavens shall declare his righteousness: for God is judge himself. Selah.
verses.bsb: And the heavens proclaim His righteousness, for God Himself is Judge. Selah

verses.kjv: Hear, O my people, and I will speak; O Israel, and I will testify against thee: I am God, even thy God.
verses.bsb: “Hear, O My people, and I will speak, O Israel, and I will testify against you: I am God, your God.

verses.kjv: I will not reprove thee for thy sacrifices or thy burnt offerings, to have been continually before me.
verses.bsb: I do not rebuke you for your sacrifices, and your burnt offerings are ever before Me.

verses.kjv: I will take no bullock out of thy house, nor he goats out of thy folds.
verses.bsb: I have no need for a bull from your stall or goats from your pens,

verses.kjv: For every beast of the forest is mine, and the cattle upon a thousand hills.
verses.bsb: for every beast of the forest is Mine— the cattle on a thousand hills.

verses.kjv: I know all the fowls of the mountains: and the wild beasts of the field are mine.
verses.bsb: I know every bird in the mountains, and the creatures of the field are Mine.

verses.kjv: If I were hungry, I would not tell thee: for the world is mine, and the fulness thereof.
verses.bsb: If I were hungry, I would not tell you, for the world is Mine, and the fullness thereof.

verses.kjv: Will I eat the flesh of bulls, or drink the blood of goats?
verses.bsb: Do I eat the flesh of bulls, or drink the blood of goats?

verses.kjv: Offer unto God thanksgiving; and pay thy vows unto the most High:
verses.bsb: Sacrifice a thank offering to God, and fulfill your vows to the Most High.

verses.kjv: And call upon me in the day of trouble: I will deliver thee, and thou shalt glorify me.
verses.bsb: Call upon Me in the day of trouble; I will deliver you, and you will honor Me.”

verses.kjv: But unto the wicked God saith, What hast thou to do to declare my statutes, or that thou shouldest take my covenant in thy mouth?
verses.bsb: To the wicked, however, God says, “What right have you to recite My statutes and to bear My covenant on your lips?

verses.kjv: Seeing thou hatest instruction, and castest my words behind thee.
verses.bsb: For you hate My instruction and cast My words behind you.

verses.kjv: When thou sawest a thief, then thou consentedst with him, and hast been partaker with adulterers.
verses.bsb: When you see a thief, you befriend him, and throw in your lot with adulterers.

verses.kjv: Thou givest thy mouth to evil, and thy tongue frameth deceit.
verses.bsb: You unleash your mouth for evil and unharness your tongue for deceit.

verses.kjv: Thou sittest and speakest against thy brother; thou slanderest thine own mother’s son.
verses.bsb: You sit and malign your brother; you slander your own mother’s son.

verses.kjv: These things hast thou done, and I kept silence; thou thoughtest that I was altogether such an one as thyself: but I will reprove thee, and set them in order before thine eyes.
verses.bsb: You have done these things, and I kept silent; you thought I was just like you. But now I rebuke you and accuse you to your face.

verses.kjv: Now consider this, ye that forget God, lest I tear you in pieces, and there be none to deliver.
verses.bsb: Now consider this, you who forget God, lest I tear you to pieces, with no one to rescue you:

verses.kjv: Whoso offereth praise glorifieth me: and to him that ordereth his conversation aright will I shew the salvation of God.
verses.bsb: He who sacrifices a thank offering honors Me, and to him who rights his way, I will show the salvation of God.”

verses.kjv: HAVE mercy upon me, O God, according to thy lovingkindness: according unto the multitude of thy tender mercies blot out my transgressions.
verses.bsb: Have mercy on me, O God, according to Your loving devotion; according to Your great compassion, blot out my transgressions.

verses.kjv: Wash me throughly from mine iniquity, and cleanse me from my sin.
verses.bsb: Wash me clean of my iniquity and cleanse me from my sin.

verses.kjv: For I acknowledge my transgressions: and my sin is ever before me.
verses.bsb: For I know my transgressions, and my sin is always before me.

verses.kjv: Against thee, thee only, have I sinned, and done this evil in thy sight: that thou mightest be justified when thou speakest, and be clear when thou judgest.
verses.bsb: Against You, You only, have I sinned and done what is evil in Your sight, so that You may be proved right when You speak and blameless when You judge.

verses.kjv: Behold, I was shapen in iniquity; and in sin did my mother conceive me.
verses.bsb: Surely I was brought forth in iniquity; I was sinful when my mother conceived me.

verses.kjv: Behold, thou desirest truth in the inward parts: and in the hidden part thou shalt make me to know wisdom.
verses.bsb: Surely You desire truth in the inmost being; You teach me wisdom in the inmost place.

verses.kjv: Purge me with hyssop, and I shall be clean: wash me, and I shall be whiter than snow.
verses.bsb: Purify me with hyssop, and I will be clean; wash me, and I will be whiter than snow.

verses.kjv: Make me to hear joy and gladness; that the bones which thou hast broken may rejoice.
verses.bsb: Let me hear joy and gladness; let the bones You have crushed rejoice.

verses.kjv: Hide thy face from my sins, and blot out all mine iniquities.
verses.bsb: Hide Your face from my sins and blot out all my iniquities.
