verses.kjv: Create in me a clean heart, O God; and renew a right spirit within me.
verses.bsb: Create in me a clean heart, O God, and renew a right spirit within me.

verses.kjv: Cast me not away from thy presence; and take not thy holy spirit from me.
verses.bsb: Cast me not away from Your presence; take not Your Holy Spirit from me.

verses.kjv: Restore unto me the joy of thy salvation; and uphold me with thy free spirit.
verses.bsb: Restore to me the joy of Your salvation, and sustain me with a willing spirit.

verses.kjv: Then will I teach transgressors thy ways; and sinners shall be converted unto thee.
verses.bsb: Then I will teach transgressors Your ways, and sinners will return to You.

verses.kjv: Deliver me from bloodguiltiness, O God, thou God of my salvation: and my tongue shall sing aloud of thy righteousness.
verses.bsb: Deliver me from bloodguilt, O God, the God of my salvation, and my tongue will sing of Your righteousness.

verses.kjv: O Lord, open thou my lips; and my mouth shall shew forth thy praise.
verses.bsb: O Lord, open my lips, and my mouth will declare Your praise.

verses.kjv: For thou desirest not sacrifice; else would I give it: thou delightest not in burnt offering.
verses.bsb: For You do not delight in sacrifice, or I would bring it; You take no pleasure in burnt offerings.

verses.kjv: The sacrifices of God are a broken spirit: a broken and a contrite heart, O God, thou wilt not despise.
verses.bsb: The sacrifices of God are a broken spirit; a broken and a contrite heart, O God, You will not despise.

verses.kjv: Do good in thy good pleasure unto Zion: build thou the walls of Jerusalem.
verses.bsb: In Your good pleasure, cause Zion to prosper; build up the walls of Jerusalem.

verses.kjv: Then shalt thou be pleased with the sacrifices of righteousness, with burnt offering and whole burnt offering: then shall they offer bullocks upon thine altar.
verses.bsb: Then You will delight in righteous sacrifices, in whole burnt offerings; then bulls will be offered on Your altar.

verses.kjv: WHY boastest thou thyself in mischief, O mighty man? the goodness of God endureth continually.
verses.bsb: Why do you boast of evil, O mighty man? The loving devotion of God endures all day long.

verses.kjv: Thy tongue deviseth mischiefs; like a sharp rasor, working deceitfully.
verses.bsb: Your tongue devises destruction like a sharpened razor, O worker of deceit.

verses.kjv: Thou lovest evil more than good; and lying rather than to speak righteousness. Selah.
verses.bsb: You love evil more than good, falsehood more than speaking truth. Selah

verses.kjv: Thou lovest all devouring words, O thou deceitful tongue.
verses.bsb: You love every word that devours, O deceitful tongue.

verses.kjv: God shall likewise destroy thee for ever, he shall take thee away, and pluck thee out of thy dwelling place, and root thee out of the land of the living. Selah.
verses.bsb: Surely God will bring you down to everlasting ruin; He will snatch you up and tear you away from your tent; He will uproot you from the land of the living. Selah

verses.kjv: The righteous also shall see, and fear, and shall laugh at him:
verses.bsb: The righteous will see and fear; they will mock the evildoer, saying,

verses.kjv: Lo, this is the man that made not God his strength; but trusted in the abundance of his riches, and strengthened himself in his wickedness.
verses.bsb: “Look at the man who did not make God his refuge, but trusted in the abundance of his wealth and strengthened himself by destruction.”

verses.kjv: But I am like a green olive tree in the house of God: I trust in the mercy of God for ever and ever.
verses.bsb: But I am like an olive tree flourishing in the house of God; I trust in the loving devotion of God forever and ever.

verses.kjv: I will praise thee for ever, because thou hast done it: and I will wait on thy name; for it is good before thy saints.
verses.bsb: I will praise You forever, because You have done it. I will wait on Your name— for it is good— in the presence of Your saints.

verses.kjv: THE fool hath said in his heart, There is no God. Corrupt are they, and have done abominable iniquity: there is none that doeth good.
verses.bsb: The fool says in his heart, “There is no God.”   They are corrupt; their ways are vile. There is no one who does good.

verses.kjv: God looked down from heaven upon the children of men, to see if there were any that did understand, that did seek God.
verses.bsb: God looks down from heaven upon the sons of men to see if any understand, if any seek God.

verses.kjv: Every one of them is gone back: they are altogether become filthy; there is none that doeth good, no, not one.
verses.bsb: All have turned away, they have together become corrupt; there is no one who does good, not even one.

verses.kjv: Have the workers of iniquity no knowledge? who eat up my people as they eat bread: they have not called upon God.
verses.bsb: Will the workers of iniquity never learn?   They devour my people like bread; they refuse to call upon God.

verses.kjv: There were they in great fear, where no fear was: for God hath scattered the bones of him that encampeth against thee: thou hast put them to shame, because God hath despised them.
verses.bsb: There they are, overwhelmed with dread, where there was nothing to fear. For God has scattered the bones of those who besieged you. You put them to shame, for God has despised them.

verses.kjv: Oh that the salvation of Israel were come out of Zion! When God bringeth back the captivity of his people, Jacob shall rejoice, and Israel shall be glad.
verses.bsb: Oh, that the salvation of Israel would come from Zion!   When God restores His captive people, let Jacob rejoice, let Israel be glad!

verses.kjv: SAVE me, O God, by thy name, and judge me by thy strength.
verses.bsb: Save me, O God, by Your name, and vindicate me by Your might!

verses.kjv: Hear my prayer, O God; give ear to the words of my mouth.
verses.bsb: Hear my prayer, O God; listen to the words of my mouth.

verses.kjv: For strangers are risen up against me, and oppressors seek after my soul: they have not set God before them. Selah.
verses.bsb: For strangers rise up against me, and ruthless men seek my life— men with no regard for God. Selah

verses.kjv: Behold, God is mine helper: the Lord is with them that uphold my soul.
verses.bsb: Surely God is my helper; the Lord is the sustainer of my soul.

verses.kjv: He shall reward evil unto mine enemies: cut them off in thy truth.
verses.bsb: He will reward my enemies with evil. In Your faithfulness, destroy them.

verses.kjv: I will freely sacrifice unto thee: I will praise thy name, O Lord; for it is good.
verses.bsb: Freely I will sacrifice to You; I will praise Your name, O LORD, for it is good.

verses.kjv: For he hath delivered me out of all trouble: and mine eye hath seen his desire upon mine enemies.
verses.bsb: For He has delivered me from every trouble, and my eyes have stared down my foes.

verses.kjv: GIVE ear to my prayer, O God; and hide not thyself from my supplication.
verses.bsb: Listen to my prayer, O God, and do not ignore my plea.

verses.kjv: Attend unto me, and hear me: I mourn in my complaint, and make a noise;
verses.bsb: Attend to me and answer me. I am restless in my complaint, and distraught

verses.kjv: Because of the voice of the enemy, because of the oppression of the wicked: for they cast iniquity upon me, and in wrath they hate me.
verses.bsb: at the voice of the enemy, at the pressure of the wicked. For they release disaster upon me and revile me in their anger.

verses.kjv: My heart is sore pained within me: and the terrors of death are fallen upon me.
verses.bsb: My heart murmurs within me, and the terrors of death assail me.

verses.kjv: Fearfulness and trembling are come upon me, and horror hath overwhelmed me.
verses.bsb: Fear and trembling grip me, and horror has overwhelmed me.

verses.kjv: And I said, Oh that I had wings like a dove! for then would I fly away, and be at rest.
verses.bsb: I said, “Oh, that I had wings like a dove! I would fly away and find rest.

verses.kjv: Lo, then would I wander far off, and remain in the wilderness. Selah.
verses.bsb: How far away I would flee! In the wilderness I would remain. Selah

verses.kjv: I would hasten my escape from the windy storm and tempest.
verses.bsb: I would hurry to my shelter, far from this raging tempest.”

verses.kjv: Destroy, O Lord, and divide their tongues: for I have seen violence and strife in the city.
verses.bsb: O Lord, confuse and confound their speech, for I see violence and strife in the city.

verses.kjv: Day and night they go about it upon the walls thereof: mischief also and sorrow are in the midst of it.
verses.bsb: Day and night they encircle the walls, while malice and trouble lie within.

verses.kjv: Wickedness is in the midst thereof: deceit and guile depart not from her streets.
verses.bsb: Destruction is within; oppression and deceit never leave the streets.

verses.kjv: For it was not an enemy that reproached me; then I could have borne it: neither was it he that hated me that did magnify himself against me; then I would have hid myself from him:
verses.bsb: For it is not an enemy who insults me; that I could endure. It is not a foe who rises against me; from him I could hide.

verses.kjv: But it was thou, a man mine equal, my guide, and mine acquaintance.
verses.bsb: But it is you, a man like myself, my companion and close friend.

verses.kjv: We took sweet counsel together, and walked unto the house of God in company.
verses.bsb: We shared sweet fellowship together; we walked with the crowd into the house of God.

verses.kjv: Let death seize upon them, and let them go down quick into hell: for wickedness is in their dwellings, and among them.
verses.bsb: Let death seize them by surprise; let them go down to Sheol alive, for evil is with them in their homes.

verses.kjv: As for me, I will call upon God; and the Lord shall save me.
verses.bsb: But I call to God, and the LORD saves me.

verses.kjv: Evening, and morning, and at noon, will I pray, and cry aloud: and he shall hear my voice.
verses.bsb: Morning, noon, and night, I cry out in distress, and He hears my voice.

verses.kjv: He hath delivered my soul in peace from the battle that was against me: for there were many with me.
verses.bsb: He redeems my soul in peace from the battle waged against me, even though many oppose me.

verses.kjv: God shall hear, and afflict them, even he that abideth of old. Selah. Because they have no changes, therefore they fear not God.
verses.bsb: God will hear and humiliate them— the One enthroned for the ages— Selah because they do not change and they have no fear of God.

verses.kjv: He hath put forth his hands against such as be at peace with him: he hath broken his covenant.
verses.bsb: My companion attacks his friends; he violates his covenant.

verses.kjv: The words of his mouth were smoother than butter, but war was in his heart: his words were softer than oil, yet were they drawn swords.
verses.bsb: His speech is smooth as butter, but war is in his heart. His words are softer than oil, yet they are swords unsheathed.

verses.kjv: Cast thy burden upon the Lord, and he shall sustain thee: he shall never suffer the righteous to be moved.
verses.bsb: Cast your burden upon the LORD and He will sustain you; He will never let the righteous be shaken.

verses.kjv: But thou, O God, shalt bring them down into the pit of destruction: bloody and deceitful men shall not live out half their days; but I will trust in thee.
verses.bsb: But You, O God, will bring them down to the Pit of destruction; men of bloodshed and deceit will not live out half their days.   But I will trust in You.

verses.kjv: BE merciful unto me, O God: for man would swallow me up; he fighting daily oppresseth me.
verses.bsb: Be merciful to me, O God, for men are hounding me; all day they press their attack.

verses.kjv: Mine enemies would daily swallow me up: for they be many that fight against me, O thou most High.
verses.bsb: My enemies pursue me all day long, for many proudly assail me.

verses.kjv: What time I am afraid, I will trust in thee.
verses.bsb: When I am afraid, I put my trust in You.

verses.kjv: In God I will praise his word, in God I have put my trust; I will not fear what flesh can do unto me.
verses.bsb: In God, whose word I praise— in God I trust. I will not be afraid. What can man do to me?

verses.kjv: Every day they wrest my words: all their thoughts are against me for evil.
verses.bsb: All day long they twist my words; all their thoughts are on my demise.

verses.kjv: They gather themselves together, they hide themselves, they mark my steps, when they wait for my soul.
verses.bsb: They conspire, they lurk, they watch my steps while they wait to take my life.

verses.kjv: Shall they escape by iniquity? in thine anger cast down the people, O God.
verses.bsb: In spite of such sin, will they escape? In Your anger, O God, cast down the nations.

verses.kjv: Thou tellest my wanderings: put thou my tears into thy bottle: are they not in thy book?
verses.bsb: You have taken account of my wanderings. Put my tears in Your bottle— are they not in Your book?

verses.kjv: When I cry unto thee, then shall mine enemies turn back: this I know; for God is for me.
verses.bsb: Then my enemies will retreat on the day I cry for help. By this I will know that God is on my side.

verses.kjv: In God will I praise his word: in the Lord will I praise his word.
verses.bsb: In God, whose word I praise, in the LORD, whose word I praise,

verses.kjv: In God have I put my trust: I will not be afraid what man can do unto me.
verses.bsb: in God I trust; I will not be afraid. What can man do to me?

verses.kjv: Thy vows are upon me, O God: I will render praises unto thee.
verses.bsb: Your vows are upon me, O God; I will render thank offerings to You.

verses.kjv: For thou hast delivered my soul from death: wilt not thou deliver my feet from falling, that I may walk before God in the light of the living?
verses.bsb: For You have delivered my soul from death, and my feet from stumbling, that I may walk before God in the light of life.

verses.kjv: BE merciful unto me, O God, be merciful unto me: for my soul trusteth in thee: yea, in the shadow of thy wings will I make my refuge, until these calamities be overpast.
verses.bsb: Have mercy on me, O God, have mercy, for in You my soul takes refuge. In the shadow of Your wings I will take shelter until the danger has passed.

verses.kjv: I will cry unto God most high; unto God that performeth all things for me.
verses.bsb: I cry out to God Most High, to God who fulfills His purpose for me.

verses.kjv: He shall send from heaven, and save me from the reproach of him that would swallow me up. Selah. God shall send forth his mercy and his truth.
verses.bsb: He reaches down from heaven and saves me; He rebukes those who trample me. Selah God sends forth His loving devotion and His truth.

verses.kjv: My soul is among lions: and I lie even among them that are set on fire, even the sons of men, whose teeth are spears and arrows, and their tongue a sharp sword.
verses.bsb: My soul is among the lions; I lie down with ravenous beasts— with men whose teeth are spears and arrows, whose tongues are sharp swords.

verses.kjv: Be thou exalted, O God, above the heavens; let thy glory be above all the earth.
verses.bsb: Be exalted, O God, above the heavens; may Your glory cover all the earth.

verses.kjv: They have prepared a net for my steps; my soul is bowed down: they have digged a pit before me, into the midst whereof they are fallen themselves. Selah.
verses.bsb: They spread a net for my feet; my soul was despondent. They dug a pit before me, but they themselves have fallen into it! Selah

verses.kjv: My heart is fixed, O God, my heart is fixed: I will sing and give praise.
verses.bsb: My heart is steadfast, O God, my heart is steadfast. I will sing and make music.

verses.kjv: Awake up, my glory; awake, psaltery and harp: I myself will awake early.
verses.bsb: Awake, my glory! Awake, O harp and lyre! I will awaken the dawn.

verses.kjv: I will praise thee, O Lord, among the people: I will sing unto thee among the nations.
verses.bsb: I will praise You, O Lord, among the nations; I will sing Your praises among the peoples.

verses.kjv: For thy mercy is great unto the heavens, and thy truth unto the clouds.
verses.bsb: For Your loving devotion reaches to the heavens, and Your faithfulness to the clouds.

verses.kjv: Be thou exalted, O God, above the heavens: let thy glory be above all the earth.
verses.bsb: Be exalted, O God, above the heavens; may Your glory cover all the earth.

verses.kjv: DO ye indeed speak righteousness, O congregation? do ye judge uprightly, O ye sons of men?
verses.bsb: Do you indeed speak justly, O rulers? Do you judge uprightly, O sons of men?

verses.kjv: Yea, in heart ye work wickedness; ye weigh the violence of your hands in the earth.
verses.bsb: No, in your hearts you devise injustice; with your hands you mete out violence on the earth.

verses.kjv: The wicked are estranged from the womb: they go astray as soon as they be born, speaking lies.
verses.bsb: The wicked are estranged from the womb; the liars go astray from birth.

verses.kjv: Their poison is like the poison of a serpent: they are like the deaf adder that stoppeth her ear;
verses.bsb: Their venom is like the venom of a snake, like a cobra that shuts its ears,

verses.kjv: Which will not hearken to the voice of charmers, charming never so wisely.
verses.bsb: refusing to hear the tune of the charmer who skillfully weaves his spell.

verses.kjv: Break their teeth, O God, in their mouth: break out the great teeth of the young lions, O Lord.
verses.bsb: O God, shatter their teeth in their mouths; O LORD, tear out the fangs of the lions.

verses.kjv: Let them melt away as waters which run continually: when he bendeth his bow to shoot his arrows, let them be as cut in pieces.
verses.bsb: May they vanish like water that runs off; when they draw the bow, may their arrows be blunted.

verses.kjv: As a snail which melteth, let every one of them pass away: like the untimely birth of a woman, that they may not see the sun.
verses.bsb: Like a slug that dissolves in its slime, like a woman’s stillborn child, may they never see the sun.

verses.kjv: Before your pots can feel the thorns, he shall take them away as with a whirlwind, both living, and in his wrath.
verses.bsb: Before your pots can feel the burning thorns— whether green or dry— He will sweep them away.

verses.kjv: The righteous shall rejoice when he seeth the vengeance: he shall wash his feet in the blood of the wicked.
verses.bsb: The righteous will rejoice when they see they are avenged; they will wash their feet in the blood of the wicked.

verses.kjv: So that a man shall say, Verily there is a reward for the righteous: verily he is a God that judgeth in the earth.
verses.bsb: Then men will say, “There is surely a reward for the righteous! There is surely a God who judges the earth!”

verses.kjv: DELIVER me from mine enemies, O my God: defend me from them that rise up against me.
verses.bsb: Deliver me from my enemies, O my God; protect me from those who rise against me.

verses.kjv: Deliver me from the workers of iniquity, and save me from bloody men.
verses.bsb: Deliver me from workers of iniquity, and save me from men of bloodshed.

verses.kjv: For, lo, they lie in wait for my soul: the mighty are gathered against me; not for my transgression, nor for my sin, O Lord.
verses.bsb: See how they lie in wait for me. Fierce men conspire against me for no transgression or sin of my own, O LORD.

verses.kjv: They run and prepare themselves without my fault: awake to help me, and behold.
verses.bsb: For no fault of my own, they move swiftly to attack me. Arise to help me, and take notice.

verses.kjv: Thou therefore, O Lord God of hosts, the God of Israel, awake to visit all the heathen: be not merciful to any wicked transgressors. Selah.
verses.bsb: O LORD God of Hosts, the God of Israel, rouse Yourself to punish all the nations; show no mercy to the wicked traitors. Selah

verses.kjv: They return at evening: they make a noise like a dog, and go round about the city.
verses.bsb: They return in the evening, snarling like dogs and prowling around the city.

verses.kjv: Behold, they belch out with their mouth: swords are in their lips: for who, say they, doth hear?
verses.bsb: See what they spew from their mouths— sharp words from their lips: “For who can hear us?”

verses.kjv: But thou, O Lord, shalt laugh at them; thou shalt have all the heathen in derision.
verses.bsb: But You, O LORD, laugh at them; You scoff at all the nations.

verses.kjv: Because of his strength will I wait upon thee: for God is my defence.
verses.bsb: I will keep watch for You, O my strength, because You, O God, are my fortress.

verses.kjv: The God of my mercy shall prevent me: God shall let me see my desire upon mine enemies.
verses.bsb: My God of loving devotion will come to meet me; God will let me stare down my foes.

verses.kjv: Slay them not, lest my people forget: scatter them by thy power; and bring them down, O Lord our shield.
verses.bsb: Do not kill them, or my people will forget. Scatter them by Your power, and bring them down, O Lord, our shield.

verses.kjv: For the sin of their mouth and the words of their lips let them even be taken in their pride: and for cursing and lying which they speak.
verses.bsb: By the sins of their mouths and the words of their lips, let them be trapped in their pride, in the curses and lies they utter.

verses.kjv: Consume them in wrath, consume them, that they may not be: and let them know that God ruleth in Jacob unto the ends of the earth. Selah.
verses.bsb: Consume them in wrath; consume them till they are no more, so it may be known to the ends of the earth that God rules over Jacob. Selah

verses.kjv: And at evening let them return; and let them make a noise like a dog, and go round about the city.
verses.bsb: They return in the evening, snarling like dogs and prowling around the city.

verses.kjv: Let them wander up and down for meat, and grudge if they be not satisfied.
verses.bsb: They scavenge for food, and growl if they are not satisfied.

verses.kjv: But I will sing of thy power; yea, I will sing aloud of thy mercy in the morning: for thou hast been my defence and refuge in the day of my trouble.
verses.bsb: But I will sing of Your strength and proclaim Your loving devotion in the morning. For You are my fortress, my refuge in times of trouble.

verses.kjv: Unto thee, O my strength, will I sing: for God is my defence, and the God of my mercy.
verses.bsb: To You, O my strength, I sing praises, for You, O God, are my fortress, my God of loving devotion.

verses.kjv: O GOD, thou hast cast us off, thou hast scattered us, thou hast been displeased; O turn thyself to us again.
verses.bsb: You have rejected us, O God; You have broken us; You have been angry; restore us!

verses.kjv: Thou hast made the earth to tremble; thou hast broken it: heal the breaches thereof; for it shaketh.
verses.bsb: You have shaken the land and torn it open. Heal its fractures, for it is quaking.

verses.kjv: Thou hast shewed thy people hard things: thou hast made us to drink the wine of astonishment.
verses.bsb: You have shown Your people hardship; we are staggered from the wine You made us drink.

verses.kjv: Thou hast given a banner to them that fear thee, that it may be displayed because of the truth. Selah.
verses.bsb: You have raised a banner for those who fear You, that they may flee the bow. Selah

verses.kjv: That thy beloved may be delivered; save with thy right hand, and hear me.
verses.bsb: Respond and save us with Your right hand, that Your beloved may be delivered.

verses.kjv: God hath spoken in his holiness; I will rejoice, I will divide Shechem, and mete out the valley of Succoth.
verses.bsb: God has spoken from His sanctuary: “I will triumph! I will parcel out Shechem and apportion the Valley of Succoth.

verses.kjv: Gilead is mine, and Manasseh is mine; Ephraim also is the strength of mine head; Judah is my lawgiver;
verses.bsb: Gilead is Mine, and Manasseh is Mine; Ephraim is My helmet, Judah is My scepter.

verses.kjv: Moab is my washpot; over Edom will I cast out my shoe: Philistia, triumph thou because of me.
verses.bsb: Moab is My washbasin; upon Edom I toss My sandal; over Philistia I shout in triumph.”

verses.kjv: Who will bring me into the strong city? who will lead me into Edom?
verses.bsb: Who will bring me to the fortified city? Who will lead me to Edom?

verses.kjv: Wilt not thou, O God, which hadst cast us off? and thou, O God, which didst not go out with our armies?
verses.bsb: Have You not rejected us, O God? Will You no longer march out, O God, with our armies?

verses.kjv: Give us help from trouble: for vain is the help of man.
verses.bsb: Give us aid against the enemy, for the help of man is worthless.

verses.kjv: Through God we shall do valiantly: for he it is that shall tread down our enemies.
verses.bsb: With God we will perform with valor, and He will trample our enemies.

verses.kjv: HEAR my cry, O God; attend unto my prayer.
verses.bsb: Hear my cry, O God; attend to my prayer.

verses.kjv: From the end of the earth will I cry unto thee, when my heart is overwhelmed: lead me to the rock that is higher than I.
verses.bsb: From the ends of the earth I call out to You whenever my heart is faint. Lead me to the rock that is higher than I.

verses.kjv: For thou hast been a shelter for me, and a strong tower from the enemy.
verses.bsb: For You have been my refuge, a tower of strength against the enemy.

verses.kjv: I will abide in thy tabernacle for ever: I will trust in the covert of thy wings. Selah.
verses.bsb: Let me dwell in Your tent forever and take refuge in the shelter of Your wings. Selah

verses.kjv: For thou, O God, hast heard my vows: thou hast given me the heritage of those that fear thy name.
verses.bsb: For You have heard my vows, O God; You have given me the inheritance reserved for those who fear Your name.

verses.kjv: Thou wilt prolong the king’s life: and his years as many generations.
verses.bsb: Increase the days of the king’s life; may his years span many generations.

verses.kjv: He shall abide before God for ever: O prepare mercy and truth, which may preserve him.
verses.bsb: May he sit enthroned in God’s presence forever; appoint Your loving devotion and Your faithfulness to guard him.

verses.kjv: So will I sing praise unto thy name for ever, that I may daily perform my vows.
verses.bsb: Then I will ever sing praise to Your name and fulfill my vows day by day.

verses.kjv: TRULY my soul waiteth upon God: from him cometh my salvation.
verses.bsb: In God alone my soul finds rest; my salvation comes from Him.

verses.kjv: He only is my rock and my salvation; he is my defence; I shall not be greatly moved.
verses.bsb: He alone is my rock and my salvation. He is my fortress; I will never be shaken.

verses.kjv: How long will ye imagine mischief against a man? ye shall be slain all of you: as a bowing wall shall ye be, and as a tottering fence.
verses.bsb: How long will you threaten a man? Will all of you throw him down like a leaning wall or a tottering fence?

verses.kjv: They only consult to cast him down from his excellency: they delight in lies: they bless with their mouth, but they curse inwardly. Selah.
verses.bsb: They fully intend to cast him down from his lofty perch; they delight in lies; with their mouths they bless, but inwardly they curse. Selah

verses.kjv: My soul, wait thou only upon God; for my expectation is from him.
verses.bsb: Rest in God alone, O my soul, for my hope comes from Him.

verses.kjv: He only is my rock and my salvation: he is my defence; I shall not be moved.
verses.bsb: He alone is my rock and my salvation; He is my fortress; I will not be shaken.

verses.kjv: In God is my salvation and my glory: the rock of my strength, and my refuge, is in God.
verses.bsb: My salvation and my honor rest on God, my strong rock; my refuge is in God.

verses.kjv: Trust in him at all times; ye people, pour out your heart before him: God is a refuge for us. Selah.
verses.bsb: Trust in Him at all times, O people; pour out your hearts before Him. God is our refuge. Selah

verses.kjv: Surely men of low degree are vanity, and men of high degree are a lie: to be laid in the balance, they are altogether lighter than vanity.
verses.bsb: Lowborn men are but a vapor, the exalted but a lie. Weighed on the scale, they go up; together they are but a vapor.

verses.kjv: Trust not in oppression, and become not vain in robbery: if riches increase, set not your heart upon them.
verses.bsb: Place no trust in extortion, or false hope in stolen goods. If your riches increase, do not set your heart upon them.

verses.kjv: God hath spoken once; twice have I heard this; that power belongeth unto God.
verses.bsb: God has spoken once; I have heard this twice: that power belongs to God,

verses.kjv: Also unto thee, O Lord, belongeth mercy: for thou renderest to every man according to his work.
verses.bsb: and loving devotion to You, O Lord. For You will repay each man according to his deeds.

verses.kjv: O GOD, thou art my God; early will I seek thee: my soul thirsteth for thee, my flesh longeth for thee in a dry and thirsty land, where no water is;
verses.bsb: O God, You are my God. Earnestly I seek You; my soul thirsts for You. My body yearns for You in a dry and weary land without water.

verses.kjv: To see thy power and thy glory, so as I have seen thee in the sanctuary.
verses.bsb: So I have seen You in the sanctuary and beheld Your power and glory.

verses.kjv: Because thy lovingkindness is better than life, my lips shall praise thee.
verses.bsb: Because Your loving devotion is better than life, my lips will glorify You.

verses.kjv: Thus will I bless thee while I live: I will lift up my hands in thy name.
verses.bsb: So I will bless You as long as I live; in Your name I will lift my hands.

verses.kjv: My soul shall be satisfied as with marrow and fatness; and my mouth shall praise thee with joyful lips:
verses.bsb: My soul is satisfied as with the richest of foods; with joyful lips my mouth will praise You.

verses.kjv: When I remember thee upon my bed, and meditate on thee in the night watches.
verses.bsb: When I remember You on my bed, I think of You through the watches of the night.

verses.kjv: Because thou hast been my help, therefore in the shadow of thy wings will I rejoice.
verses.bsb: For You are my help; I will sing for joy in the shadow of Your wings.

verses.kjv: My soul followeth hard after thee: thy right hand upholdeth me.
verses.bsb: My soul clings to You; Your right hand upholds me.

verses.kjv: But those that seek my soul, to destroy it, shall go into the lower parts of the earth.
verses.bsb: But those who seek my life to destroy it will go into the depths of the earth.

verses.kjv: They shall fall by the sword: they shall be a portion for foxes.
verses.bsb: They will fall to the power of the sword; they will become a portion for foxes.

verses.kjv: But the king shall rejoice in God; every one that sweareth by him shall glory: but the mouth of them that speak lies shall be stopped.
verses.bsb: But the king will rejoice in God; all who swear by Him will exult, for the mouths of liars will be shut.

verses.kjv: HEAR my voice, O God, in my prayer: preserve my life from fear of the enemy.
verses.bsb: Hear, O God, my voice of complaint; preserve my life from dread of the enemy.

verses.kjv: Hide me from the secret counsel of the wicked; from the insurrection of the workers of iniquity:
verses.bsb: Hide me from the scheming of the wicked, from the mob of workers of iniquity,

verses.kjv: Who whet their tongue like a sword, and bend their bows to shoot their arrows, even bitter words:
verses.bsb: who sharpen their tongues like swords and aim their bitter words like arrows,

verses.kjv: That they may shoot in secret at the perfect: suddenly do they shoot at him, and fear not.
verses.bsb: ambushing the innocent in seclusion, shooting suddenly, without fear.

verses.kjv: They encourage themselves in an evil matter: they commune of laying snares privily; they say, Who shall see them?
verses.bsb: They hold fast to their evil purpose; they speak of hiding their snares. “Who will see them?” they say.

verses.kjv: They search out iniquities; they accomplish a diligent search: both the inward thought of every one of them, and the heart, is deep.
verses.bsb: They devise injustice and say, “We have perfected a secret plan.” For the inner man and the heart are mysterious.

verses.kjv: But God shall shoot at them with an arrow; suddenly shall they be wounded.
verses.bsb: But God will shoot them with arrows; suddenly they will be wounded.

verses.kjv: So they shall make their own tongue to fall upon themselves: all that see them shall flee away.
verses.bsb: They will be made to stumble, their own tongues turned against them. All who see will shake their heads.

verses.kjv: And all men shall fear, and shall declare the work of God; for they shall wisely consider of his doing.
verses.bsb: Then all mankind will fear and proclaim the work of God; so they will ponder what He has done.

verses.kjv: The righteous shall be glad in the Lord, and shall trust in him; and all the upright in heart shall glory.
verses.bsb: Let the righteous rejoice in the LORD and take refuge in Him; let all the upright in heart exult.

verses.kjv: PRAISE waiteth for thee, O God, in Sion: and unto thee shall the vow be performed.
verses.bsb: Praise awaits You, O God, in Zion; to You our vows will be fulfilled.

verses.kjv: O thou that hearest prayer, unto thee shall all flesh come.
verses.bsb: O You who listen to prayer, all people will come to You.

verses.kjv: Iniquities prevail against me: as for our transgressions, thou shalt purge them away.
verses.bsb: When iniquities prevail against me, You atone for our transgressions.

verses.kjv: Blessed is the man whom thou choosest, and causest to approach unto thee, that he may dwell in thy courts: we shall be satisfied with the goodness of thy house, even of thy holy temple.
verses.bsb: Blessed is the one You choose and bring near to dwell in Your courts! We are filled with the goodness of Your house, the holiness of Your temple.

verses.kjv: By terrible things in righteousness wilt thou answer us, O God of our salvation; who art the confidence of all the ends of the earth, and of them that are afar off upon the sea:
verses.bsb: With awesome deeds of righteousness You answer us, O God of our salvation, the hope of all the ends of the earth and of the farthest seas.

verses.kjv: Which by his strength setteth fast the mountains; being girded with power:
verses.bsb: You formed the mountains by Your power, having girded Yourself with might.

verses.kjv: Which stilleth the noise of the seas, the noise of their waves, and the tumult of the people.
verses.bsb: You stilled the roaring of the seas, the pounding of their waves, and the tumult of the nations.

verses.kjv: They also that dwell in the uttermost parts are afraid at thy tokens: thou makest the outgoings of the morning and evening to rejoice.
verses.bsb: Those who live far away fear Your wonders; You make the dawn and sunset shout for joy.

verses.kjv: Thou visitest the earth, and waterest it: thou greatly enrichest it with the river of God, which is full of water: thou preparest them corn, when thou hast so provided for it.
verses.bsb: You attend to the earth and water it; with abundance You enrich it. The streams of God are full of water, for You prepare our grain by providing for the earth.

verses.kjv: Thou waterest the ridges thereof abundantly: thou settlest the furrows thereof: thou makest it soft with showers: thou blessest the springing thereof.
verses.bsb: You soak its furrows and level its ridges; You soften it with showers and bless its growth.

verses.kjv: Thou crownest the year with thy goodness; and thy paths drop fatness.
verses.bsb: You crown the year with Your bounty, and Your paths overflow with plenty.

verses.kjv: They drop upon the pastures of the wilderness: and the little hills rejoice on every side.
verses.bsb: The pastures of the wilderness overflow; the hills are robed with joy.

verses.kjv: The pastures are clothed with flocks; the valleys also are covered over with corn; they shout for joy, they also sing.
verses.bsb: The pastures are clothed with flocks, and the valleys are decked with grain. They shout in triumph; indeed, they sing.

verses.kjv: MAKE a joyful noise unto God, all ye lands:
verses.bsb: Make a joyful noise to God, all the earth!

verses.kjv: Sing forth the honour of his name: make his praise glorious.
verses.bsb: Sing the glory of His name; make His praise glorious.

verses.kjv: Say unto God, How terrible art thou in thy works! through the greatness of thy power shall thine enemies submit themselves unto thee.
verses.bsb: Say to God, “How awesome are Your deeds! So great is Your power that Your enemies cower before You.

verses.kjv: All the earth shall worship thee, and shall sing unto thee; they shall sing to thy name. Selah.
verses.bsb: All the earth bows down to You; they sing praise to You; they sing praise to Your name.” Selah

verses.kjv: Come and see the works of God: he is terrible in his doing toward the children of men.
verses.bsb: Come and see the works of God; how awesome are His deeds toward mankind.

verses.kjv: He turned the sea into dry land: they went through the flood on foot: there did we rejoice in him.
verses.bsb: He turned the sea into dry land; they passed through the waters on foot; there we rejoiced in Him.

verses.kjv: He ruleth by his power for ever; his eyes behold the nations: let not the rebellious exalt themselves. Selah.
verses.bsb: He rules forever by His power; His eyes watch the nations. Do not let the rebellious exalt themselves. Selah

verses.kjv: O bless our God, ye people, and make the voice of his praise to be heard:
verses.bsb: Bless our God, O peoples; let the sound of His praise be heard.

verses.kjv: Which holdeth our soul in life, and suffereth not our feet to be moved.
verses.bsb: He preserves our lives and keeps our feet from slipping.

verses.kjv: For thou, O God, hast proved us: thou hast tried us, as silver is tried.
verses.bsb: For You, O God, have tested us; You have refined us like silver.

verses.kjv: Thou broughtest us into the net; thou laidst affliction upon our loins.
verses.bsb: You led us into the net; You laid burdens on our backs.

verses.kjv: Thou hast caused men to ride over our heads; we went through fire and through water: but thou broughtest us out into a wealthy place.
verses.bsb: You let men ride over our heads; we went through fire and water, but You brought us into abundance.

verses.kjv: I will go into thy house with burnt offerings: I will pay thee my vows,
verses.bsb: I will enter Your house with burnt offerings; I will fulfill my vows to You—

verses.kjv: Which my lips have uttered, and my mouth hath spoken, when I was in trouble.
verses.bsb: the vows that my lips promised and my mouth spoke in my distress.

verses.kjv: I will offer unto thee burnt sacrifices of fatlings, with the incense of rams; I will offer bullocks with goats. Selah.
verses.bsb: I will offer You fatlings as burnt offerings, with the fragrant smoke of rams; I will offer bulls and goats. Selah

verses.kjv: Come and hear, all ye that fear God, and I will declare what he hath done for my soul.
verses.bsb: Come and listen, all you who fear God, and I will declare what He has done for me.

verses.kjv: I cried unto him with my mouth, and he was extolled with my tongue.
verses.bsb: I cried out to Him with my mouth and praised Him with my tongue.

verses.kjv: If I regard iniquity in my heart, the Lord will not hear me:
verses.bsb: If I had cherished iniquity in my heart, the Lord would not have listened.

verses.kjv: But verily God hath heard me; he hath attended to the voice of my prayer.
verses.bsb: But God has surely heard; He has attended to the sound of my prayer.

verses.kjv: Blessed be God, which hath not turned away my prayer, nor his mercy from me.
verses.bsb: Blessed be God, who has not rejected my prayer or withheld from me His loving devotion!

verses.kjv: GOD be merciful unto us, and bless us; and cause his face to shine upon us; Selah.
verses.bsb: May God be gracious to us and bless us, and cause His face to shine upon us, Selah

verses.kjv: That thy way may be known upon earth, thy saving health among all nations.
verses.bsb: that Your ways may be known on earth, Your salvation among all nations.

verses.kjv: Let the people praise thee, O God; let all the people praise thee.
verses.bsb: Let the peoples praise You, O God; let all the peoples praise You.

verses.kjv: O let the nations be glad and sing for joy: for thou shalt judge the people righteously, and govern the nations upon earth. Selah.
verses.bsb: Let the nations be glad and sing for joy, for You judge the peoples justly and lead the nations of the earth. Selah

verses.kjv: Let the people praise thee, O God; let all the people praise thee.
verses.bsb: Let the peoples praise You, O God; let all the peoples praise You.

verses.kjv: Then shall the earth yield her increase; and God, even our own God, shall bless us.
verses.bsb: The earth has yielded its harvest; God, our God, blesses us.

verses.kjv: God shall bless us; and all the ends of the earth shall fear him.
verses.bsb: God blesses us, that all the ends of the earth shall fear Him.

verses.kjv: LET God arise, let his enemies be scattered: let them also that hate him flee before him.
verses.bsb: God arises. His enemies are scattered, and those who hate Him flee His presence.

verses.kjv: As smoke is driven away, so drive them away: as wax melteth before the fire, so let the wicked perish at the presence of God.
verses.bsb: As smoke is blown away, You will drive them out; as wax melts before the fire, the wicked will perish in the presence of God.

verses.kjv: But let the righteous be glad; let them rejoice before God: yea, let them exceedingly rejoice.
verses.bsb: But the righteous will be glad and rejoice before God; they will celebrate with joy.

verses.kjv: Sing unto God, sing praises to his name: extol him that rideth upon the heavens by his name JAH, and rejoice before him.
verses.bsb: Sing to God! Sing praises to His name. Exalt Him who rides on the clouds— His name is the LORD— and rejoice before Him.

verses.kjv: A father of the fatherless, and a judge of the widows, is God in his holy habitation.
verses.bsb: A father of the fatherless, and a defender of the widows, is God in His holy habitation.

verses.kjv: God setteth the solitary in families: he bringeth out those which are bound with chains: but the rebellious dwell in a dry land.
verses.bsb: God settles the lonely in families; He leads the prisoners out to prosperity, but the rebellious dwell in a sun-scorched land.

verses.kjv: O God, when thou wentest forth before thy people, when thou didst march through the wilderness; Selah:
verses.bsb: O God, when You went out before Your people, when You marched through the wasteland, Selah

verses.kjv: The earth shook, the heavens also dropped at the presence of God: even Sinai itself was moved at the presence of God, the God of Israel.
verses.bsb: the earth shook and the heavens poured down rain before God, the One on Sinai, before God, the God of Israel.

verses.kjv: Thou, O God, didst send a plentiful rain, whereby thou didst confirm thine inheritance, when it was weary.
verses.bsb: You sent abundant rain, O God; You refreshed Your weary inheritance.

verses.kjv: Thy congregation hath dwelt therein: thou, O God, hast prepared of thy goodness for the poor.
verses.bsb: Your flock settled therein; O God, from Your bounty You provided for the poor.

verses.kjv: The Lord gave the word: great was the company of those that published it.
verses.bsb: The Lord gives the command; a great company of women proclaim it:

verses.kjv: Kings of armies did flee apace: and she that tarried at home divided the spoil.
verses.bsb: “Kings and their armies flee in haste; she who waits at home divides the plunder.

verses.kjv: Though ye have lien among the pots, yet shall ye be as the wings of a dove covered with silver, and her feathers with yellow gold.
verses.bsb: Though you lie down among the sheepfolds, the wings of the dove are covered with silver, and her feathers with shimmering gold.”

verses.kjv: When the Almighty scattered kings in it, it was white as snow in Salmon.
verses.bsb: When the Almighty scattered the kings in the land, it was like the snow falling on Zalmon.

verses.kjv: The hill of God is as the hill of Bashan; an high hill as the hill of Bashan.
verses.bsb: A mountain of God is Mount Bashan; a mountain of many peaks is Mount Bashan.

verses.kjv: Why leap ye, ye high hills? this is the hill which God desireth to dwell in; yea, the Lord will dwell in it for ever.
verses.bsb: Why do you gaze in envy, O mountains of many peaks? This is the mountain God chose for His dwelling, where the LORD will surely dwell forever.

verses.kjv: The chariots of God are twenty thousand, even thousands of angels: the Lord is among them, as in Sinai, in the holy place.
verses.bsb: The chariots of God are tens of thousands— thousands of thousands are they; the Lord is in His sanctuary as He was at Sinai.

verses.kjv: Thou hast ascended on high, thou hast led captivity captive: thou hast received gifts for men; yea, for the rebellious also, that the Lord God might dwell among them.
verses.bsb: You have ascended on high; You have led captives away. You have received gifts from men, even from the rebellious, that the LORD God may dwell there.

verses.kjv: Blessed be the Lord, who daily loadeth us with benefits, even the God of our salvation. Selah.
verses.bsb: Blessed be the Lord, who daily bears our burden, the God of our salvation. Selah

verses.kjv: He that is our God is the God of salvation; and unto God the Lord belong the issues from death.
verses.bsb: Our God is a God of deliverance; the Lord GOD is our rescuer from death.

verses.kjv: But God shall wound the head of his enemies, and the hairy scalp of such an one as goeth on still in his trespasses.
verses.bsb: Surely God will crush the heads of His enemies, the hairy crowns of those who persist in guilty ways.

verses.kjv: The Lord said, I will bring again from Bashan, I will bring my people again from the depths of the sea:
verses.bsb: The Lord said, “I will retrieve them from Bashan, I will bring them up from the depths of the sea,

verses.kjv: That thy foot may be dipped in the blood of thine enemies, and the tongue of thy dogs in the same.
verses.bsb: that your foot may be dipped in the blood of your foes— the tongues of your dogs in the same.”

verses.kjv: They have seen thy goings, O God; even the goings of my God, my King, in the sanctuary.
verses.bsb: They have seen Your procession, O God— the march of my God and King into the sanctuary.

verses.kjv: The singers went before, the players on instruments followed after; among them were the damsels playing with timbrels.
verses.bsb: The singers lead the way, the musicians follow after, among the maidens playing tambourines.

verses.kjv: Bless ye God in the congregations, even the Lord, from the fountain of Israel.
verses.bsb: Bless God in the great congregation; bless the LORD from the fountain of Israel.

verses.kjv: There is little Benjamin with their ruler, the princes of Judah and their council, the princes of Zebulun, and the princes of Naphtali.
verses.bsb: There is Benjamin, the youngest, ruling them, the princes of Judah in their company, the princes of Zebulun and of Naphtali.

verses.kjv: Thy God hath commanded thy strength: strengthen, O God, that which thou hast wrought for us.
verses.bsb: Summon Your power, O God; show Your strength, O God, which You have exerted on our behalf.

verses.kjv: Because of thy temple at Jerusalem shall kings bring presents unto thee.
verses.bsb: Because of Your temple at Jerusalem kings will bring You gifts.

verses.kjv: Rebuke the company of spearmen, the multitude of the bulls, with the calves of the people, till every one submit himself with pieces of silver: scatter thou the people that delight in war.
verses.bsb: Rebuke the beast in the reeds, the herd of bulls among the calves of the nations, until it submits, bringing bars of silver. Scatter the nations who delight in war.

verses.kjv: Princes shall come out of Egypt; Ethiopia shall soon stretch out her hands unto God.
verses.bsb: Envoys will arrive from Egypt; Cush will stretch out her hands to God.

verses.kjv: Sing unto God, ye kingdoms of the earth; O sing praises unto the Lord; Selah:
verses.bsb: Sing to God, O kingdoms of the earth; sing praises to the Lord— Selah

verses.kjv: To him that rideth upon the heavens of heavens, which were of old; lo, he doth send out his voice, and that a mighty voice.
verses.bsb: to Him who rides upon the highest heavens of old; behold, His mighty voice resounds.

verses.kjv: Ascribe ye strength unto God: his excellency is over Israel, and his strength is in the clouds.
verses.bsb: Ascribe the power to God, whose majesty is over Israel, whose strength is in the skies.

verses.kjv: O God, thou art terrible out of thy holy places: the God of Israel is he that giveth strength and power unto his people. Blessed be God.
verses.bsb: O God, You are awesome in Your sanctuary; the God of Israel Himself gives strength and power to His people.   Blessed be God!

verses.kjv: SAVE me, O God; for the waters are come in unto my soul.
verses.bsb: Save me, O God, for the waters are up to my neck.

verses.kjv: I sink in deep mire, where there is no standing: I am come into deep waters, where the floods overflow me.
verses.bsb: I have sunk into the miry depths, where there is no footing; I have drifted into deep waters, where the flood engulfs me.

verses.kjv: I am weary of my crying: my throat is dried: mine eyes fail while I wait for my God.
verses.bsb: I am weary from my crying; my throat is parched. My eyes fail, looking for my God.

verses.kjv: They that hate me without a cause are more than the hairs of mine head: they that would destroy me, being mine enemies wrongfully, are mighty: then I restored that which I took not away.
verses.bsb: Those who hate me without cause outnumber the hairs of my head; many are those who would destroy me— my enemies for no reason. Though I did not steal, I must repay.

verses.kjv: O God, thou knowest my foolishness; and my sins are not hid from thee.
verses.bsb: You know my folly, O God, and my guilt is not hidden from You.

verses.kjv: Let not them that wait on thee, O Lord God of hosts, be ashamed for my sake: let not those that seek thee be confounded for my sake, O God of Israel.
verses.bsb: May those who hope in You not be ashamed through me, O Lord GOD of Hosts; may those who seek You not be dishonored through me, O God of Israel.

verses.kjv: Because for thy sake I have borne reproach; shame hath covered my face.
verses.bsb: For I have endured scorn for Your sake, and shame has covered my face.

verses.kjv: I am become a stranger unto my brethren, and an alien unto my mother’s children.
verses.bsb: I have become a stranger to my brothers and a foreigner to my mother’s sons,

verses.kjv: For the zeal of thine house hath eaten me up; and the reproaches of them that reproached thee are fallen upon me.
verses.bsb: because zeal for Your house has consumed me, and the insults of those who insult You have fallen on me.

verses.kjv: When I wept, and chastened my soul with fasting, that was to my reproach.
verses.bsb: I wept and fasted, but it brought me reproach.

verses.kjv: I made sackcloth also my garment; and I became a proverb to them.
verses.bsb: I made sackcloth my clothing, and I was sport to them.

verses.kjv: They that sit in the gate speak against me; and I was the song of the drunkards.
verses.bsb: Those who sit at the gate mock me, and I am the song of drunkards.

verses.kjv: But as for me, my prayer is unto thee, O Lord, in an acceptable time: O God, in the multitude of thy mercy hear me, in the truth of thy salvation.
verses.bsb: But my prayer to You, O LORD, is for a time of favor. In Your abundant loving devotion, O God, answer me with Your sure salvation.

verses.kjv: Deliver me out of the mire, and let me not sink: let me be delivered from them that hate me, and out of the deep waters.
verses.bsb: Rescue me from the mire and do not let me sink; deliver me from my foes and out of the deep waters.

verses.kjv: Let not the waterflood overflow me, neither let the deep swallow me up, and let not the pit shut her mouth upon me.
verses.bsb: Do not let the floods engulf me or the depths swallow me up; let not the Pit close its mouth over me.

verses.kjv: Hear me, O Lord; for thy lovingkindness is good: turn unto me according to the multitude of thy tender mercies.
verses.bsb: Answer me, O LORD, for Your loving devotion is good; turn to me in keeping with Your great compassion.

verses.kjv: And hide not thy face from thy servant; for I am in trouble: hear me speedily.
verses.bsb: Hide not Your face from Your servant, for I am in distress. Answer me quickly!

verses.kjv: Draw nigh unto my soul, and redeem it: deliver me because of mine enemies.
verses.bsb: Draw near to my soul and redeem me; ransom me because of my foes.

verses.kjv: Thou hast known my reproach, and my shame, and my dishonour: mine adversaries are all before thee.
verses.bsb: You know my reproach, my shame and disgrace. All my adversaries are before You.

verses.kjv: Reproach hath broken my heart; and I am full of heaviness: and I looked for some to take pity, but there was none; and for comforters, but I found none.
verses.bsb: Insults have broken my heart, and I am in despair. I looked for sympathy, but there was none, for comforters, but I found no one.

verses.kjv: They gave me also gall for my meat; and in my thirst they gave me vinegar to drink.
verses.bsb: They poisoned my food with gall and gave me vinegar to quench my thirst.

verses.kjv: Let their table become a snare before them: and that which should have been for their welfare, let it become a trap.
verses.bsb: May their table become a snare; may it be a retribution and a trap.

verses.kjv: Let their eyes be darkened, that they see not; and make their loins continually to shake.
verses.bsb: May their eyes be darkened so they cannot see, and their backs be bent forever.

verses.kjv: Pour out thine indignation upon them, and let thy wrathful anger take hold of them.
verses.bsb: Pour out Your wrath upon them, and let Your burning anger overtake them.

verses.kjv: Let their habitation be desolate; and let none dwell in their tents.
verses.bsb: May their place be deserted; let there be no one to dwell in their tents.

verses.kjv: For they persecute him whom thou hast smitten; and they talk to the grief of those whom thou hast wounded.
verses.bsb: For they persecute the one You struck and recount the pain of those You wounded.

verses.kjv: Add iniquity unto their iniquity: and let them not come into thy righteousness.
verses.bsb: Add iniquity to their iniquity; let them not share in Your righteousness.

verses.kjv: Let them be blotted out of the book of the living, and not be written with the righteous.
verses.bsb: May they be blotted out of the Book of Life and not listed with the righteous.

verses.kjv: But I am poor and sorrowful: let thy salvation, O God, set me up on high.
verses.bsb: But I am in pain and distress; let Your salvation protect me, O God.

verses.kjv: I will praise the name of God with a song, and will magnify him with thanksgiving.
verses.bsb: I will praise God’s name in song and exalt Him with thanksgiving.

verses.kjv: This also shall please the Lord better than an ox or bullock that hath horns and hoofs.
verses.bsb: And this will please the LORD more than an ox, more than a bull with horns and hooves.

verses.kjv: The humble shall see this, and be glad: and your heart shall live that seek God.
verses.bsb: The humble will see and rejoice. You who seek God, let your hearts be revived!

verses.kjv: For the Lord heareth the poor, and despiseth not his prisoners.
verses.bsb: For the LORD listens to the needy and does not despise His captive people.

verses.kjv: Let the heaven and earth praise him, the seas, and every thing that moveth therein.
verses.bsb: Let heaven and earth praise Him, the seas and everything that moves in them.

verses.kjv: For God will save Zion, and will build the cities of Judah: that they may dwell there, and have it in possession.
verses.bsb: For God will save Zion and rebuild the cities of Judah, that they may dwell there and possess it.

verses.kjv: The seed also of his servants shall inherit it: and they that love his name shall dwell therein.
verses.bsb: The descendants of His servants will inherit it, and those who love His name will settle in it.

verses.kjv: MAKE haste, O God, to deliver me; make haste to help me, O Lord.
verses.bsb: Make haste, O God, to deliver me! Hurry, O LORD, to help me!

verses.kjv: Let them be ashamed and confounded that seek after my soul: let them be turned backward, and put to confusion, that desire my hurt.
verses.bsb: May those who seek my life be ashamed and confounded; may those who wish me harm be repelled and humiliated.

verses.kjv: Let them be turned back for a reward of their shame that say, Aha, aha.
verses.bsb: May those who say, “Aha, aha!” retreat because of their shame.

verses.kjv: Let all those that seek thee rejoice and be glad in thee: and let such as love thy salvation say continually, Let God be magnified.
verses.bsb: May all who seek You rejoice and be glad in You; may those who love Your salvation always say, “Let God be magnified!”

verses.kjv: But I am poor and needy: make haste unto me, O God: thou art my help and my deliverer; O Lord, make no tarrying.
verses.bsb: But I am poor and needy; hurry to me, O God. You are my help and my deliverer; O LORD, do not delay.

verses.kjv: IN thee, O Lord, do I put my trust: let me never be put to confusion.
verses.bsb: In You, O LORD, I have taken refuge; let me never be put to shame.

verses.kjv: Deliver me in thy righteousness, and cause me to escape: incline thine ear unto me, and save me.
verses.bsb: In Your justice, rescue and deliver me; incline Your ear and save me.

verses.kjv: Be thou my strong habitation, whereunto I may continually resort: thou hast given commandment to save me; for thou art my rock and my fortress.
verses.bsb: Be my rock of refuge, where I can always go. Give the command to save me, for You are my rock and my fortress.

verses.kjv: Deliver me, O my God, out of the hand of the wicked, out of the hand of the unrighteous and cruel man.
verses.bsb: Deliver me, O my God, from the hand of the wicked, from the grasp of the unjust and ruthless.

verses.kjv: For thou art my hope, O Lord God: thou art my trust from my youth.
verses.bsb: For You are my hope, O Lord GOD, my confidence from my youth.

verses.kjv: By thee have I been holden up from the womb: thou art he that took me out of my mother’s bowels: my praise shall be continually of thee.
verses.bsb: I have leaned on You since birth; You pulled me from my mother’s womb. My praise is always for You.

verses.kjv: I am as a wonder unto many; but thou art my strong refuge.
verses.bsb: I have become a portent to many, but You are my strong refuge.

verses.kjv: Let my mouth be filled with thy praise and with thy honour all the day.
verses.bsb: My mouth is filled with Your praise and with Your splendor all day long.

verses.kjv: Cast me not off in the time of old age; forsake me not when my strength faileth.
verses.bsb: Do not discard me in my old age; do not forsake me when my strength fails.

verses.kjv: For mine enemies speak against me; and they that lay wait for my soul take counsel together,
verses.bsb: For my enemies speak against me, and those who lie in wait for my life conspire,

verses.kjv: Saying, God hath forsaken him: persecute and take him; for there is none to deliver him.
verses.bsb: saying, “God has forsaken him; pursue him and seize him, for there is no one to rescue him.”

verses.kjv: O God, be not far from me: O my God, make haste for my help.
verses.bsb: Be not far from me, O God. Hurry, O my God, to help me.

verses.kjv: Let them be confounded and consumed that are adversaries to my soul; let them be covered with reproach and dishonour that seek my hurt.
verses.bsb: May the accusers of my soul be ashamed and consumed; may those who seek my harm be covered with scorn and disgrace.

verses.kjv: But I will hope continually, and will yet praise thee more and more.
verses.bsb: But I will always hope and will praise You more and more.

verses.kjv: My mouth shall shew forth thy righteousness and thy salvation all the day; for I know not the numbers thereof.
verses.bsb: My mouth will declare Your righteousness and Your salvation all day long, though I cannot know their full measure.

verses.kjv: I will go in the strength of the Lord God: I will make mention of thy righteousness, even of thine only.
verses.bsb: I will enter in the strength of the Lord GOD; I will proclaim Your righteousness—Yours alone.

verses.kjv: O God, thou hast taught me from my youth: and hitherto have I declared thy wondrous works.
verses.bsb: O God, You have taught me from my youth, and to this day I proclaim Your marvelous deeds.

verses.kjv: Now also when I am old and grayheaded, O God, forsake me not; until I have shewed thy strength unto this generation, and thy power to every one that is to come.
verses.bsb: Even when I am old and gray, do not forsake me, O God, until I proclaim Your power to the next generation, Your might to all who are to come.

verses.kjv: Thy righteousness also, O God, is very high, who hast done great things: O God, who is like unto thee!
verses.bsb: Your righteousness reaches to the heavens, O God, You who have done great things. Who, O God, is like You?

verses.kjv: Thou, which hast shewed me great and sore troubles, shalt quicken me again, and shalt bring me up again from the depths of the earth.
verses.bsb: Though You have shown me many troubles and misfortunes, You will revive me once again. Even from the depths of the earth You will bring me back up.

verses.kjv: Thou shalt increase my greatness, and comfort me on every side.
verses.bsb: You will increase my honor and comfort me once again.

verses.kjv: I will also praise thee with the psaltery, even thy truth, O my God: unto thee will I sing with the harp, O thou Holy One of Israel.
verses.bsb: So I will praise You with the harp for Your faithfulness, O my God; I will sing praise to You with the lyre, O Holy One of Israel.

verses.kjv: My lips shall greatly rejoice when I sing unto thee; and my soul, which thou hast redeemed.
verses.bsb: When I sing praise to You my lips will shout for joy, along with my soul, which You have redeemed.

verses.kjv: My tongue also shall talk of thy righteousness all the day long: for they are confounded, for they are brought unto shame, that seek my hurt.
verses.bsb: My tongue will indeed proclaim Your righteousness all day long, for those who seek my harm are disgraced and confounded.

verses.kjv: GIVE the king thy judgments, O God, and thy righteousness unto the king’s son.
verses.bsb: Endow the king with Your justice, O God, and the son of the king with Your righteousness.

verses.kjv: He shall judge thy people with righteousness, and thy poor with judgment.
verses.bsb: May he judge Your people with righteousness and Your afflicted with justice.

verses.kjv: The mountains shall bring peace to the people, and the little hills, by righteousness.
verses.bsb: May the mountains bring peace to the people, and the hills bring righteousness.

verses.kjv: He shall judge the poor of the people, he shall save the children of the needy, and shall break in pieces the oppressor.
verses.bsb: May he vindicate the afflicted among the people; may he save the children of the needy and crush the oppressor.

verses.kjv: They shall fear thee as long as the sun and moon endure, throughout all generations.
verses.bsb: May they fear him as long as the sun shines, as long as the moon remains, through all generations.

verses.kjv: He shall come down like rain upon the mown grass: as showers that water the earth.
verses.bsb: May he be like rain that falls on freshly cut grass, like spring showers that water the earth.

verses.kjv: In his days shall the righteous flourish; and abundance of peace so long as the moon endureth.
verses.bsb: May the righteous flourish in his days and prosperity abound, until the moon is no more.

verses.kjv: He shall have dominion also from sea to sea, and from the river unto the ends of the earth.
verses.bsb: May he rule from sea to sea, and from the Euphrates to the ends of the earth.

verses.kjv: They that dwell in the wilderness shall bow before him; and his enemies shall lick the dust.
verses.bsb: May the nomads bow before him, and his enemies lick the dust.

verses.kjv: The kings of Tarshish and of the isles shall bring presents: the kings of Sheba and Seba shall offer gifts.
verses.bsb: May the kings of Tarshish and distant shores bring tribute; may the kings of Sheba and Seba offer gifts.

verses.kjv: Yea, all kings shall fall down before him: all nations shall serve him.
verses.bsb: May all kings bow down to him and all nations serve him.

verses.kjv: For he shall deliver the needy when he crieth; the poor also, and him that hath no helper.
verses.bsb: For he will deliver the needy who cry out and the afflicted who have no helper.

verses.kjv: He shall spare the poor and needy, and shall save the souls of the needy.
verses.bsb: He will take pity on the poor and needy and save the lives of the oppressed.

verses.kjv: He shall redeem their soul from deceit and violence: and precious shall their blood be in his sight.
verses.bsb: He will redeem them from oppression and violence, for their blood is precious in his sight.

verses.kjv: And he shall live, and to him shall be given of the gold of Sheba: prayer also shall be made for him continually; and daily shall he be praised.
verses.bsb: Long may he live! May gold from Sheba be given him. May people ever pray for him; may they bless him all day long.

verses.kjv: There shall be an handful of corn in the earth upon the top of the mountains; the fruit thereof shall shake like Lebanon: and they of the city shall flourish like grass of the earth.
verses.bsb: May there be an abundance of grain in the land; may it sway atop the hills. May its fruit trees flourish like the forests of Lebanon, and its people like the grass of the field.

verses.kjv: His name shall endure for ever: his name shall be continued as long as the sun: and men shall be blessed in him: all nations shall call him blessed.
verses.bsb: May his name endure forever; may his name continue as long as the sun shines. In him may all nations be blessed; may they call him blessed.

verses.kjv: Blessed be the Lord God, the God of Israel, who only doeth wondrous things.
verses.bsb: Blessed be the LORD God, the God of Israel, who alone does marvelous deeds.

verses.kjv: And blessed be his glorious name for ever: and let the whole earth be filled with his glory; Amen, and Amen.
verses.bsb: And blessed be His glorious name forever; may all the earth be filled with His glory.   Amen and amen.

verses.kjv: The prayers of David the son of Jesse are ended.
verses.bsb: Thus conclude the prayers of David son of Jesse.

verses.kjv: TRULY God is good to Israel, even to such as are of a clean heart.
verses.bsb: Surely God is good to Israel, to those who are pure in heart.

verses.kjv: But as for me, my feet were almost gone; my steps had well nigh slipped.
verses.bsb: But as for me, my feet had almost stumbled; my steps had nearly slipped.

verses.kjv: For I was envious at the foolish, when I saw the prosperity of the wicked.
verses.bsb: For I envied the arrogant when I saw the prosperity of the wicked.

verses.kjv: For there are no bands in their death: but their strength is firm.
verses.bsb: They have no struggle in their death; their bodies are well-fed.

verses.kjv: They are not in trouble as other men; neither are they plagued like other men.
verses.bsb: They are free of the burdens others carry; they are not afflicted like other men.

verses.kjv: Therefore pride compasseth them about as a chain; violence covereth them as a garment.
verses.bsb: Therefore pride is their necklace; a garment of violence covers them.

verses.kjv: Their eyes stand out with fatness: they have more than heart could wish.
verses.bsb: From their prosperity proceeds iniquity; the imaginations of their hearts run wild.

verses.kjv: They are corrupt, and speak wickedly concerning oppression: they speak loftily.
verses.bsb: They mock and speak with malice; with arrogance they threaten oppression.

verses.kjv: They set their mouth against the heavens, and their tongue walketh through the earth.
verses.bsb: They set their mouths against the heavens, and their tongues strut across the earth.

verses.kjv: Therefore his people return hither: and waters of a full cup are wrung out to them.
verses.bsb: So their people return to this place and drink up waters in abundance.

verses.kjv: And they say, How doth God know? and is there knowledge in the most High?
verses.bsb: The wicked say, “How can God know? Does the Most High have knowledge?”

verses.kjv: Behold, these are the ungodly, who prosper in the world; they increase in riches.
verses.bsb: Behold, these are the wicked— always carefree as they increase their wealth.

verses.kjv: Verily I have cleansed my heart in vain, and washed my hands in innocency.
verses.bsb: Surely in vain I have kept my heart pure; in innocence I have washed my hands.

verses.kjv: For all the day long have I been plagued, and chastened every morning.
verses.bsb: For I am afflicted all day long and punished every morning.

verses.kjv: If I say, I will speak thus; behold, I should offend against the generation of thy children.
verses.bsb: If I had said, “I will speak this way,” then I would have betrayed Your children.

verses.kjv: When I thought to know this, it was too painful for me;
verses.bsb: When I tried to understand all this, it was troublesome in my sight

verses.kjv: Until I went into the sanctuary of God; then understood I their end.
verses.bsb: until I entered God’s sanctuary; then I discerned their end.

verses.kjv: Surely thou didst set them in slippery places: thou castedst them down into destruction.
verses.bsb: Surely You set them on slick ground; You cast them down into ruin.

verses.kjv: How are they brought into desolation, as in a moment! they are utterly consumed with terrors.
verses.bsb: How suddenly they are laid waste, completely swept away by terrors!

verses.kjv: As a dream when one awaketh; so, O Lord, when thou awakest, thou shalt despise their image.
verses.bsb: Like one waking from a dream, so You, O Lord, awaken and despise their form.

verses.kjv: Thus my heart was grieved, and I was pricked in my reins.
verses.bsb: When my heart was grieved and I was pierced within,

verses.kjv: So foolish was I, and ignorant: I was as a beast before thee.
verses.bsb: I was senseless and ignorant; I was a brute beast before You.

verses.kjv: Nevertheless I am continually with thee: thou hast holden me by my right hand.
verses.bsb: Yet I am always with You; You hold my right hand.

verses.kjv: Thou shalt guide me with thy counsel, and afterward receive me to glory.
verses.bsb: You guide me with Your counsel, and later receive me in glory.

verses.kjv: Whom have I in heaven but thee? and there is none upon earth that I desire beside thee.
verses.bsb: Whom have I in heaven but You? And on earth I desire no one besides You.

verses.kjv: My flesh and my heart faileth: but God is the strength of my heart, and my portion for ever.
verses.bsb: My flesh and my heart may fail, but God is the strength of my heart and my portion forever.

verses.kjv: For, lo, they that are far from thee shall perish: thou hast destroyed all them that go a whoring from thee.
verses.bsb: Those far from You will surely perish; You destroy all who are unfaithful to You.

verses.kjv: But it is good for me to draw near to God: I have put my trust in the Lord God, that I may declare all thy works.
verses.bsb: But as for me, it is good to draw near to God. I have made the Lord GOD my refuge, that I may proclaim all Your works.

verses.kjv: O GOD, why hast thou cast us off for ever? why doth thine anger smoke against the sheep of thy pasture?
verses.bsb: Why have You rejected us forever, O God? Why does Your anger smolder against the sheep of Your pasture?

verses.kjv: Remember thy congregation, which thou hast purchased of old; the rod of thine inheritance, which thou hast redeemed; this mount Zion, wherein thou hast dwelt.
verses.bsb: Remember Your congregation, which You purchased long ago and redeemed as the tribe of Your inheritance— Mount Zion where You dwell.

verses.kjv: Lift up thy feet unto the perpetual desolations; even all that the enemy hath done wickedly in the sanctuary.
verses.bsb: Turn Your steps to the everlasting ruins, to everything in the sanctuary the enemy has destroyed.

verses.kjv: Thine enemies roar in the midst of thy congregations; they set up their ensigns for signs.
verses.bsb: Your foes have roared within Your meeting place; they have unfurled their banners as signs,

verses.kjv: A man was famous according as he had lifted up axes upon the thick trees.
verses.bsb: like men wielding axes in a thicket of trees

verses.kjv: But now they break down the carved work thereof at once with axes and hammers.
verses.bsb: and smashing all the carvings with hatchets and picks.

verses.kjv: They have cast fire into thy sanctuary, they have defiled by casting down the dwelling place of thy name to the ground.
verses.bsb: They have burned Your sanctuary to the ground; they have defiled the dwelling place of Your Name.

verses.kjv: They said in their hearts, Let us destroy them together: they have burned up all the synagogues of God in the land.
verses.bsb: They said in their hearts, “We will crush them completely.” They burned down every place where God met us in the land.

verses.kjv: We see not our signs: there is no more any prophet: neither is there among us any that knoweth how long.
verses.bsb: There are no signs for us to see. There is no longer any prophet. And none of us knows how long this will last.

verses.kjv: O God, how long shall the adversary reproach? shall the enemy blaspheme thy name for ever?
verses.bsb: How long, O God, will the enemy taunt You? Will the foe revile Your name forever?

verses.kjv: Why withdrawest thou thy hand, even thy right hand? pluck it out of thy bosom.
verses.bsb: Why do You withdraw Your strong right hand? Stretch it out to destroy them!

verses.kjv: For God is my King of old, working salvation in the midst of the earth.
verses.bsb: Yet God is my King from ancient times, working salvation on the earth.

verses.kjv: Thou didst divide the sea by thy strength: thou brakest the heads of the dragons in the waters.
verses.bsb: You divided the sea by Your strength; You smashed the heads of the dragons of the sea;

verses.kjv: Thou brakest the heads of leviathan in pieces, and gavest him to be meat to the people inhabiting the wilderness.
verses.bsb: You crushed the heads of Leviathan; You fed him to the creatures of the desert.

verses.kjv: Thou didst cleave the fountain and the flood: thou driedst up mighty rivers.
verses.bsb: You broke open the fountain and the flood; You dried up the ever-flowing rivers.

verses.kjv: The day is thine, the night also is thine: thou hast prepared the light and the sun.
verses.bsb: The day is Yours, and also the night; You established the moon and the sun.

verses.kjv: Thou hast set all the borders of the earth: thou hast made summer and winter.
verses.bsb: You set all the boundaries of the earth; You made the summer and winter.

verses.kjv: Remember this, that the enemy hath reproached, O Lord, and that the foolish people have blasphemed thy name.
verses.bsb: Remember how the enemy has mocked You, O LORD, how a foolish people has spurned Your name.

verses.kjv: O deliver not the soul of thy turtledove unto the multitude of the wicked: forget not the congregation of thy poor for ever.
verses.bsb: Do not deliver the soul of Your dove to beasts; do not forget the lives of Your afflicted forever.

verses.kjv: Have respect unto the covenant: for the dark places of the earth are full of the habitations of cruelty.
verses.bsb: Consider Your covenant, for haunts of violence fill the dark places of the land.

verses.kjv: O let not the oppressed return ashamed: let the poor and needy praise thy name.
verses.bsb: Do not let the oppressed retreat in shame; may the poor and needy praise Your name.

verses.kjv: Arise, O God, plead thine own cause: remember how the foolish man reproacheth thee daily.
verses.bsb: Rise up, O God; defend Your cause! Remember how the fool mocks You all day long.

verses.kjv: Forget not the voice of thine enemies: the tumult of those that rise up against thee increaseth continually.
verses.bsb: Do not disregard the clamor of Your adversaries, the uproar of Your enemies that ascends continually.

verses.kjv: UNTO thee, O God, do we give thanks, unto thee do we give thanks: for that thy name is near thy wondrous works declare.
verses.bsb: We give thanks to You, O God; we give thanks, for Your Name is near. The people declare Your wondrous works.

verses.kjv: When I shall receive the congregation I will judge uprightly.
verses.bsb: “When I choose a time, I will judge fairly.

verses.kjv: The earth and all the inhabitants thereof are dissolved: I bear up the pillars of it. Selah.
verses.bsb: When the earth and all its dwellers quake, it is I who bear up its pillars. Selah

verses.kjv: I said unto the fools, Deal not foolishly: and to the wicked, Lift not up the horn:
verses.bsb: I say to the proud, ‘Do not boast,’ and to the wicked, ‘Do not lift up your horn.

verses.kjv: Lift not up your horn on high: speak not with a stiff neck.
verses.bsb: Do not lift up your horn against heaven or speak with an outstretched neck.’”

verses.kjv: For promotion cometh neither from the east, nor from the west, nor from the south.
verses.bsb: For exaltation comes neither from east nor west, nor out of the desert,

verses.kjv: But God is the judge: he putteth down one, and setteth up another.
verses.bsb: but it is God who judges; He brings down one and exalts another.

verses.kjv: For in the hand of the Lord there is a cup, and the wine is red; it is full of mixture; and he poureth out of the same: but the dregs thereof, all the wicked of the earth shall wring them out, and drink them.
verses.bsb: For a cup is in the hand of the LORD, full of foaming wine mixed with spices. He pours from His cup, and all the wicked of the earth drink it down to the dregs.

verses.kjv: But I will declare for ever; I will sing praises to the God of Jacob.
verses.bsb: But I will proclaim Him forever; I will sing praise to the God of Jacob.

verses.kjv: All the horns of the wicked also will I cut off; but the horns of the righteous shall be exalted.
verses.bsb: “All the horns of the wicked I will cut off, but the horns of the righteous will be exalted.”

verses.kjv: IN Judah is God known: his name is great in Israel.
verses.bsb: God is known in Judah; His name is great in Israel.
