verses.kjv: In Salem also is his tabernacle, and his dwelling place in Zion.
verses.bsb: His tent is in Salem, His dwelling place in Zion.

verses.kjv: There brake he the arrows of the bow, the shield, and the sword, and the battle. Selah.
verses.bsb: There He shattered the flaming arrows, the shield and sword and weapons of war. Selah

verses.kjv: Thou art more glorious and excellent than the mountains of prey.
verses.bsb: You are resplendent with light, more majestic than mountains filled with game.

verses.kjv: The stouthearted are spoiled, they have slept their sleep: and none of the men of might have found their hands.
verses.bsb: The valiant lie plundered; they sleep their last sleep. No men of might could lift a hand.

verses.kjv: At thy rebuke, O God of Jacob, both the chariot and horse are cast into a dead sleep.
verses.bsb: At Your rebuke, O God of Jacob, both horse and rider lie stunned.

verses.kjv: Thou, even thou, art to be feared: and who may stand in thy sight when once thou art angry?
verses.bsb: You alone are to be feared. When You are angry, who can stand before You?

verses.kjv: Thou didst cause judgment to be heard from heaven; the earth feared, and was still,
verses.bsb: From heaven You pronounced judgment, and the earth feared and was still

verses.kjv: When God arose to judgment, to save all the meek of the earth. Selah.
verses.bsb: when God rose up to judge, to save all the lowly of the earth. Selah

verses.kjv: Surely the wrath of man shall praise thee: the remainder of wrath shalt thou restrain.
verses.bsb: Even the wrath of man shall praise You; with the survivors of wrath You will clothe Yourself.

verses.kjv: Vow, and pay unto the Lord your God: let all that be round about him bring presents unto him that ought to be feared.
verses.bsb: Make and fulfill your vows to the LORD your God; let all the neighboring lands bring tribute to Him who is to be feared.

verses.kjv: He shall cut off the spirit of princes: he is terrible to the kings of the earth.
verses.bsb: He breaks the spirits of princes; He is feared by the kings of the earth.

verses.kjv: I CRIED unto God with my voice, even unto God with my voice; and he gave ear unto me.
verses.bsb: I cried out to God; I cried aloud to God to hear me.

verses.kjv: In the day of my trouble I sought the Lord: my sore ran in the night, and ceased not: my soul refused to be comforted.
verses.bsb: In the day of trouble I sought the Lord; through the night my outstretched hands did not grow weary; my soul refused to be comforted.

verses.kjv: I remembered God, and was troubled: I complained, and my spirit was overwhelmed. Selah.
verses.bsb: I remembered You, O God, and I groaned; I mused and my spirit grew faint. Selah

verses.kjv: Thou holdest mine eyes waking: I am so troubled that I cannot speak.
verses.bsb: You have kept my eyes from closing; I am too troubled to speak.

verses.kjv: I have considered the days of old, the years of ancient times.
verses.bsb: I considered the days of old, the years long in the past.

verses.kjv: I call to remembrance my song in the night: I commune with mine own heart: and my spirit made diligent search.
verses.bsb: At night I remembered my song; in my heart I mused, and my spirit pondered:

verses.kjv: Will the Lord cast off for ever? and will he be favourable no more?
verses.bsb: “Will the Lord spurn us forever and never show His favor again?

verses.kjv: Is his mercy clean gone for ever? doth his promise fail for evermore?
verses.bsb: Is His loving devotion gone forever? Has His promise failed for all time?

verses.kjv: Hath God forgotten to be gracious? hath he in anger shut up his tender mercies? Selah.
verses.bsb: Has God forgotten to be gracious? Has His anger shut off His compassion?” Selah

verses.kjv: And I said, This is my infirmity: but I will remember the years of the right hand of the most High.
verses.bsb: So I said, “I am grieved that the right hand of the Most High has changed.”

verses.kjv: I will remember the works of the Lord: surely I will remember thy wonders of old.
verses.bsb: I will remember the works of the LORD; yes, I will remember Your wonders of old.

verses.kjv: I will meditate also of all thy work, and talk of thy doings.
verses.bsb: I will reflect on all You have done and ponder Your mighty deeds.

verses.kjv: Thy way, O God, is in the sanctuary: who is so great a God as our God?
verses.bsb: Your way, O God, is holy. What god is so great as our God?

verses.kjv: Thou art the God that doest wonders: thou hast declared thy strength among the people.
verses.bsb: You are the God who works wonders; You display Your strength among the peoples.

verses.kjv: Thou hast with thine arm redeemed thy people, the sons of Jacob and Joseph. Selah.
verses.bsb: With power You redeemed Your people, the sons of Jacob and Joseph. Selah

verses.kjv: The waters saw thee, O God, the waters saw thee; they were afraid: the depths also were troubled.
verses.bsb: The waters saw You, O God; the waters saw You and swirled; even the depths were shaken.

verses.kjv: The clouds poured out water: the skies sent out a sound: thine arrows also went abroad.
verses.bsb: The clouds poured down water; the skies resounded with thunder; Your arrows flashed back and forth.

verses.kjv: The voice of thy thunder was in the heaven: the lightnings lightened the world: the earth trembled and shook.
verses.bsb: Your thunder resounded in the whirlwind; the lightning lit up the world; the earth trembled and quaked.

verses.kjv: Thy way is in the sea, and thy path in the great waters, and thy footsteps are not known.
verses.bsb: Your path led through the sea, Your way through the mighty waters, but Your footprints were not to be found.

verses.kjv: Thou leddest thy people like a flock by the hand of Moses and Aaron.
verses.bsb: You led Your people like a flock by the hand of Moses and Aaron.

verses.kjv: GIVE ear, O my people, to my law: incline your ears to the words of my mouth.
verses.bsb: Give ear, O my people, to my instruction; listen to the words of my mouth.

verses.kjv: I will open my mouth in a parable: I will utter dark sayings of old:
verses.bsb: I will open my mouth in parables; I will utter things hidden from the beginning,

verses.kjv: Which we have heard and known, and our fathers have told us.
verses.bsb: that we have heard and known and our fathers have relayed to us.

verses.kjv: We will not hide them from their children, shewing to the generation to come the praises of the Lord, and his strength, and his wonderful works that he hath done.
verses.bsb: We will not hide them from their children, but will declare to the next generation the praises of the LORD and His might, and the wonders He has performed.

verses.kjv: For he established a testimony in Jacob, and appointed a law in Israel, which he commanded our fathers, that they should make them known to their children:
verses.bsb: For He established a testimony in Jacob and appointed a law in Israel, which He commanded our fathers to teach to their children,

verses.kjv: That the generation to come might know them, even the children which should be born; who should arise and declare them to their children:
verses.bsb: that the coming generation would know them— even children yet to be born— to arise and tell their own children

verses.kjv: That they might set their hope in God, and not forget the works of God, but keep his commandments:
verses.bsb: that they should put their confidence in God, not forgetting His works, but keeping His commandments.

verses.kjv: And might not be as their fathers, a stubborn and rebellious generation; a generation that set not their heart aright, and whose spirit was not stedfast with God.
verses.bsb: Then they will not be like their fathers, a stubborn and rebellious generation, whose heart was not loyal, whose spirit was not faithful to God.

verses.kjv: The children of Ephraim, being armed, and carrying bows, turned back in the day of battle.
verses.bsb: The archers of Ephraim turned back on the day of battle.

verses.kjv: They kept not the covenant of God, and refused to walk in his law;
verses.bsb: They failed to keep God’s covenant and refused to live by His law.

verses.kjv: And forgat his works, and his wonders that he had shewed them.
verses.bsb: They forgot what He had done, the wonders He had shown them.

verses.kjv: Marvellous things did he in the sight of their fathers, in the land of Egypt, in the field of Zoan.
verses.bsb: He worked wonders before their fathers in the land of Egypt, in the region of Zoan.

verses.kjv: He divided the sea, and caused them to pass through; and he made the waters to stand as an heap.
verses.bsb: He split the sea and brought them through; He set the waters upright like a wall.

verses.kjv: In the daytime also he led them with a cloud, and all the night with a light of fire.
verses.bsb: He led them with a cloud by day and with a light of fire all night.

verses.kjv: He clave the rocks in the wilderness, and gave them drink as out of the great depths.
verses.bsb: He split the rocks in the wilderness and gave them drink as abundant as the seas.

verses.kjv: He brought streams also out of the rock, and caused waters to run down like rivers.
verses.bsb: He brought streams from the stone and made water flow down like rivers.

verses.kjv: And they sinned yet more against him by provoking the most High in the wilderness.
verses.bsb: But they continued to sin against Him, rebelling in the desert against the Most High.

verses.kjv: And they tempted God in their heart by asking meat for their lust.
verses.bsb: They willfully tested God by demanding the food they craved.

verses.kjv: Yea, they spake against God; they said, Can God furnish a table in the wilderness?
verses.bsb: They spoke against God, saying, “Can God really prepare a table in the wilderness?

verses.kjv: Behold, he smote the rock, that the waters gushed out, and the streams overflowed; can he give bread also? can he provide flesh for his people?
verses.bsb: When He struck the rock, water gushed out and torrents raged. But can He also give bread or supply His people with meat?”

verses.kjv: Therefore the Lord heard this, and was wroth: so a fire was kindled against Jacob, and anger also came up against Israel;
verses.bsb: Therefore the LORD heard and was filled with wrath; so a fire was kindled against Jacob, and His anger flared against Israel,

verses.kjv: Because they believed not in God, and trusted not in his salvation:
verses.bsb: because they did not believe God or rely on His salvation.

verses.kjv: Though he had commanded the clouds from above, and opened the doors of heaven,
verses.bsb: Yet He commanded the clouds above and opened the doors of the heavens.

verses.kjv: And had rained down manna upon them to eat, and had given them of the corn of heaven.
verses.bsb: He rained down manna for them to eat; He gave them grain from heaven.

verses.kjv: Man did eat angels’ food: he sent them meat to the full.
verses.bsb: Man ate the bread of angels; He sent them food in abundance.

verses.kjv: He caused an east wind to blow in the heaven: and by his power he brought in the south wind.
verses.bsb: He stirred the east wind from the heavens and drove the south wind by His might.

verses.kjv: He rained flesh also upon them as dust, and feathered fowls like as the sand of the sea:
verses.bsb: He rained meat on them like dust, and winged birds like the sand of the sea.

verses.kjv: And he let it fall in the midst of their camp, round about their habitations.
verses.bsb: He felled them in the midst of their camp, all around their dwellings.

verses.kjv: So they did eat, and were well filled: for he gave them their own desire;
verses.bsb: So they ate and were well filled, for He gave them what they craved.

verses.kjv: They were not estranged from their lust. But while their meat was yet in their mouths,
verses.bsb: Yet before they had filled their desire, with the food still in their mouths,

verses.kjv: The wrath of God came upon them, and slew the fattest of them, and smote down the chosen men of Israel.
verses.bsb: God’s anger flared against them, and He put to death their strongest and subdued the young men of Israel.

verses.kjv: For all this they sinned still, and believed not for his wondrous works.
verses.bsb: In spite of all this, they kept on sinning; despite His wonderful works, they did not believe.

verses.kjv: Therefore their days did he consume in vanity, and their years in trouble.
verses.bsb: So He ended their days in futility, and their years in sudden terror.

verses.kjv: When he slew them, then they sought him: and they returned and inquired early after God.
verses.bsb: When He slew them, they would seek Him; they repented and searched for God.

verses.kjv: And they remembered that God was their rock, and the high God their redeemer.
verses.bsb: And they remembered that God was their Rock, that God Most High was their Redeemer.

verses.kjv: Nevertheless they did flatter him with their mouth, and they lied unto him with their tongues.
verses.bsb: But they deceived Him with their mouths, and lied to Him with their tongues.

verses.kjv: For their heart was not right with him, neither were they stedfast in his covenant.
verses.bsb: Their hearts were disloyal to Him, and they were unfaithful to His covenant.

verses.kjv: But he, being full of compassion, forgave their iniquity, and destroyed them not: yea, many a time turned he his anger away, and did not stir up all his wrath.
verses.bsb: And yet He was compassionate; He forgave their iniquity and did not destroy them. He often restrained His anger and did not unleash His full wrath.

verses.kjv: For he remembered that they were but flesh; a wind that passeth away, and cometh not again.
verses.bsb: He remembered that they were but flesh, a passing breeze that does not return.

verses.kjv: How oft did they provoke him in the wilderness, and grieve him in the desert!
verses.bsb: How often they disobeyed Him in the wilderness and grieved Him in the desert!

verses.kjv: Yea, they turned back and tempted God, and limited the Holy One of Israel.
verses.bsb: Again and again they tested God and provoked the Holy One of Israel.

verses.kjv: They remembered not his hand, nor the day when he delivered them from the enemy.
verses.bsb: They did not remember His power— the day He redeemed them from the adversary,

verses.kjv: How he had wrought his signs in Egypt, and his wonders in the field of Zoan:
verses.bsb: when He performed His signs in Egypt and His wonders in the fields of Zoan.

verses.kjv: And had turned their rivers into blood; and their floods, that they could not drink.
verses.bsb: He turned their rivers to blood, and from their streams they could not drink.

verses.kjv: He sent divers sorts of flies among them, which devoured them; and frogs, which destroyed them.
verses.bsb: He sent swarms of flies that devoured them, and frogs that devastated them.

verses.kjv: He gave also their increase unto the caterpiller, and their labour unto the locust.
verses.bsb: He gave their crops to the grasshopper, the fruit of their labor to the locust.

verses.kjv: He destroyed their vines with hail, and their sycomore trees with frost.
verses.bsb: He killed their vines with hailstones and their sycamore-figs with sleet.

verses.kjv: He gave up their cattle also to the hail, and their flocks to hot thunderbolts.
verses.bsb: He abandoned their cattle to the hail and their livestock to bolts of lightning.

verses.kjv: He cast upon them the fierceness of his anger, wrath, and indignation, and trouble, by sending evil angels among them.
verses.bsb: He unleashed His fury against them, wrath, indignation, and calamity— a band of destroying angels.

verses.kjv: He made a way to his anger; he spared not their soul from death, but gave their life over to the pestilence;
verses.bsb: He cleared a path for His anger; He did not spare them from death but delivered their lives to the plague.

verses.kjv: And smote all the firstborn in Egypt; the chief of their strength in the tabernacles of Ham:
verses.bsb: He struck all the firstborn of Egypt, the virility in the tents of Ham.

verses.kjv: But made his own people to go forth like sheep, and guided them in the wilderness like a flock.
verses.bsb: He led out His people like sheep and guided them like a flock in the wilderness.

verses.kjv: And he led them on safely, so that they feared not: but the sea overwhelmed their enemies.
verses.bsb: He led them safely, so they did not fear, but the sea engulfed their enemies.

verses.kjv: And he brought them to the border of his sanctuary, even to this mountain, which his right hand had purchased.
verses.bsb: He brought them to His holy land, to the mountain His right hand had acquired.

verses.kjv: He cast out the heathen also before them, and divided them an inheritance by line, and made the tribes of Israel to dwell in their tents.
verses.bsb: He drove out nations before them and apportioned their inheritance; He settled the tribes of Israel in their tents.

verses.kjv: Yet they tempted and provoked the most high God, and kept not his testimonies:
verses.bsb: But they tested and disobeyed God Most High, for they did not keep His decrees.

verses.kjv: But turned back, and dealt unfaithfully like their fathers: they were turned aside like a deceitful bow.
verses.bsb: They turned back and were faithless like their fathers, twisted like a faulty bow.

verses.kjv: For they provoked him to anger with their high places, and moved him to jealousy with their graven images.
verses.bsb: They enraged Him with their high places and provoked His jealousy with their idols.

verses.kjv: When God heard this, he was wroth, and greatly abhorred Israel:
verses.bsb: On hearing it, God was furious and rejected Israel completely.

verses.kjv: So that he forsook the tabernacle of Shiloh, the tent which he placed among men;
verses.bsb: He abandoned the tabernacle of Shiloh, the tent He had pitched among men.

verses.kjv: And delivered his strength into captivity, and his glory into the enemy’s hand.
verses.bsb: He delivered His strength to captivity, and His splendor to the hand of the adversary.

verses.kjv: He gave his people over also unto the sword; and was wroth with his inheritance.
verses.bsb: He surrendered His people to the sword because He was enraged by His heritage.

verses.kjv: The fire consumed their young men; and their maidens were not given to marriage.
verses.bsb: Fire consumed His young men, and their maidens were left without wedding songs.

verses.kjv: Their priests fell by the sword; and their widows made no lamentation.
verses.bsb: His priests fell by the sword, but their widows could not lament.

verses.kjv: Then the Lord awaked as one out of sleep, and like a mighty man that shouteth by reason of wine.
verses.bsb: Then the Lord awoke as from sleep, like a mighty warrior overcome by wine.

verses.kjv: And he smote his enemies in the hinder parts: he put them to a perpetual reproach.
verses.bsb: He beat back His foes; He put them to everlasting shame.

verses.kjv: Moreover he refused the tabernacle of Joseph, and chose not the tribe of Ephraim:
verses.bsb: He rejected the tent of Joseph and refused the tribe of Ephraim.

verses.kjv: But chose the tribe of Judah, the mount Zion which he loved.
verses.bsb: But He chose the tribe of Judah, Mount Zion, which He loved.

verses.kjv: And he built his sanctuary like high palaces, like the earth which he hath established for ever.
verses.bsb: He built His sanctuary like the heights, like the earth He has established forever.

verses.kjv: He chose David also his servant, and took him from the sheepfolds:
verses.bsb: He chose David His servant and took him from the sheepfolds;

verses.kjv: From following the ewes great with young he brought him to feed Jacob his people, and Israel his inheritance.
verses.bsb: from tending the ewes He brought him to be shepherd of His people Jacob, of Israel His inheritance.

verses.kjv: So he fed them according to the integrity of his heart; and guided them by the skilfulness of his hands.
verses.bsb: So David shepherded them with integrity of heart and guided them with skillful hands.

verses.kjv: O GOD, the heathen are come into thine inheritance; thy holy temple have they defiled; they have laid Jerusalem on heaps.
verses.bsb: The nations, O God, have invaded Your inheritance; they have defiled Your holy temple and reduced Jerusalem to rubble.

verses.kjv: The dead bodies of thy servants have they given to be meat unto the fowls of the heaven, the flesh of thy saints unto the beasts of the earth.
verses.bsb: They have given the corpses of Your servants as food to the birds of the air, the flesh of Your saints to the beasts of the earth.

verses.kjv: Their blood have they shed like water round about Jerusalem; and there was none to bury them.
verses.bsb: They have poured out their blood like water all around Jerusalem, and there is no one to bury the dead.

verses.kjv: We are become a reproach to our neighbours, a scorn and derision to them that are round about us.
verses.bsb: We have become a reproach to our neighbors, a scorn and derision to those around us.

verses.kjv: How long, Lord? wilt thou be angry for ever? shall thy jealousy burn like fire?
verses.bsb: How long, O LORD? Will You be angry forever? Will Your jealousy burn like fire?

verses.kjv: Pour out thy wrath upon the heathen that have not known thee, and upon the kingdoms that have not called upon thy name.
verses.bsb: Pour out Your wrath on the nations that do not acknowledge You, on the kingdoms that refuse to call on Your name,

verses.kjv: For they have devoured Jacob, and laid waste his dwelling place.
verses.bsb: for they have devoured Jacob and devastated his homeland.

verses.kjv: O remember not against us former iniquities: let thy tender mercies speedily prevent us: for we are brought very low.
verses.bsb: Do not hold past sins against us; let Your compassion come quickly, for we are brought low.

verses.kjv: Help us, O God of our salvation, for the glory of thy name: and deliver us, and purge away our sins, for thy name’s sake.
verses.bsb: Help us, O God of our salvation, for the glory of Your name; deliver us and atone for our sins, for the sake of Your name.

verses.kjv: Wherefore should the heathen say, Where is their God? let him be known among the heathen in our sight by the revenging of the blood of thy servants which is shed.
verses.bsb: Why should the nations ask, “Where is their God?” Before our eyes, make known among the nations Your vengeance for the bloodshed of Your servants.

verses.kjv: Let the sighing of the prisoner come before thee; according to the greatness of thy power preserve thou those that are appointed to die;
verses.bsb: May the groans of the captives reach You; by the strength of Your arm preserve those condemned to death.

verses.kjv: And render unto our neighbours sevenfold into their bosom their reproach, wherewith they have reproached thee, O Lord.
verses.bsb: Pay back into the laps of our neighbors sevenfold the reproach they hurled at You, O Lord.

verses.kjv: So we thy people and sheep of thy pasture will give thee thanks for ever: we will shew forth thy praise to all generations.
verses.bsb: Then we Your people, the sheep of Your pasture, will thank You forever; from generation to generation we will declare Your praise.

verses.kjv: GIVE ear, O Shepherd of Israel, thou that leadest Joseph like a flock; thou that dwellest between the cherubims, shine forth.
verses.bsb: Hear us, O Shepherd of Israel, who leads Joseph like a flock; You who sit enthroned between the cherubim, shine forth

verses.kjv: Before Ephraim and Benjamin and Manasseh stir up thy strength, and come and save us.
verses.bsb: before Ephraim, Benjamin, and Manasseh. Rally Your mighty power and come to save us.

verses.kjv: Turn us again, O God, and cause thy face to shine; and we shall be saved.
verses.bsb: Restore us, O God, and cause Your face to shine upon us, that we may be saved.

verses.kjv: O Lord God of hosts, how long wilt thou be angry against the prayer of thy people?
verses.bsb: O LORD God of Hosts, how long will Your anger smolder against the prayers of Your people?

verses.kjv: Thou feedest them with the bread of tears; and givest them tears to drink in great measure.
verses.bsb: You fed them with the bread of tears and made them drink the full measure of their tears.

verses.kjv: Thou makest us a strife unto our neighbours: and our enemies laugh among themselves.
verses.bsb: You make us contend with our neighbors; our enemies mock us.

verses.kjv: Turn us again, O God of hosts, and cause thy face to shine; and we shall be saved.
verses.bsb: Restore us, O God of Hosts, and cause Your face to shine upon us, that we may be saved.

verses.kjv: Thou hast brought a vine out of Egypt: thou hast cast out the heathen, and planted it.
verses.bsb: You uprooted a vine from Egypt; You drove out the nations and transplanted it.

verses.kjv: Thou preparedst room before it, and didst cause it to take deep root, and it filled the land.
verses.bsb: You cleared the ground for it, and it took root and filled the land.

verses.kjv: The hills were covered with the shadow of it, and the boughs thereof were like the goodly cedars.
verses.bsb: The mountains were covered by its shade, and the mighty cedars with its branches.

verses.kjv: She sent out her boughs unto the sea, and her branches unto the river.
verses.bsb: It sent out its branches to the Sea, and its shoots toward the River.

verses.kjv: Why hast thou then broken down her hedges, so that all they which pass by the way do pluck her?
verses.bsb: Why have You broken down its walls, so that all who pass by pick its fruit?

verses.kjv: The boar out of the wood doth waste it, and the wild beast of the field doth devour it.
verses.bsb: The boar from the forest ravages it, and the creatures of the field feed upon it.

verses.kjv: Return, we beseech thee, O God of hosts: look down from heaven, and behold, and visit this vine;
verses.bsb: Return, O God of Hosts, we pray! Look down from heaven and see! Attend to this vine—

verses.kjv: And the vineyard which thy right hand hath planted, and the branch that thou madest strong for thyself.
verses.bsb: the root Your right hand has planted, the son You have raised up for Yourself.

verses.kjv: It is burned with fire, it is cut down: they perish at the rebuke of thy countenance.
verses.bsb: Your vine has been cut down and burned; they perish at the rebuke of Your countenance.

verses.kjv: Let thy hand be upon the man of thy right hand, upon the son of man whom thou madest strong for thyself.
verses.bsb: Let Your hand be upon the man at Your right hand, on the son of man You have raised up for Yourself.

verses.kjv: So will not we go back from thee: quicken us, and we will call upon thy name.
verses.bsb: Then we will not turn away from You; revive us, and we will call on Your name.

verses.kjv: Turn us again, O Lord God of hosts, cause thy face to shine; and we shall be saved.
verses.bsb: Restore us, O LORD God of Hosts; cause Your face to shine upon us, that we may be saved.

verses.kjv: SING aloud unto God our strength: make a joyful noise unto the God of Jacob.
verses.bsb: Sing for joy to God our strength; make a joyful noise to the God of Jacob.

verses.kjv: Take a psalm, and bring hither the timbrel, the pleasant harp with the psaltery.
verses.bsb: Lift up a song, strike the tambourine, play the sweet-sounding harp and lyre.

verses.kjv: Blow up the trumpet in the new moon, in the time appointed, on our solemn feast day.
verses.bsb: Sound the ram’s horn at the New Moon, and at the full moon on the day of our Feast.

verses.kjv: For this was a statute for Israel, and a law of the God of Jacob.
verses.bsb: For this is a statute for Israel, an ordinance of the God of Jacob.

verses.kjv: This he ordained in Joseph for a testimony, when he went out through the land of Egypt: where I heard a language that I understood not.
verses.bsb: He ordained it as a testimony for Joseph when he went out over the land of Egypt, where I heard an unfamiliar language:

verses.kjv: I removed his shoulder from the burden: his hands were delivered from the pots.
verses.bsb: “I relieved his shoulder of the burden; his hands were freed from the basket.

verses.kjv: Thou calledst in trouble, and I delivered thee; I answered thee in the secret place of thunder: I proved thee at the waters of Meribah. Selah.
verses.bsb: You called out in distress, and I rescued you; I answered you from the cloud of thunder; I tested you at the waters of Meribah. Selah

verses.kjv: Hear, O my people, and I will testify unto thee: O Israel, if thou wilt hearken unto me;
verses.bsb: Hear, O My people, and I will warn you: O Israel, if only you would listen to Me!

verses.kjv: There shall no strange god be in thee; neither shalt thou worship any strange god.
verses.bsb: There must be no strange god among you, nor shall you bow to a foreign god.

verses.kjv: I am the Lord thy God, which brought thee out of the land of Egypt: open thy mouth wide, and I will fill it.
verses.bsb: I am the LORD your God, who brought you up out of Egypt. Open wide your mouth, and I will fill it.

verses.kjv: But my people would not hearken to my voice; and Israel would none of me.
verses.bsb: But My people would not listen to Me, and Israel would not obey Me.

verses.kjv: So I gave them up unto their own hearts’ lust: and they walked in their own counsels.
verses.bsb: So I gave them up to their stubborn hearts to follow their own devices.

verses.kjv: Oh that my people had hearkened unto me, and Israel had walked in my ways!
verses.bsb: If only My people would listen to Me, if Israel would follow My ways,

verses.kjv: I should soon have subdued their enemies, and turned my hand against their adversaries.
verses.bsb: how soon I would subdue their enemies and turn My hand against their foes!

verses.kjv: The haters of the Lord should have submitted themselves unto him: but their time should have endured for ever.
verses.bsb: Those who hate the LORD would feign obedience, and their doom would last forever.

verses.kjv: He should have fed them also with the finest of the wheat: and with honey out of the rock should I have satisfied thee.
verses.bsb: But I would feed you the finest wheat; with honey from the rock I would satisfy you.”

verses.kjv: GOD standeth in the congregation of the mighty; he judgeth among the gods.
verses.bsb: God presides in the divine assembly; He renders judgment among the gods:

verses.kjv: How long will ye judge unjustly, and accept the persons of the wicked? Selah.
verses.bsb: “How long will you judge unjustly and show partiality to the wicked? Selah

verses.kjv: Defend the poor and fatherless: do justice to the afflicted and needy.
verses.bsb: Defend the cause of the weak and fatherless; uphold the rights of the afflicted and oppressed.

verses.kjv: Deliver the poor and needy: rid them out of the hand of the wicked.
verses.bsb: Rescue the weak and needy; save them from the hand of the wicked.

verses.kjv: They know not, neither will they understand; they walk on in darkness: all the foundations of the earth are out of course.
verses.bsb: They do not know or understand; they wander in the darkness; all the foundations of the earth are shaken.

verses.kjv: I have said, Ye are gods; and all of you are children of the most High.
verses.bsb: I have said, ‘You are gods; you are all sons of the Most High.’

verses.kjv: But ye shall die like men, and fall like one of the princes.
verses.bsb: But like mortals you will die, and like rulers you will fall.”

verses.kjv: Arise, O God, judge the earth: for thou shalt inherit all nations.
verses.bsb: Arise, O God, judge the earth, for all the nations are Your inheritance.

verses.kjv: KEEP not thou silence, O God: hold not thy peace, and be not still, O God.
verses.bsb: O God, be not silent; be not speechless; be not still, O God.

verses.kjv: For, lo, thine enemies make a tumult: and they that hate thee have lifted up the head.
verses.bsb: See how Your enemies rage, how Your foes have reared their heads.

verses.kjv: They have taken crafty counsel against thy people, and consulted against thy hidden ones.
verses.bsb: With cunning they scheme against Your people and conspire against those You cherish,

verses.kjv: They have said, Come, and let us cut them off from being a nation; that the name of Israel may be no more in remembrance.
verses.bsb: saying, “Come, let us erase them as a nation; may the name of Israel be remembered no more.”

verses.kjv: For they have consulted together with one consent: they are confederate against thee:
verses.bsb: For with one mind they plot together, they form an alliance against You—

verses.kjv: The tabernacles of Edom, and the Ishmaelites; of Moab, and the Hagarenes;
verses.bsb: the tents of Edom and the Ishmaelites, of Moab and the Hagrites,

verses.kjv: Gebal, and Ammon, and Amalek; the Philistines with the inhabitants of Tyre;
verses.bsb: of Gebal, Ammon, and Amalek, of Philistia with the people of Tyre.

verses.kjv: Assur also is joined with them: they have holpen the children of Lot. Selah.
verses.bsb: Even Assyria has joined them, lending strength to the sons of Lot. Selah

verses.kjv: Do unto them as unto the Midianites; as to Sisera, as to Jabin, at the brook of Kison:
verses.bsb: Do to them as You did to Midian, as to Sisera and Jabin at the River Kishon,

verses.kjv: Which perished at En-dor: they became as dung for the earth.
verses.bsb: who perished at Endor and became like dung on the ground.

verses.kjv: Make their nobles like Oreb, and like Zeeb: yea, all their princes as Zebah, and as Zalmunna:
verses.bsb: Make their nobles like Oreb and Zeeb, and all their princes like Zebah and Zalmunna,

verses.kjv: Who said, Let us take to ourselves the houses of God in possession.
verses.bsb: who said, “Let us possess for ourselves the pastures of God.”

verses.kjv: O my God, make them like a wheel; as the stubble before the wind.
verses.bsb: Make them like tumbleweed, O my God, like chaff before the wind.

verses.kjv: As the fire burneth a wood, and as the flame setteth the mountains on fire;
verses.bsb: As fire consumes a forest, as a flame sets the mountains ablaze,

verses.kjv: So persecute them with thy tempest, and make them afraid with thy storm.
verses.bsb: so pursue them with Your tempest, and terrify them with Your storm.

verses.kjv: Fill their faces with shame; that they may seek thy name, O Lord.
verses.bsb: Cover their faces with shame, that they may seek Your name, O LORD.

verses.kjv: Let them be confounded and troubled for ever; yea, let them be put to shame, and perish:
verses.bsb: May they be ever ashamed and terrified; may they perish in disgrace.

verses.kjv: That men may know that thou, whose name alone is JEHOVAH, art the most high over all the earth.
verses.bsb: May they know that You alone, whose name is the LORD, are Most High over all the earth.

verses.kjv: HOW amiable are thy tabernacles, O Lord of hosts!
verses.bsb: How lovely is Your dwelling place, O LORD of Hosts!

verses.kjv: My soul longeth, yea, even fainteth for the courts of the Lord: my heart and my flesh crieth out for the living God.
verses.bsb: My soul longs, even faints, for the courts of the LORD; my heart and my flesh cry out for the living God.

verses.kjv: Yea, the sparrow hath found an house, and the swallow a nest for herself, where she may lay her young, even thine altars, O Lord of hosts, my King, and my God.
verses.bsb: Even the sparrow has found a home, and the swallow a nest for herself, where she places her young near Your altars, O LORD of Hosts, my King and my God.

verses.kjv: Blessed are they that dwell in thy house: they will be still praising thee. Selah.
verses.bsb: How blessed are those who dwell in Your house! They are ever praising You. Selah

verses.kjv: Blessed is the man whose strength is in thee; in whose heart are the ways of them.
verses.bsb: Blessed are those whose strength is in You, whose hearts are set on pilgrimage.

verses.kjv: Who passing through the valley of Baca make it a well; the rain also filleth the pools.
verses.bsb: As they pass through the Valley of Baca, they make it a place of springs; even the autumn rain covers it with pools.

verses.kjv: They go from strength to strength, every one of them in Zion appeareth before God.
verses.bsb: They go from strength to strength, until each appears before God in Zion.

verses.kjv: O Lord God of hosts, hear my prayer: give ear, O God of Jacob. Selah.
verses.bsb: O LORD God of Hosts, hear my prayer; give ear, O God of Jacob. Selah

verses.kjv: Behold, O God our shield, and look upon the face of thine anointed.
verses.bsb: Take notice of our shield, O God, and look with favor on the face of Your anointed.

verses.kjv: For a day in thy courts is better than a thousand. I had rather be a doorkeeper in the house of my God, than to dwell in the tents of wickedness.
verses.bsb: For better is one day in Your courts than a thousand elsewhere. I would rather be a doorkeeper in the house of my God than dwell in the tents of the wicked.

verses.kjv: For the Lord God is a sun and shield: the Lord will give grace and glory: no good thing will he withhold from them that walk uprightly.
verses.bsb: For the LORD God is a sun and a shield; the LORD gives grace and glory; He withholds no good thing from those who walk with integrity.

verses.kjv: O Lord of hosts, blessed is the man that trusteth in thee.
verses.bsb: O LORD of Hosts, how blessed is the man who trusts in You!

verses.kjv: LORD, thou hast been favourable unto thy land: thou hast brought back the captivity of Jacob.
verses.bsb: You showed favor to Your land, O LORD; You restored Jacob from captivity.

verses.kjv: Thou hast forgiven the iniquity of thy people, thou hast covered all their sin. Selah.
verses.bsb: You forgave the iniquity of Your people; You covered all their sin. Selah

verses.kjv: Thou hast taken away all thy wrath: thou hast turned thyself from the fierceness of thine anger.
verses.bsb: You withheld all Your fury; You turned from Your burning anger.

verses.kjv: Turn us, O God of our salvation, and cause thine anger toward us to cease.
verses.bsb: Restore us, O God of our salvation, and put away Your displeasure toward us.

verses.kjv: Wilt thou be angry with us for ever? wilt thou draw out thine anger to all generations?
verses.bsb: Will You be angry with us forever? Will You draw out Your anger to all generations?

verses.kjv: Wilt thou not revive us again: that thy people may rejoice in thee?
verses.bsb: Will You not revive us again, that Your people may rejoice in You?

verses.kjv: Shew us thy mercy, O Lord, and grant us thy salvation.
verses.bsb: Show us Your loving devotion, O LORD, and grant us Your salvation.

verses.kjv: I will hear what God the Lord will speak: for he will speak peace unto his people, and to his saints: but let them not turn again to folly.
verses.bsb: I will listen to what God the LORD will say; for He will surely speak peace to His people and His saints; He will not let them return to folly.

verses.kjv: Surely his salvation is nigh them that fear him; that glory may dwell in our land.
verses.bsb: Surely His salvation is near to those who fear Him, that His glory may dwell in our land.

verses.kjv: Mercy and truth are met together; righteousness and peace have kissed each other.
verses.bsb: Loving devotion and faithfulness have joined together; righteousness and peace have kissed.

verses.kjv: Truth shall spring out of the earth; and righteousness shall look down from heaven.
verses.bsb: Faithfulness sprouts from the earth, and righteousness looks down from heaven.

verses.kjv: Yea, the Lord shall give that which is good; and our land shall yield her increase.
verses.bsb: The LORD will indeed provide what is good, and our land will yield its increase.

verses.kjv: Righteousness shall go before him; and shall set us in the way of his steps.
verses.bsb: Righteousness will go before Him to prepare the way for His steps.

verses.kjv: BOW down thine ear, O Lord, hear me: for I am poor and needy.
verses.bsb: Incline Your ear, O LORD, and answer me, for I am poor and needy.

verses.kjv: Preserve my soul; for I am holy: O thou my God, save thy servant that trusteth in thee.
verses.bsb: Preserve my soul, for I am godly. You are my God; save Your servant who trusts in You.

verses.kjv: Be merciful unto me, O Lord: for I cry unto thee daily.
verses.bsb: Be merciful to me, O Lord, for I call to You all day long.

verses.kjv: Rejoice the soul of thy servant: for unto thee, O Lord, do I lift up my soul.
verses.bsb: Bring joy to Your servant, for to You, O Lord, I lift up my soul.

verses.kjv: For thou, Lord, art good, and ready to forgive; and plenteous in mercy unto all them that call upon thee.
verses.bsb: For You, O Lord, are kind and forgiving, rich in loving devotion to all who call on You.

verses.kjv: Give ear, O Lord, unto my prayer; and attend to the voice of my supplications.
verses.bsb: Hear my prayer, O LORD, and attend to my plea for mercy.

verses.kjv: In the day of my trouble I will call upon thee: for thou wilt answer me.
verses.bsb: In the day of my distress I call on You, because You answer me.

verses.kjv: Among the gods there is none like unto thee, O Lord; neither are there any works like unto thy works.
verses.bsb: O Lord, there is none like You among the gods, nor any works like Yours.

verses.kjv: All nations whom thou hast made shall come and worship before thee, O Lord; and shall glorify thy name.
verses.bsb: All the nations You have made will come and bow before You, O Lord, and they will glorify Your name.

verses.kjv: For thou art great, and doest wondrous things: thou art God alone.
verses.bsb: For You are great and perform wonders; You alone are God.

verses.kjv: Teach me thy way, O Lord; I will walk in thy truth: unite my heart to fear thy name.
verses.bsb: Teach me Your way, O LORD, that I may walk in Your truth. Give me an undivided heart, that I may fear Your name.

verses.kjv: I will praise thee, O Lord my God, with all my heart: and I will glorify thy name for evermore.
verses.bsb: I will praise You, O Lord my God, with all my heart; I will glorify Your name forever.

verses.kjv: For great is thy mercy toward me: and thou hast delivered my soul from the lowest hell.
verses.bsb: For great is Your loving devotion to me; You have delivered me from the depths of Sheol.

verses.kjv: O God, the proud are risen against me, and the assemblies of violent men have sought after my soul; and have not set thee before them.
verses.bsb: The arrogant rise against me, O God; a band of ruthless men seeks my life, with no regard for You.

verses.kjv: But thou, O Lord, art a God full of compassion, and gracious, longsuffering, and plenteous in mercy and truth.
verses.bsb: But You, O Lord, are a compassionate and gracious God, slow to anger, abounding in loving devotion and faithfulness.

verses.kjv: O turn unto me, and have mercy upon me; give thy strength unto thy servant, and save the son of thine handmaid.
verses.bsb: Turn to me and have mercy; grant Your strength to Your servant; save the son of Your maidservant.

verses.kjv: Shew me a token for good; that they which hate me may see it, and be ashamed: because thou, Lord, hast holpen me, and comforted me.
verses.bsb: Show me a sign of Your goodness, that my enemies may see and be ashamed; for You, O LORD, have helped me and comforted me.

verses.kjv: HIS foundation is in the holy mountains.
verses.bsb: He has founded His city on the holy mountains.

verses.kjv: The Lord loveth the gates of Zion more than all the dwellings of Jacob.
verses.bsb: The LORD loves the gates of Zion more than all the dwellings of Jacob.

verses.kjv: Glorious things are spoken of thee, O city of God. Selah.
verses.bsb: Glorious things are ascribed to you, O city of God. Selah

verses.kjv: I will make mention of Rahab and Babylon to them that know me: behold Philistia, and Tyre, with Ethiopia; this man was born there.
verses.bsb: “I will mention Rahab and Babylon among those who know Me— along with Philistia, Tyre, and Cush— when I say, ‘This one was born in Zion.’”

verses.kjv: And of Zion it shall be said, This and that man was born in her: and the highest himself shall establish her.
verses.bsb: And it will be said of Zion: “This one and that one were born in her, and the Most High Himself will establish her.”

verses.kjv: The Lord shall count, when he writeth up the people, that this man was born there. Selah.
verses.bsb: The LORD will record in the register of the peoples: “This one was born in Zion.” Selah

verses.kjv: As well the singers as the players on instruments shall be there: all my springs are in thee.
verses.bsb: Singers and pipers will proclaim, “All my springs of joy are in You.”

verses.kjv: O LORD God of my salvation, I have cried day and night before thee:
verses.bsb: O LORD, the God of my salvation, day and night I cry out before You.

verses.kjv: Let my prayer come before thee: incline thine ear unto my cry;
verses.bsb: May my prayer come before You; incline Your ear to my cry.

verses.kjv: For my soul is full of troubles: and my life draweth nigh unto the grave.
verses.bsb: For my soul is full of troubles, and my life draws near to Sheol.

verses.kjv: I am counted with them that go down into the pit: I am as a man that hath no strength:
verses.bsb: I am counted among those descending to the Pit. I am like a man without strength.

verses.kjv: Free among the dead, like the slain that lie in the grave, whom thou rememberest no more: and they are cut off from thy hand.
verses.bsb: I am forsaken among the dead, like the slain who lie in the grave, whom You remember no more, who are cut off from Your care.

verses.kjv: Thou hast laid me in the lowest pit, in darkness, in the deeps.
verses.bsb: You have laid me in the lowest Pit, in the darkest of the depths.

verses.kjv: Thy wrath lieth hard upon me, and thou hast afflicted me with all thy waves. Selah.
verses.bsb: Your wrath weighs heavily upon me; all Your waves have submerged me. Selah

verses.kjv: Thou hast put away mine acquaintance far from me; thou hast made me an abomination unto them: I am shut up, and I cannot come forth.
verses.bsb: You have removed my friends from me; You have made me repulsive to them; I am confined and cannot escape.

verses.kjv: Mine eye mourneth by reason of affliction: Lord, I have called daily upon thee, I have stretched out my hands unto thee.
verses.bsb: My eyes grow dim with grief. I call to You daily, O LORD; I spread out my hands to You.

verses.kjv: Wilt thou shew wonders to the dead? shall the dead arise and praise thee? Selah.
verses.bsb: Do You work wonders for the dead? Do departed spirits rise up to praise You? Selah

verses.kjv: Shall thy lovingkindness be declared in the grave? or thy faithfulness in destruction?
verses.bsb: Can Your loving devotion be proclaimed in the grave, Your faithfulness in Abaddon?

verses.kjv: Shall thy wonders be known in the dark? and thy righteousness in the land of forgetfulness?
verses.bsb: Will Your wonders be known in the darkness, or Your righteousness in the land of oblivion?

verses.kjv: But unto thee have I cried, O Lord; and in the morning shall my prayer prevent thee.
verses.bsb: But to You, O LORD, I cry for help; in the morning my prayer comes before You.

verses.kjv: Lord, why castest thou off my soul? why hidest thou thy face from me?
verses.bsb: Why, O LORD, do You reject me? Why do You hide Your face from me?

verses.kjv: I am afflicted and ready to die from my youth up: while I suffer thy terrors I am distracted.
verses.bsb: From my youth I was afflicted and near death. I have borne Your terrors; I am in despair.

verses.kjv: Thy fierce wrath goeth over me; thy terrors have cut me off.
verses.bsb: Your wrath has swept over me; Your terrors have destroyed me.

verses.kjv: They came round about me daily like water; they compassed me about together.
verses.bsb: All day long they engulf me like water; they enclose me on every side.

verses.kjv: Lover and friend hast thou put far from me, and mine acquaintance into darkness.
verses.bsb: You have removed my beloved and my friend; darkness is my closest companion.

verses.kjv: I WILL sing of the mercies of the Lord for ever: with my mouth will I make known thy faithfulness to all generations.
verses.bsb: I will sing of the loving devotion of the LORD forever; with my mouth I will proclaim Your faithfulness to all generations.

verses.kjv: For I have said, Mercy shall be built up for ever: thy faithfulness shalt thou establish in the very heavens.
verses.bsb: For I have said, “Loving devotion is built up forever; in the heavens You establish Your faithfulness.”

verses.kjv: I have made a covenant with my chosen, I have sworn unto David my servant,
verses.bsb: You said, “I have made a covenant with My chosen one, I have sworn to David My servant:

verses.kjv: Thy seed will I establish for ever, and build up thy throne to all generations. Selah.
verses.bsb: ‘I will establish your offspring forever and build up your throne for all generations.’” Selah

verses.kjv: And the heavens shall praise thy wonders, O Lord: thy faithfulness also in the congregation of the saints.
verses.bsb: The heavens praise Your wonders, O LORD— Your faithfulness as well— in the assembly of the holy ones.

verses.kjv: For who in the heaven can be compared unto the Lord? who among the sons of the mighty can be likened unto the Lord?
verses.bsb: For who in the skies can compare with the LORD? Who among the heavenly beings is like the LORD?

verses.kjv: God is greatly to be feared in the assembly of the saints, and to be had in reverence of all them that are about him.
verses.bsb: In the council of the holy ones, God is greatly feared, and awesome above all who surround Him.

verses.kjv: O Lord God of hosts, who is a strong Lord like unto thee? or to thy faithfulness round about thee?
verses.bsb: O LORD God of Hosts, who is like You? O mighty LORD, Your faithfulness surrounds You.

verses.kjv: Thou rulest the raging of the sea: when the waves thereof arise, thou stillest them.
verses.bsb: You rule the raging sea; when its waves mount up, You still them.

verses.kjv: Thou hast broken Rahab in pieces, as one that is slain; thou hast scattered thine enemies with thy strong arm.
verses.bsb: You crushed Rahab like a carcass; You scattered Your enemies with Your mighty arm.

verses.kjv: The heavens are thine, the earth also is thine: as for the world and the fulness thereof, thou hast founded them.
verses.bsb: The heavens are Yours, and also the earth. The earth and its fullness You founded.

verses.kjv: The north and the south thou hast created them: Tabor and Hermon shall rejoice in thy name.
verses.bsb: North and south You created; Tabor and Hermon shout for joy at Your name.

verses.kjv: Thou hast a mighty arm: strong is thy hand, and high is thy right hand.
verses.bsb: Mighty is Your arm; strong is Your hand. Your right hand is exalted.

verses.kjv: Justice and judgment are the habitation of thy throne: mercy and truth shall go before thy face.
verses.bsb: Righteousness and justice are the foundation of Your throne; loving devotion and faithfulness go before You.

verses.kjv: Blessed is the people that know the joyful sound: they shall walk, O Lord, in the light of thy countenance.
verses.bsb: Blessed are those who know the joyful sound, who walk, O LORD, in the light of Your presence.

verses.kjv: In thy name shall they rejoice all the day: and in thy righteousness shall they be exalted.
verses.bsb: They rejoice in Your name all day long, and in Your righteousness they exult.

verses.kjv: For thou art the glory of their strength: and in thy favour our horn shall be exalted.
verses.bsb: For You are the glory of their strength, and by Your favor our horn is exalted.

verses.kjv: For the Lord is our defence; and the Holy One of Israel is our king.
verses.bsb: Surely our shield belongs to the LORD, and our king to the Holy One of Israel.

verses.kjv: Then thou spakest in vision to thy holy one, and saidst, I have laid help upon one that is mighty; I have exalted one chosen out of the people.
verses.bsb: You once spoke in a vision; to Your godly ones You said, “I have bestowed help on a warrior; I have exalted one chosen from the people.

verses.kjv: I have found David my servant; with my holy oil have I anointed him:
verses.bsb: I have found My servant David; with My sacred oil I have anointed him.

verses.kjv: With whom my hand shall be established: mine arm also shall strengthen him.
verses.bsb: My hand will sustain him; surely My arm will strengthen him.

verses.kjv: The enemy shall not exact upon him; nor the son of wickedness afflict him.
verses.bsb: No enemy will exact tribute; no wicked man will oppress him.

verses.kjv: And I will beat down his foes before his face, and plague them that hate him.
verses.bsb: I will crush his foes before him and strike down those who hate him.

verses.kjv: But my faithfulness and my mercy shall be with him: and in my name shall his horn be exalted.
verses.bsb: My faithfulness and loving devotion will be with him, and through My name his horn will be exalted.

verses.kjv: I will set his hand also in the sea, and his right hand in the rivers.
verses.bsb: I will set his hand over the sea, and his right hand upon the rivers.

verses.kjv: He shall cry unto me, Thou art my father, my God, and the rock of my salvation.
verses.bsb: He will call to Me, ‘You are my Father, my God, the Rock of my salvation.’

verses.kjv: Also I will make him my firstborn, higher than the kings of the earth.
verses.bsb: I will indeed appoint him as My firstborn, the highest of the kings of the earth.

verses.kjv: My mercy will I keep for him for evermore, and my covenant shall stand fast with him.
verses.bsb: I will forever preserve My loving devotion for him, and My covenant with him will stand fast.

verses.kjv: His seed also will I make to endure for ever, and his throne as the days of heaven.
verses.bsb: I will establish his line forever, his throne as long as the heavens endure.

verses.kjv: If his children forsake my law, and walk not in my judgments;
verses.bsb: If his sons forsake My law and do not walk in My judgments,

verses.kjv: If they break my statutes, and keep not my commandments;
verses.bsb: if they violate My statutes and fail to keep My commandments,

verses.kjv: Then will I visit their transgression with the rod, and their iniquity with stripes.
verses.bsb: I will attend to their transgression with the rod, and to their iniquity with stripes.

verses.kjv: Nevertheless my lovingkindness will I not utterly take from him, nor suffer my faithfulness to fail.
verses.bsb: But I will not withdraw My loving devotion from him, nor ever betray My faithfulness.

verses.kjv: My covenant will I not break, nor alter the thing that is gone out of my lips.
verses.bsb: I will not violate My covenant or alter the utterance of My lips.

verses.kjv: Once have I sworn by my holiness that I will not lie unto David.
verses.bsb: Once and for all I have sworn by My holiness— I will not lie to David—

verses.kjv: His seed shall endure for ever, and his throne as the sun before me.
verses.bsb: his offspring shall endure forever, and his throne before Me like the sun,

verses.kjv: It shall be established for ever as the moon, and as a faithful witness in heaven. Selah.
verses.bsb: like the moon, established forever, a faithful witness in the sky.” Selah

verses.kjv: But thou hast cast off and abhorred, thou hast been wroth with thine anointed.
verses.bsb: Now, however, You have spurned and rejected him; You are enraged by Your anointed one.

verses.kjv: Thou hast made void the covenant of thy servant: thou hast profaned his crown by casting it to the ground.
verses.bsb: You have renounced the covenant with Your servant and sullied his crown in the dust.

verses.kjv: Thou hast broken down all his hedges; thou hast brought his strong holds to ruin.
verses.bsb: You have broken down all his walls; You have reduced his strongholds to rubble.

verses.kjv: All that pass by the way spoil him: he is a reproach to his neighbours.
verses.bsb: All who pass by plunder him; he has become a reproach to his neighbors.

verses.kjv: Thou hast set up the right hand of his adversaries; thou hast made all his enemies to rejoice.
verses.bsb: You have exalted the right hand of his foes; You have made all his enemies rejoice.

verses.kjv: Thou hast also turned the edge of his sword, and hast not made him to stand in the battle.
verses.bsb: You have bent the edge of his sword and have not sustained him in battle.

verses.kjv: Thou hast made his glory to cease, and cast his throne down to the ground.
verses.bsb: You have ended his splendor and cast his throne to the ground.

verses.kjv: The days of his youth hast thou shortened: thou hast covered him with shame. Selah.
verses.bsb: You have cut short the days of his youth; You have covered him with shame. Selah

verses.kjv: How long, Lord? wilt thou hide thyself for ever? shall thy wrath burn like fire?
verses.bsb: How long, O LORD? Will You hide Yourself forever? Will Your wrath keep burning like fire?

verses.kjv: Remember how short my time is: wherefore hast thou made all men in vain?
verses.bsb: Remember the briefness of my lifespan! For what futility You have created all men!

verses.kjv: What man is he that liveth, and shall not see death? shall he deliver his soul from the hand of the grave? Selah.
verses.bsb: What man can live and never see death? Can he deliver his soul from the power of Sheol? Selah

verses.kjv: Lord, where are thy former lovingkindnesses, which thou swarest unto David in thy truth?
verses.bsb: Where, O Lord, is Your loving devotion of old, which You faithfully swore to David?

verses.kjv: Remember, Lord, the reproach of thy servants; how I do bear in my bosom the reproach of all the mighty people;
verses.bsb: Remember, O Lord, the reproach of Your servants, which I bear in my heart from so many people—

verses.kjv: Wherewith thine enemies have reproached, O Lord; wherewith they have reproached the footsteps of thine anointed.
verses.bsb: how Your enemies have taunted, O LORD, and have mocked every step of Your anointed one!

verses.kjv: Blessed be the Lord for evermore. Amen, and Amen.
verses.bsb: Blessed be the LORD forever!   Amen and amen.

verses.kjv: LORD, thou hast been our dwelling place in all generations.
verses.bsb: Lord, You have been our dwelling place through all generations.

verses.kjv: Before the mountains were brought forth, or ever thou hadst formed the earth and the world, even from everlasting to everlasting, thou art God.
verses.bsb: Before the mountains were born or You brought forth the earth and the world, from everlasting to everlasting You are God.

verses.kjv: Thou turnest man to destruction; and sayest, Return, ye children of men.
verses.bsb: You return man to dust, saying, “Return, O sons of mortals.”

verses.kjv: For a thousand years in thy sight are but as yesterday when it is past, and as a watch in the night.
verses.bsb: For in Your sight a thousand years are but a day that passes, or a watch of the night.

verses.kjv: Thou carriest them away as with a flood; they are as a sleep: in the morning they are like grass which groweth up.
verses.bsb: You whisk them away in their sleep; they are like the new grass of the morning—

verses.kjv: In the morning it flourisheth, and groweth up; in the evening it is cut down, and withereth.
verses.bsb: in the morning it springs up new, but by evening it fades and withers.

verses.kjv: For we are consumed by thine anger, and by thy wrath are we troubled.
verses.bsb: For we are consumed by Your anger and terrified by Your wrath.

verses.kjv: Thou hast set our iniquities before thee, our secret sins in the light of thy countenance.
verses.bsb: You have set our iniquities before You, our secret sins in the light of Your presence.

verses.kjv: For all our days are passed away in thy wrath: we spend our years as a tale that is told.
verses.bsb: For all our days decline in Your fury; we finish our years with a sigh.

verses.kjv: The days of our years are threescore years and ten; and if by reason of strength they be fourscore years, yet is their strength labour and sorrow; for it is soon cut off, and we fly away.
verses.bsb: The length of our days is seventy years— or eighty if we are strong— yet their pride is but labor and sorrow, for they quickly pass, and we fly away.

verses.kjv: Who knoweth the power of thine anger? even according to thy fear, so is thy wrath.
verses.bsb: Who knows the power of Your anger? Your wrath matches the fear You are due.

verses.kjv: So teach us to number our days, that we may apply our hearts unto wisdom.
verses.bsb: So teach us to number our days, that we may present a heart of wisdom.

verses.kjv: Return, O Lord, how long? and let it repent thee concerning thy servants.
verses.bsb: Return, O LORD! How long will it be? Have compassion on Your servants.

verses.kjv: O satisfy us early with thy mercy; that we may rejoice and be glad all our days.
verses.bsb: Satisfy us in the morning with Your loving devotion, that we may sing for joy and be glad all our days.

verses.kjv: Make us glad according to the days wherein thou hast afflicted us, and the years wherein we have seen evil.
verses.bsb: Make us glad for as many days as You have afflicted us, for as many years as we have seen evil.

verses.kjv: Let thy work appear unto thy servants, and thy glory unto their children.
verses.bsb: May Your work be shown to Your servants, and Your splendor to their children.

verses.kjv: And let the beauty of the Lord our God be upon us: and establish thou the work of our hands upon us; yea, the work of our hands establish thou it.
verses.bsb: May the favor of the Lord our God rest upon us; establish for us the work of our hands— yes, establish the work of our hands!

verses.kjv: HE that dwelleth in the secret place of the most High shall abide under the shadow of the Almighty.
verses.bsb: He who dwells in the shelter of the Most High will abide in the shadow of the Almighty.

verses.kjv: I will say of the Lord, He is my refuge and my fortress: my God; in him will I trust.
verses.bsb: I will say to the LORD, “You are my refuge and my fortress, my God, in whom I trust.”

verses.kjv: Surely he shall deliver thee from the snare of the fowler, and from the noisome pestilence.
verses.bsb: Surely He will deliver you from the snare of the fowler, and from the deadly plague.

verses.kjv: He shall cover thee with his feathers, and under his wings shalt thou trust: his truth shall be thy shield and buckler.
verses.bsb: He will cover you with His feathers; under His wings you will find refuge; His faithfulness is a shield and rampart.

verses.kjv: Thou shalt not be afraid for the terror by night; nor for the arrow that flieth by day;
verses.bsb: You will not fear the terror of the night, nor the arrow that flies by day,

verses.kjv: Nor for the pestilence that walketh in darkness; nor for the destruction that wasteth at noonday.
verses.bsb: nor the pestilence that stalks in the darkness, nor the calamity that destroys at noon.

verses.kjv: A thousand shall fall at thy side, and ten thousand at thy right hand; but it shall not come nigh thee.
verses.bsb: Though a thousand may fall at your side, and ten thousand at your right hand, no harm will come near you.

verses.kjv: Only with thine eyes shalt thou behold and see the reward of the wicked.
verses.bsb: You will only see it with your eyes and witness the punishment of the wicked.

verses.kjv: Because thou hast made the Lord, which is my refuge, even the most High, thy habitation;
verses.bsb: Because you have made the LORD your dwelling— my refuge, the Most High—

verses.kjv: There shall no evil befall thee, neither shall any plague come nigh thy dwelling.
verses.bsb: no evil will befall you, no plague will approach your tent.

verses.kjv: For he shall give his angels charge over thee, to keep thee in all thy ways.
verses.bsb: For He will command His angels concerning you to guard you in all your ways.

verses.kjv: They shall bear thee up in their hands, lest thou dash thy foot against a stone.
verses.bsb: They will lift you up in their hands, so that you will not strike your foot against a stone.

verses.kjv: Thou shalt tread upon the lion and adder: the young lion and the dragon shalt thou trample under feet.
verses.bsb: You will tread on the lion and cobra; you will trample the young lion and serpent.

verses.kjv: Because he hath set his love upon me, therefore will I deliver him: I will set him on high, because he hath known my name.
verses.bsb: “Because he loves Me, I will deliver him; because he knows My name, I will protect him.

verses.kjv: He shall call upon me, and I will answer him: I will be with him in trouble; I will deliver him, and honour him.
verses.bsb: When he calls out to Me, I will answer him; I will be with him in trouble. I will deliver him and honor him.

verses.kjv: With long life will I satisfy him, and shew him my salvation.
verses.bsb: With long life I will satisfy him and show him My salvation.”

verses.kjv: IT is a good thing to give thanks unto the Lord, and to sing praises unto thy name, O most High:
verses.bsb: It is good to praise the LORD, and to sing praises to Your name, O Most High,

verses.kjv: To shew forth thy lovingkindness in the morning, and thy faithfulness every night,
verses.bsb: to proclaim Your loving devotion in the morning and Your faithfulness at night

verses.kjv: Upon an instrument of ten strings, and upon the psaltery; upon the harp with a solemn sound.
verses.bsb: with the ten-stringed harp and the melody of the lyre.

verses.kjv: For thou, Lord, hast made me glad through thy work: I will triumph in the works of thy hands.
verses.bsb: For You, O LORD, have made me glad by Your deeds; I sing for joy at the works of Your hands.

verses.kjv: O Lord, how great are thy works! and thy thoughts are very deep.
verses.bsb: How great are Your works, O LORD, how deep are Your thoughts!

verses.kjv: A brutish man knoweth not; neither doth a fool understand this.
verses.bsb: A senseless man does not know, and a fool does not understand,

verses.kjv: When the wicked spring as the grass, and when all the workers of iniquity do flourish; it is that they shall be destroyed for ever:
verses.bsb: that though the wicked sprout like grass, and all evildoers flourish, they will be forever destroyed.

verses.kjv: But thou, Lord, art most high for evermore.
verses.bsb: But You, O LORD, are exalted forever!

verses.kjv: For, lo, thine enemies, O Lord, for, lo, thine enemies shall perish; all the workers of iniquity shall be scattered.
verses.bsb: For surely Your enemies, O LORD, surely Your enemies will perish; all evildoers will be scattered.

verses.kjv: But my horn shalt thou exalt like the horn of an unicorn: I shall be anointed with fresh oil.
verses.bsb: But You have exalted my horn like that of a wild ox; with fine oil I have been anointed.

verses.kjv: Mine eye also shall see my desire on mine enemies, and mine ears shall hear my desire of the wicked that rise up against me.
verses.bsb: My eyes see the downfall of my enemies; my ears hear the wailing of my wicked foes.

verses.kjv: The righteous shall flourish like the palm tree: he shall grow like a cedar in Lebanon.
verses.bsb: The righteous will flourish like a palm tree, and grow like a cedar in Lebanon.

verses.kjv: Those that be planted in the house of the Lord shall flourish in the courts of our God.
verses.bsb: Planted in the house of the LORD, they will flourish in the courts of our God.

verses.kjv: They shall still bring forth fruit in old age; they shall be fat and flourishing;
verses.bsb: In old age they will still bear fruit; healthy and green they will remain,

verses.kjv: To shew that the Lord is upright: he is my rock, and there is no unrighteousness in him.
verses.bsb: to proclaim, “The LORD is upright; He is my Rock, and in Him there is no unrighteousness.”

verses.kjv: THE Lord reigneth, he is clothed with majesty; the Lord is clothed with strength, wherewith he hath girded himself: the world also is stablished, that it cannot be moved.
verses.bsb: The LORD reigns! He is robed in majesty; the LORD has clothed and armed Himself with strength. The world indeed is firmly established; it cannot be moved.

verses.kjv: Thy throne is established of old: thou art from everlasting.
verses.bsb: Your throne was established long ago; You are from all eternity.

verses.kjv: The floods have lifted up, O Lord, the floods have lifted up their voice; the floods lift up their waves.
verses.bsb: The floodwaters have risen, O LORD; the rivers have raised their voice; the seas lift up their pounding waves.

verses.kjv: The Lord on high is mightier than the noise of many waters, yea, than the mighty waves of the sea.
verses.bsb: Above the roar of many waters— the mighty breakers of the sea— the LORD on high is majestic.

verses.kjv: Thy testimonies are very sure: holiness becometh thine house, O Lord, for ever.
verses.bsb: Your testimonies are fully confirmed; holiness adorns Your house, O LORD, for all the days to come.

verses.kjv: O LORD God, to whom vengeance belongeth; O God, to whom vengeance belongeth, shew thyself.
verses.bsb: O LORD, God of vengeance, O God of vengeance, shine forth.

verses.kjv: Lift up thyself, thou judge of the earth: render a reward to the proud.
verses.bsb: Rise up, O Judge of the earth; render a reward to the proud.

verses.kjv: Lord, how long shall the wicked, how long shall the wicked triumph?
verses.bsb: How long will the wicked, O LORD, how long will the wicked exult?

verses.kjv: How long shall they utter and speak hard things? and all the workers of iniquity boast themselves?
verses.bsb: They pour out arrogant words; all workers of iniquity boast.

verses.kjv: They break in pieces thy people, O Lord, and afflict thine heritage.
verses.bsb: They crush Your people, O LORD; they oppress Your heritage.

verses.kjv: They slay the widow and the stranger, and murder the fatherless.
verses.bsb: They kill the widow and the foreigner; they murder the fatherless.

verses.kjv: Yet they say, The Lord shall not see, neither shall the God of Jacob regard it.
verses.bsb: They say, “The LORD does not see; the God of Jacob pays no heed.”

verses.kjv: Understand, ye brutish among the people: and ye fools, when will ye be wise?
verses.bsb: Take notice, O senseless among the people! O fools, when will you be wise?

verses.kjv: He that planted the ear, shall he not hear? he that formed the eye, shall he not see?
verses.bsb: He who affixed the ear, can He not hear? He who formed the eye, can He not see?

verses.kjv: He that chastiseth the heathen, shall not he correct? he that teacheth man knowledge, shall not he know?
verses.bsb: He who admonishes the nations, does He not discipline? He who teaches man, does He lack knowledge?

verses.kjv: The Lord knoweth the thoughts of man, that they are vanity.
verses.bsb: The LORD knows the thoughts of man, that they are futile.

verses.kjv: Blessed is the man whom thou chastenest, O Lord, and teachest him out of thy law;
verses.bsb: Blessed is the man You discipline, O LORD, and teach from Your law,

verses.kjv: That thou mayest give him rest from the days of adversity, until the pit be digged for the wicked.
verses.bsb: to grant him relief from days of trouble, until a pit is dug for the wicked.

verses.kjv: For the Lord will not cast off his people, neither will he forsake his inheritance.
verses.bsb: For the LORD will not forsake His people; He will never abandon His heritage.

verses.kjv: But judgment shall return unto righteousness: and all the upright in heart shall follow it.
verses.bsb: Surely judgment will again be righteous, and all the upright in heart will follow it.

verses.kjv: Who will rise up for me against the evildoers? or who will stand up for me against the workers of iniquity?
verses.bsb: Who will rise up for me against the wicked? Who will stand for me against the workers of iniquity?

verses.kjv: Unless the Lord had been my help, my soul had almost dwelt in silence.
verses.bsb: Unless the LORD had been my helper, I would soon have dwelt in the abode of silence.

verses.kjv: When I said, My foot slippeth; thy mercy, O Lord, held me up.
verses.bsb: If I say, “My foot is slipping,” Your loving devotion, O LORD, supports me.

verses.kjv: In the multitude of my thoughts within me thy comforts delight my soul.
verses.bsb: When anxiety overwhelms me, Your consolation delights my soul.

verses.kjv: Shall the throne of iniquity have fellowship with thee, which frameth mischief by a law?
verses.bsb: Can a corrupt throne be Your ally— one devising mischief by decree?

verses.kjv: They gather themselves together against the soul of the righteous, and condemn the innocent blood.
verses.bsb: They band together against the righteous and condemn the innocent to death.

verses.kjv: But the Lord is my defence; and my God is the rock of my refuge.
verses.bsb: But the LORD has been my stronghold, and my God is my rock of refuge.

verses.kjv: And he shall bring upon them their own iniquity, and shall cut them off in their own wickedness; yea, the Lord our God shall cut them off.
verses.bsb: He will bring upon them their own iniquity and destroy them for their wickedness. The LORD our God will destroy them.

verses.kjv: O COME, let us sing unto the Lord: let us make a joyful noise to the rock of our salvation.
verses.bsb: Come, let us sing for joy to the LORD; let us shout to the Rock of our salvation!

verses.kjv: Let us come before his presence with thanksgiving, and make a joyful noise unto him with psalms.
verses.bsb: Let us enter His presence with thanksgiving; let us make a joyful noise to Him in song.

verses.kjv: For the Lord is a great God, and a great King above all gods.
verses.bsb: For the LORD is a great God, a great King above all gods.

verses.kjv: In his hand are the deep places of the earth: the strength of the hills is his also.
verses.bsb: In His hand are the depths of the earth, and the mountain peaks belong to Him.

verses.kjv: The sea is his, and he made it: and his hands formed the dry land.
verses.bsb: The sea is His, for He made it, and His hands formed the dry land.

verses.kjv: O come, let us worship and bow down: let us kneel before the Lord our maker.
verses.bsb: O come, let us worship and bow down; let us kneel before the LORD our Maker.

verses.kjv: For he is our God; and we are the people of his pasture, and the sheep of his hand. To day if ye will hear his voice,
verses.bsb: For He is our God, and we are the people of His pasture, the sheep under His care.   Today, if you hear His voice,

verses.kjv: Harden not your heart, as in the provocation, and as in the day of temptation in the wilderness:
verses.bsb: do not harden your hearts as you did at Meribah, in the day at Massah in the wilderness,

verses.kjv: When your fathers tempted me, proved me, and saw my work.
verses.bsb: where your fathers tested and tried Me, though they had seen My work.

verses.kjv: Forty years long was I grieved with this generation, and said, It is a people that do err in their heart, and they have not known my ways:
verses.bsb: For forty years I was angry with that generation, and I said, “They are a people whose hearts go astray, and they have not known My ways.”

verses.kjv: Unto whom I sware in my wrath that they should not enter into my rest.
verses.bsb: So I swore on oath in My anger, “They shall never enter My rest.”

verses.kjv: O SING unto the Lord a new song: sing unto the Lord, all the earth.
verses.bsb: Sing to the LORD a new song; sing to the LORD, all the earth.

verses.kjv: Sing unto the Lord, bless his name; shew forth his salvation from day to day.
verses.bsb: Sing to the LORD, bless His name; proclaim His salvation day after day.

verses.kjv: Declare his glory among the heathen, his wonders among all people.
verses.bsb: Declare His glory among the nations, His wonderful deeds among all peoples.

verses.kjv: For the Lord is great, and greatly to be praised: he is to be feared above all gods.
verses.bsb: For great is the LORD, and greatly to be praised; He is to be feared above all gods.

verses.kjv: For all the gods of the nations are idols: but the Lord made the heavens.
verses.bsb: For all the gods of the nations are idols, but it is the LORD who made the heavens.

verses.kjv: Honour and majesty are before him: strength and beauty are in his sanctuary.
verses.bsb: Splendor and majesty are before Him; strength and beauty fill His sanctuary.

verses.kjv: Give unto the Lord, O ye kindreds of the people, give unto the Lord glory and strength.
verses.bsb: Ascribe to the LORD, O families of the nations, ascribe to the LORD glory and strength.

verses.kjv: Give unto the Lord the glory due unto his name: bring an offering, and come into his courts.
verses.bsb: Ascribe to the LORD the glory due His name; bring an offering and enter His courts.

verses.kjv: O worship the Lord in the beauty of holiness: fear before him, all the earth.
verses.bsb: Worship the LORD in the splendor of His holiness; tremble before Him, all the earth.

verses.kjv: Say among the heathen that the Lord reigneth: the world also shall be established that it shall not be moved: he shall judge the people righteously.
verses.bsb: Declare among the nations: “The LORD reigns!” The world is firmly established; it cannot be moved; He will judge the peoples with equity.

verses.kjv: Let the heavens rejoice, and let the earth be glad; let the sea roar, and the fulness thereof.
verses.bsb: Let the heavens be glad and the earth rejoice; let the sea resound, and all that fills it.

verses.kjv: Let the field be joyful, and all that is therein: then shall all the trees of the wood rejoice
verses.bsb: Let the fields exult, and all that is in them. Then all the trees of the forest will sing for joy

verses.kjv: Before the Lord: for he cometh, for he cometh to judge the earth: he shall judge the world with righteousness, and the people with his truth.
verses.bsb: before the LORD, for He is coming— He is coming to judge the earth. He will judge the world in righteousness and the peoples in His faithfulness.

verses.kjv: THE Lord reigneth; let the earth rejoice; let the multitude of isles be glad thereof.
verses.bsb: The LORD reigns, let the earth rejoice; let the distant shores be glad.

verses.kjv: Clouds and darkness are round about him: righteousness and judgment are the habitation of his throne.
verses.bsb: Clouds and darkness surround Him; righteousness and justice are His throne’s foundation.

verses.kjv: A fire goeth before him, and burneth up his enemies round about.
verses.bsb: Fire goes before Him and consumes His foes on every side.

verses.kjv: His lightnings enlightened the world: the earth saw, and trembled.
verses.bsb: His lightning illuminates the world; the earth sees and trembles.

verses.kjv: The hills melted like wax at the presence of the Lord, at the presence of the Lord of the whole earth.
verses.bsb: The mountains melt like wax at the presence of the LORD, before the Lord of all the earth.

verses.kjv: The heavens declare his righteousness, and all the people see his glory.
verses.bsb: The heavens proclaim His righteousness; all the peoples see His glory.

verses.kjv: Confounded be all they that serve graven images, that boast themselves of idols: worship him, all ye gods.
verses.bsb: All worshipers of images are put to shame— those who boast in idols. Worship Him, all you gods!
